EDCS-00300195.text_edition: Ferrandus   Felix Liberi fidelis in pace vixit annis LXX
EDCS-00300195.translation: Ferrandus Felix, faithful servant of Liber, lived in peace for 70 years.
EDCS-00300195.summary: This is a Christian epitaph for Ferrandus Felix, a devoted follower of Liber, who lived to the age of 70.

EDCS-00381014.text_edition: InIn C(ai)? Clodi equitis legionis et legionis III Augustae decurionis emeriti et in honorem Cai Clodii equitis Romani filii et heredis eius IIviri II flaminis perpetui decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-00381014.translation: In honor of Gaius Clodius, cavalryman of the legion and decurion emeritus of the Third Augustan Legion, and in honor of his son and heir, Gaius Clodius, Roman knight, duumvir, and perpetual flamen; by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-00381014.summary: This inscription records the public dedication, funded by the decurions, of an honorific monument for Gaius Clodius, a distinguished legionary cavalryman and decurion, and his son, Gaius Clodius, a Roman knight and priest.

EDCS-00381015.text_edition: Orfito et Priscino consulibus ex ratione Callisti numero DX Certi
EDCS-00381015.translation: From the account of Callistus, number 510, during the consulship of Orfitus and Priscinus: Certus.
EDCS-00381015.summary: This inscription is a record from Callistus's ledger, noting item number 510 for Certus, dated to the consulship of Orfitus and Priscinus (AD 270).

EDCS-00381019.text_edition: Ex ratione officinae Agenti Calisti liberti Augusti  numero X
EDCS-00381019.translation: From the account of the workshop of Agens Calistus, freedman of Augustus, number 10.
EDCS-00381019.summary: This inscription is an administrative record from the workshop of Agens Calistus, an Augusti libertus, noting item number 10.

EDCS-00381022.text_edition: Ex ratione Pluriani Augusti liberti Iun et Serviano III numero
EDCS-00381022.translation: From the account of Plurianus, freedman of Augustus, in the month of June, during the third consulship of Servianus, number [---].
EDCS-00381022.summary: This inscription is an administrative record from the account of Plurianus, an Augusti libertus, dated to June during Servianus's third consulship (AD 134).

EDCS-00381023.text_edition: Hadriani Augusti numero Iun et Serviano III
EDCS-00381023.translation: From Hadrianus Augustus, number [---], in the month of June, during the third consulship of Servianus.
EDCS-00381023.summary: This inscription is an administrative record associated with Emperor Hadrian, dated to June during Servianus's third consulship (AD 134).

EDCS-00381024.text_edition: Hadriani Augusti CV et Serviano III
EDCS-00381024.translation: From Hadrianus Augustus, 105, during the third consulship of Servianus.
EDCS-00381024.summary: This inscription is an administrative record associated with Emperor Hadrian, noting item 105, dated to Servianus's third consulship (AD 134).

EDCS-00381027.text_edition: Numero XII officinae Anullino II et Frontone consulibus caesura Athenodori procuratoris
EDCS-00381027.translation: Number 12, from the workshop, during the second consulship of Anullinus and the consulship of Fronto, under the management of Athenodorus, procurator.
EDCS-00381027.summary: This inscription is an administrative record from a workshop, noting item number 12, dated to the consulship of Anullinus II and Fronto (AD 199), and supervised by procurator Athenodorus.

EDCS-00381028.text_edition: Numero CCXXX officina Caelestis Luciano et Fabiano consulibus caesura Athenodori procuratoris
EDCS-00381028.translation: Number 230, from the workshop of Caelestis, during the consulship of Lucianus and Fabianus, under the management of Athenodorus, procurator.
EDCS-00381028.summary: This inscription is an administrative record from the Caelestis workshop, noting item number 230, dated to the consulship of Lucianus and Fabianus (AD 256), and supervised by procurator Athenodorus.

EDCS-00381033.text_edition: Hadriani Augusti de loco Camerino et Nigro consulibus sub cura  Galli
EDCS-00381033.translation: From Hadrianus Augustus, from the Camerino location, during the consulship of Niger, under the care of Gallus.
EDCS-00381033.summary: This inscription is an administrative record related to Emperor Hadrian, concerning a matter from Camerino, dated to the consulship of Niger (AD 118), and managed by Gallus.

EDCS-00381034.text_edition: Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Pii numero officina Silvano et Augurino consulibus V
EDCS-00381034.translation: From the official number of the Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, in the workshop, in the fifth consulship of Silvanus and Augurinus.
EDCS-00381034.summary: This inscription records an official number from an imperial workshop during the consulship of Silvanus and Augurinus, likely indicating a date and administrative context for the production of an object.

EDCS-00381035.text_edition: Hadriani Augusti Aelio Caesare II consule sub cura Agathae liberti
EDCS-00381035.translation: From Hadrian Augustus, in the second consulship of Aelius Caesar, under the care of Agathe, a freedwoman.
EDCS-00381035.summary: This inscription dates to the second consulship of Aelius Caesar during the reign of Hadrian Augustus, noting that it was managed by Agathe, a freedwoman.

EDCS-00381036.text_edition: Hadriani Augusti de loco Camerino et Nigro consulibus sub cura Agathae liberti
EDCS-00381036.translation: From Hadrian Augustus, from the locality of Camerinum, in the consulship of Niger, under the care of Agathe, a freedwoman.
EDCS-00381036.summary: This inscription originates from the locality of Camerinum during the reign of Hadrian Augustus and the consulship of Niger, indicating the oversight of Agathe, a freedwoman.

EDCS-00381040.text_edition: Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Pii domini numero officina Commodo et Laterano consulibus BAB
EDCS-00381040.translation: From the official number of our lord, the Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, in the workshop, in the consulship of Commodus and Lateranus, BAB.
EDCS-00381040.summary: This inscription denotes an official number from an imperial workshop belonging to Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, dated to the consulship of Commodus and Lateranus.

EDCS-00381041.text_edition: Pisone et Bolano consulibus ex ratione Felicis Augusti servi de numero XXX officina nova Caelestis
EDCS-00381041.translation: In the consulship of Piso and Bolanus, from the account of Felix, a slave of the emperor, from the number thirty, in the new workshop of Caelestis.
EDCS-00381041.summary: This inscription records an item or production from the new Caelestis workshop, managed by Felix, an imperial slave, in the consulship of Piso and Bolanus.

EDCS-00381051.text_edition: Pro salute et reditu Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini voto suscepto Pientius Augusti libertus procurator marmorum Numidicorum porticum cum cella sacerdae et cisterna perfecit eaque populo Simittuensi praebenda dedicavit
EDCS-00381051.translation: For the safety and return of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, a vow having been undertaken, Pientius, a freedman of the emperor and procurator of Numidian marbles, completed a portico with a cella for the priest and a cistern, and dedicated these for the use of the people of Simittu.
EDCS-00381051.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a portico, priest's cella, and cistern by Pientius, an imperial freedman and procurator of Numidian marbles, to the people of Simittu, fulfilling a vow for the emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus's health and return.

EDCS-00800004.publication: ILAfr 00560 = Dougga 00005 = AE 2016, +01901 = Aounallah-2016, p 211 = Aounallah-2016, p 249
EDCS-00800004.translation: To Marcus Aelius Aurelius Verus Caesar, consul for the second time, son of Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, the district and community of Thugga, by decree of the decurions, with public money, [dedicated this]. // The Divine Vespasian.
EDCS-00800004.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the pagus and civitas of Thugga to Marcus Aelius Aurelius Verus Caesar, son of Antoninus Pius, likely a statue base, also invoking the divine Vespasian.

EDCS-00800014.text_edition: Iovi Augusto sacrum Lucius Terentius Adiutoris filius Rufinianus signum Iovis Victoris Conservatoris et candelabra cum lucernis ob honorem patronatus civitatis suae Thuggensis in se decreto ordinis conlato donum dedit et ob dedicationem decurionibus denarios ternos dedit
EDCS-00800014.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Augustus. Lucius Terentius Rufinianus, son of Adiutor, presented as a gift a statue of Jupiter Victor Conservator and candelabra with lamps, in honor of the patronage of his city Thugga, conferred upon him by decree of the order, and for the dedication he gave three denarii to each decurion.
EDCS-00800014.summary: This inscription records Lucius Terentius Rufinianus's dedication of a statue of Jupiter Victor Conservator and candelabra to Jupiter Augustus in his hometown of Thugga, in gratitude for being granted patronage, and commemorating the event with a payment to the decurions.

EDCS-00800019.text_edition: Lucio Mummio Faustiano clarissimo viro consuli ordinario XVviro sacris faciundis quaestori kandidato curatori viae Appiae et alimentorum legato Hipponensium Tarrunteniae Paulinae clarissimae feminae coniugi eius Lucio Mummio Faustiano Tarruntenio Paterno clarissimo viro quaestori kandidato Mummiae Tarrunteniae Cornelianae clarissimae feminae Lucio Mummio Faustiano Iuniori clarissimo iuveni quaestori kandidato filiis eorum res publica coloniae Thuggae publice posuit
EDCS-00800019.translation: The public community of the colonia Thugga publicly erected this for Lucius Mummius Faustianus, most distinguished man, ordinary consul, quindecimvir for performing sacred rites, quaestor candidate, curator of the Appian Way and of the alimenta; for Tarruntenia Paulina, most distinguished woman, his wife; for Lucius Mummius Faustianus Tarruntenius Paternus, most distinguished man, quaestor candidate; for Mummia Tarruntenia Corneliana, most distinguished woman; for Lucius Mummius Faustianus Iunior, most distinguished youth, quaestor candidate, their children.
EDCS-00800019.summary: This inscription is a public monument erected by the colonia Thugga in honor of the prominent Mummius Faustianus family, including Lucius Mummius Faustianus, his wife Tarruntenia Paulina, and their three distinguished children, commemorating their public service and status.

EDCS-02000005.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Hadriani Augusti Silvano deo sacrum Aebutii Saturninus et Victor Titi Aebuti filii aram sua pecunia fecerunt idemque dedicarunt
EDCS-02000005.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Hadrian Augustus, sacred to the god Silvanus. Aebutius Saturninus and Victor, sons of Titus Aebutius, made this altar at their own expense and also dedicated it.
EDCS-02000005.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to the god Silvanus by Aebutius Saturninus and Victor, sons of Titus Aebutius, who funded and dedicated it for the welfare of Emperor Hadrian Augustus.

EDCS-03000851.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Messius Luci filius Galeria Fructus Myrtili miles cohortis I urbanae stipendiorum XV vixit annis XXXIIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Gampudius Tullenianus frater et heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-03000851.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Messius Fructus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, Myrtilus, a soldier of the First Urban Cohort, served 15 years, lived 34 years. He lies here. May the earth be light for you. Gampudius Tullenianus, his brother and heir, took care of having this made.
EDCS-03000851.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Messius Fructus, a soldier of the Urban Cohort, erected by his brother and heir, Gampudius Tullenianus, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-03000857.text_edition: AVGGI mentius  curator rei publicae V CIICIII dedicavit
EDCS-03000857.translation: AVGGI Mentius, curator of the Republic, dedicated this.
EDCS-03000857.summary: This is a dedication made by Mentius, a curator of the res publica, possibly a municipal official, who dedicated a monument or public work, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03000858.text_edition: orum ma potestate VI consuli III patri patriae proconsuli municipium Thagoritanum Maius devotum numini maiestatique eius decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-03000858.translation: [To ---]rum, holder of tribunician power for the sixth time, consul for the third time, father of the country, proconsul. The great Thagora municipal community, devoted to his divinity and majesty, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-03000858.summary: This is a public dedication by the municipium Thagoritanum Maius to an unnamed emperor, honoring his various titles and offices, erected by decree of the decurions with public funds, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03000859.text_edition: Frugifero Augusto sacrum pro salute Imperatorum Marci Aureli Antonini et Luci Aeli Aureli Veri Augustorumrviocc moran
EDCS-03000859.translation: Sacred to Frugifer Augustus, for the well-being of the Emperors Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Lucius Aelius Aurelius Verus Augusti.
EDCS-03000859.summary: This is a religious dedication made to Frugifer Augustus for the health and well-being of the co-emperors Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus, dating to the period of their joint rule (161-169 AD).

EDCS-03000863.text_edition: IVSA ius extricatus Karthagine  I Kalendas Maias Aproniano II et Paulo II consulibus
EDCS-03000863.translation: By the law, he was freed from Carthage on the Kalends of May, in the consulship of Apronianus II and Paulus II.
EDCS-03000863.summary: This inscription records a legal act, specifically the manumission of an individual from Carthage, dated to the Kalends of May in the year 223 AD (when Apronianus and Paulus were consuls for the second time).

EDCS-03000877.text_edition: Dexia Celido pia vixit annis XX ossa tua bene quiescant terra tibi levis sit
EDCS-03000877.translation: Pious Dexia Celido lived 20 years. May your bones rest well; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-03000877.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dexia Celido, a pious individual who died young, expressing common epitaphic wishes, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300886.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto devota Segermes numini maiestatique eius decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-03300886.translation: To Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus. Segermes, devoted to his divinity and majesty, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-03300886.summary: This is a public dedication by the city of Segermes to Emperor Constantine I, honoring his titles and expressing the city's devotion, erected by decree of the decurions with public funds, dating to his reign (306-337 AD).

EDCS-03300889.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII consul III pontem fecit per legionem III Augustam Sexto Iulio Maiore legato Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-03300889.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventh time, consul for the third time, built this bridge through the Third Augustan Legion, with Sextus Iulius Maior as legate of Augustus pro praetore.
EDCS-03300889.summary: This inscription commemorates the construction of a bridge by Emperor Hadrian, indicating his titles and the involvement of the Legio III Augusta under his legate, Sextus Iulius Maior, dated to 123 AD (based on his 7th tribunician power and 3rd consulship).

EDCS-03300890.text_edition: Calpurniae Ceiae Aemilianae clarissimae feminae coniugi Quinti Aradi Rufini clarissimi viri civitas Thibicaaensis decreto decurionum patronae
EDCS-03300890.translation: To Calpurnia Ceia Aemiliana, most distinguished woman, wife of Quintus Aradius Rufinus, most distinguished man. The city of Thibicaaensis, by decree of the decurions, to its patroness.
EDCS-03300890.summary: This is a public dedication by the city of Thibicaaensis to Calpurnia Ceia Aemiliana, a prominent woman and wife of a distinguished man, honoring her as a patroness by decree of the decurions, likely from the 3rd or 4th century AD.

EDCS-03300902.text_edition: Lucius Litussius Verianius veteranus vixit annos L semissem III dies Sextia Bona coniugi suo piissimo de suo fecit
EDCS-03300902.translation: Lucius Litussius Verianius, a veteran, lived 50 and a half years and 3 days. Sextia Bona made this from her own resources for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-03300902.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Sextia Bona for her husband, Lucius Litussius Verianius, a veteran who lived a precise number of years, months, and days, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300904.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Aufidius Muntanus vixit annos XII hic situs est Viator Auggustorum nnostrorum servus pater infelicissimus filio dulcissimo fecit
EDCS-03300904.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Aufidius Muntanus lived twelve years; he lies here. Viator, a slave of our Augusti, his most unhappy father, made this for his sweetest son.
EDCS-03300904.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aufidius Muntanus, dedicated by his father Viator, a slave of the emperors, dating probably to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300905.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Annius Ingenus vixit anno mensibus X Lucius Annnius Melicerta piissimo fecit hic situs
EDCS-03300905.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Annius Ingenus lived for one year and ten months. Lucius Annius Melicerta made this for his most devoted one. He lies here.
EDCS-03300905.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Annius Ingenus, set up by Lucius Annius Melicerta for the deceased, likely a child.

EDCS-03300906.text_edition: Antistia Stercila Luci Antisti filia vixit annis XIII hic sita
EDCS-03300906.translation: Antistia Stercila, daughter of Lucius Antistius, lived thirteen years; she lies here.
EDCS-03300906.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antistia Stercila, daughter of Lucius Antistius, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-03300907.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Catta vixit annos XXII hic sita est Aurelius Speratus uxori piae faciendum curavit
EDCS-03300907.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelia Catta lived twenty-two years; she lies here. Aurelius Speratus saw to the making of this for his devoted wife.
EDCS-03300907.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Catta, dedicated by her husband Aurelius Speratus, probably a tombstone.

EDCS-03300908.text_edition: Clodia Clodi filia Salsa pia vixit annis XXV hic sita est
EDCS-03300908.translation: Clodia Salsa, daughter of Clodius, devoted, lived twenty-five years; she lies here.
EDCS-03300908.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodia Salsa, daughter of Clodius, noting her age and piety.

EDCS-03300911.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name label from a funerary context, for Crippia Quarta.

EDCS-03300916.text_edition: Festus Luci Ortesi filius vixit nnos IX
EDCS-03300916.translation: Festus, son of Lucius Ortesis, lived nine years.
EDCS-03300916.summary: This is a short funerary inscription for Festus, son of Lucius Ortesis, indicating his young age at death.

EDCS-03300917.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Flavia Urbana pia vixit annos III menses V dies VII hic sita est Lucius Flavius Donatus filiae piissimae memoriam fecit
EDCS-03300917.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavia Urbana, devoted, lived three years, five months, seven days; she lies here. Lucius Flavius Donatus made this memorial for his most devoted daughter.
EDCS-03300917.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Urbana, a young child, dedicated by her father Lucius Flavius Donatus.

EDCS-03300918.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Iulio Luci filio Papiria Vno Extricato  iuveni egregio sancto bono  V
EDCS-03300918.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Lucius Iulius Extricatus, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, an outstanding, sacred, good youth. V.
EDCS-03300918.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Iulius Extricatus, a distinguished young man, probably from the Papirian voting tribe.

EDCS-03300919.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Donatilla vixit annos XII hic sita est
EDCS-03300919.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Donatilla lived twelve years; she lies here.
EDCS-03300919.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Donatilla, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-03300920.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Larius Maximus Saturninus vixit annis XXXC Larius Saturninus fratri carissimo fecit hic situs est
EDCS-03300920.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Larius Maximus Saturninus lived for 80 years. Larius Saturninus made this for his dearest brother. He lies here.
EDCS-03300920.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the life of Larius Maximus Saturninus, made by his brother Larius Saturninus around the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300921.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Leoncius vixit annos LXXXI
EDCS-03300921.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Leoncius lived for 81 years.
EDCS-03300921.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Leoncius, who lived to an advanced age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300922.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Longeius Vindex vixit annos XXXV hic situs est
EDCS-03300922.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Longeius Vindex lived for 35 years. He lies here.
EDCS-03300922.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Marcus Longeius Vindex, who died at 35 years old.

EDCS-03300923.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Me Catta vixit annos XXXII
EDCS-03300923.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Me Catta lived for 32 years.
EDCS-03300923.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Me Catta, who lived for 32 years.

EDCS-03300924.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Pinario Luci filio Papiria Rufino Theveste vixit annos XXXV fratres piissimi fecerunt hic situs est
EDCS-03300924.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Lucius Pinarius, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, Rufinus from Theveste, who lived for 35 years. His most devoted brothers made this. He lies here.
EDCS-03300924.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by his devoted brothers to Lucius Pinarius Rufinus, a citizen from Theveste, who died at 35 years of age.

EDCS-03300925.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Pompeius Martialis vixit annos XXX Pompeius Telesinus fratri
EDCS-03300925.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Pompeius Martialis lived for 30 years. Pompeius Telesinus [made this] for his brother.
EDCS-03300925.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Pompeius Telesinus for his brother Quintus Pompeius Martialis, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-03300926.text_edition: D(is) M(anibus) s(acrum) Pompeia  vixit annos LX meritae matri filii fecerunt
EDCS-03300926.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Pompeia lived for 60 years. Her sons made this for their deserving mother.
EDCS-03300926.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by sons to their deserving mother Pompeia, who lived for 60 years.

EDCS-03300927.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Postumius Saturninus vixit annos LIIII hic situs
EDCS-03300927.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Postumius Saturninus lived for 54 years. He lies here.
EDCS-03300927.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Postumius Saturninus, who lived to be 54 years old and is buried here.

EDCS-03300928.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Titinia Sapiens vixit annos XXXV dies XV Pompeius Telesinus maritus fecit
EDCS-03300928.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titinia Sapiens lived for 35 years and 15 days. Her husband Pompeius Telesinus made this.
EDCS-03300928.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Pompeius Telesinus for his wife Titinia Sapiens, who lived for 35 years and 15 days.

EDCS-03300929.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Turranius Felix filius aram posuit exitum de suo fecit Turranio patri piissimo vixit annis LXXXI
EDCS-03300929.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Turranius Felix, son, placed this altar. He bore the expense from his own funds for his most devoted father Turranius, who lived for 81 years.
EDCS-03300929.summary: This is a funerary altar dedicated by Marcus Turranius Felix to his most devoted father Turranius, who lived to the age of 81, with the son bearing the cost.

EDCS-03300930.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Verria Primitiva pia vixit annos LXXXX Annia Ianuaria filia matri piae fecit hic sita est
EDCS-03300930.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Verria Primitiva, dutiful, lived for 90 years. Annia Ianuaria, her daughter, made this for her dutiful mother. She lies here.
EDCS-03300930.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication by Annia Ianuaria to her mother, Verria Primitiva, who lived to the age of 90.

EDCS-03300931.text_edition: Vettia Cai filia Severina vixit annos II- hic sita est
EDCS-03300931.translation: Vettia Severina, daughter of Gaius, lived for 2- years. She lies here.
EDCS-03300931.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vettia Severina, the daughter of Gaius, whose age at death is partially lost.

EDCS-03300932.text_edition: UmbiLlia#Umbilia#UMBLLIA Cai filia Victoria pia vixit annis XXIII hic sita est Firmus vir fecit
EDCS-03300932.translation: Umbilia Victoria, dutiful daughter of Gaius, lived for 23 years. She lies here. Firmus, her husband, made this.
EDCS-03300932.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Firmus, the husband, to his dutiful wife, Umbilia Victoria, who died at age 23.

EDCS-03300933.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Volumnius Quinti filius Papiria Sollemnis vixit annos LXV hic situs est Quintus Volumnius Amabilis heres patrono piissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-03300933.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Volumnius Sollemnis, son of Quintus, of the Papirian tribe, lived for 65 years. He lies here. Quintus Volumnius Amabilis, his heir, took care to have this made for his most dutiful patron.
EDCS-03300933.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Volumnius Amabilis, the heir, to his most dutiful patron, Quintus Volumnius Sollemnis, who died at age 65.

EDCS-03300935.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Baebiae Primulae vixit  Cael[ius &
EDCS-03300935.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Baebia Primula, who lived [...]. Cael[ius...]
EDCS-03300935.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Baebia Primula, with Cael[ius] possibly being the dedicant, and the age and full dedicatory phrase missing.

EDCS-03300936.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual named [...]rmortus oecurus, who lived for 30 years, with the beginning of the name and the dedicant missing.

EDCS-03300937.text_edition: Euticius annis LVIIII menses VII dies XXII
EDCS-03300937.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the age at death of an individual named Euticius, who lived for 59 years, 7 months, and 22 days.

EDCS-03300938.text_edition: tula vixit annis LX filii fecerunt hic sita est
EDCS-03300938.translation: [...]tula lived for 60 years. Her sons made this. She lies here.
EDCS-03300938.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated by sons to their mother, [...]tula, who lived for 60 years.

EDCS-03300939.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelius Iovinus miles legioni(s) porimae Minerviae militavit aquilifer vixit annos XXX ficit sic Verila marito rarissimo titulum
EDCS-03300939.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelius Iovinus, a soldier of the First Minerva legion, served as an aquilifer (eagle-bearer). He lived for 30 years. Verila made this title for her most exceptional husband.
EDCS-03300939.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Verila to her husband, Aurelius Iovinus, a soldier and aquilifer of the First Minerva Legion, who died at age 30.

EDCS-03300940.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Castricius Crassipes vixit annos VC hic situs est Lucius Castricius Maior patrono fecit
EDCS-03300940.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Castricius Crassipes lived for 95 years. He lies here. Lucius Castricius Maior made this for his patron.
EDCS-03300940.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Castricius Maior to his patron, Lucius Castricius Crassipes, who lived for 95 years.

EDCS-03300941.text_edition: D(is) M(anibus) Iuli Luci filii Natalis vixit annos LVIII menses III hic situs est Helvia Procula uxor marito karissimo fecit Iulii patri fecerunt
EDCS-03300941.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Iulius Natalis, son of Lucius, who lived 58 years and 3 months. He is buried here. Helvia Procula, his wife, made this for her dearest husband. The Iulii made this for their father.
EDCS-03300941.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Iulius Natalis, made by his wife Helvia Procula and his children, the Iulii.

EDCS-03300942.text_edition: Iuliae Lycoridi Quintus Tedius Firmus uxori heres fecit
EDCS-03300942.translation: For Iulia Lycoris. Quintus Tedius Firmus, her heir, made this for his wife.
EDCS-03300942.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Lycoris by her husband, Quintus Tedius Firmus, acting as her heir.

EDCS-03300943.text_edition: Marcus Servilius Severus pius vixit annos V menses II dies XX hic est situs
EDCS-03300943.translation: Marcus Servilius Severus, a dutiful child, lived 5 years, 2 months, and 20 days. He is buried here.
EDCS-03300943.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Servilius Severus, a child who died young, noting his age and burial place.

EDCS-03300944.text_edition: Titus Cassius Maior miles legionis III Augustae centuria Valeri Terti vixit annos XXIV militavit nnos V hic situs est Caius Mevius optio bene merenti
EDCS-03300944.translation: Titus Cassius Maior, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, from the century of Valerius Tertius, lived 24 years, served 5 years. He is buried here. Gaius Mevius, an optio, made this for his well-deserving comrade.
EDCS-03300944.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Cassius Maior, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, set up by his optio, Gaius Mevius, commemorating his service and life.

EDCS-03300950.text_edition: Lucius Aurelius Urbanus vixit nnos XXV militavit nnos VIIII hic situs est Maria Commoda uxor faciendum curavit
EDCS-03300950.translation: Lucius Aurelius Urbanus lived 25 years, served 9 years. He is buried here. Maria Commoda, his wife, took care of having this made.
EDCS-03300950.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aurelius Urbanus, likely a soldier given his military service, arranged by his wife Maria Commoda.

EDCS-03300953.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Munatius Gallus miles legionis III Augustae probatus annos XVI et menses III militavit annos X vixit annos XXVI menses IIII Munatius Victor pater filio piissimo fecit
EDCS-03300953.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Munatius Gallus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, proven for 16 years and 3 months, served 10 years, lived 26 years and 4 months. Munatius Victor, his father, made this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-03300953.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Munatius Gallus, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, erected by his father, Munatius Victor, detailing his military career and short life.

EDCS-03300954.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Munatius Victor vixit annis LXXXV Flavia Avonia uxor piissima fecit
EDCS-03300954.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Munatius Victor lived 85 years. Flavia Avonia, his most dutiful wife, made this.
EDCS-03300954.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Munatius Victor, made by his devoted wife Flavia Avonia, indicating he lived to an advanced age.

EDCS-03300955.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Castus Vaga vixit annos XXX menses VII dies XII hic situs est Cammarenses amico optimo fecerunt
EDCS-03300955.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Castus Vaga lived 30 years, 7 months, and 12 days. He is buried here. The Cammarenses made this for their excellent friend.
EDCS-03300955.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Castus Vaga, indicating his age at death and that it was erected by a group called the Cammarenses for their friend.

EDCS-03300956.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Victoria vixit annos XXIII menses V dies VI hic sita est Iulius Hilarinus coniugi karissimae posuit
EDCS-03300956.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Victoria lived 23 years, 5 months, and 6 days. She is buried here. Iulius Hilarinus placed this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-03300956.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Victoria, detailing her age, and dedicated by her husband, Iulius Hilarinus.

EDCS-03300957.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Titus Flavius Speratus vixit annos XXXV Furia Pompeia marito piissimo fecit hic situs est
EDCS-03300957.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titus Flavius Speratus lived 35 years. Furia Pompeia made this for her most dutiful husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-03300957.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Speratus, indicating his age at death and erected by his wife, Furia Pompeia.

EDCS-03300958.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Annius Marci libertus Verecundus pius vixit annos XXX Marcus Annius Martialis |(centurio) legionis III Augustae liberto optimo
EDCS-03300958.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Annius Verecundus, freedman of Marcus, a pious man, lived for 30 years. Marcus Annius Martialis, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, made this for his excellent freedman.
EDCS-03300958.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a Roman centurion, Marcus Annius Martialis, to his freedman, Marcus Annius Verecundus, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-03300959.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Annius Venerius vixit annos XLV menses IIII dies X hic situs est Annius Floridus fratri karissimo et amantissimo fecit
EDCS-03300959.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Annius Venerius lived for 45 years, 4 months, and 10 days; he is buried here. Annius Floridus made this for his dearest and most beloved brother.
EDCS-03300959.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Annius Floridus for his brother, Annius Venerius, who lived for 45 years and some months, expressing fraternal affection.

EDCS-03300960.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iulius Hilarus se vivo sibi et Iuliae Monnicae uxori karissimae posuit Cirilio  vixit annis LV mensibus VII diebus VIII
EDCS-03300960.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Julius Hilarus, while alive, set this up for himself and for Julia Monnica, his dearest wife. Cirilus lived for 55 years, 7 months, and 8 days.
EDCS-03300960.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Julius Hilarus during his lifetime for himself and his beloved wife, Julia Monnica, also mentioning Cirilus' lifespan.

EDCS-03700799.text_edition: Feliciter Faustus votum libens animo solvit
EDCS-03700799.translation: Happily. Faustus willingly fulfilled his vow with a grateful heart.
EDCS-03700799.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Faustus happily and willingly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-03700802.text_edition: Salvo iure dominicae Restutus de suo propriae in is praediis restituit et dedicavit
EDCS-03700802.translation: With rights of ownership preserved, Restutus, from his own means, restored and dedicated this on these properties.
EDCS-03700802.summary: This inscription records that Restutus, at his own expense and while respecting existing property rights, restored and dedicated something on his properties.

EDCS-03700814.text_edition: Amineum vetus Imperatore Caesare Marco Crasso consulibus
EDCS-03700814.translation: Old Amineum. During the consulship of Imperator Caesar Marcus Crassus.
EDCS-03700814.summary: This inscription appears to date an event or restoration related to 'Old Amineum' to the consulship of Marcus Crassus, likely indicating a specific historical period.

EDCS-03700815.text_edition: Invicti  Vetus III Marco Druso Libone
EDCS-03700815.translation: Of the Invincible. Vetus, for the third time, with Marcus Drusus Libo.
EDCS-03700815.summary: This fragmented inscription likely refers to a consulship or an event dated by the iteration of an official named Vetus and Marcus Drusus Libo, possibly under the patronage of an 'Invictus' deity or emperor.

EDCS-03700816.text_edition: Caio Furnio Caio Silanio consulibus  Invicti
EDCS-03700816.translation: During the consulship of Gaius Furnius and Gaius Silanus. Of the Invincible.
EDCS-03700816.summary: This inscription dates an event or monument, possibly related to 'the Invincible' (Invictus), to the consulship of Gaius Furnius and Gaius Silanus.

EDCS-04000829.translation: To Imperator Nerva Caesar Augustus, sacred. Lucius Granius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, Honoratus, twice duumvir, supervised the making of this.
EDCS-04000829.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, with Lucius Granius Honoratus, a duumvir, overseeing its construction.

EDCS-04000907.text_edition: Marco Cornelio Frontoni Quirina Gabiniano equiti Romano ex inquisitione allecto flamini perpetuo IIvirali honestae memoriae viro Marci Corneli Victorini flaminis perpetui bis IIviralis filio splendissimus ordo et populus coloniae Madaurensium ob insignem in se amorem et frumenti copiam tempore inopiae sibi largiter praestitam honorem bigae et statuae decreverunt pecunia publica quam Corneliae Romanilla Postumiana et Victorina Claudiana et Eulogia Romanilla filiae et heredes eius sua pecunia posuerunt sportulis decurionibus et curialibus datis
EDCS-04000907.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Fronto Gabinianus, of the Quirina tribe, a Roman knight chosen by inquiry, perpetual flamen, twice duumvir, a man of honorable memory, son of Marcus Cornelius Victorinus, perpetual flamen and twice duumvir. The most splendid order and people of the colony of Madaura, on account of his remarkable love for them and the abundance of grain he generously provided in a time of scarcity, decreed him the honor of a biga and a statue, at public expense. Cornelia Romanilla Postumiana, Victorina Claudiana, and Eulogia Romanilla, his daughters and heirs, erected it with their own money, having distributed gifts to the decurions and curials.
EDCS-04000907.summary: This grand inscription commemorates Marcus Cornelius Fronto Gabinianus, a Roman knight and prominent local official in Madaura, for his beneficence in providing grain during a shortage; the city decreed a statue and biga in his honor, which his daughters paid for and erected, also providing donations to officials.

EDCS-04001431.translation: You who seek not the accidents of life, O Flavius Victorianus, who innocent in youth, while following dutiful studies... for heu, piety is not worthy... who with religious affection, they placed M[---] in perpetuity... here the earth will cover the body S[---] now resting in peace, his parents.
EDCS-04001431.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely for a young man named Flavius Victorianus, set up by his parents, lamenting his untimely death.

EDCS-04001574.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Babullius Felix, a veteran, lived 80 years; he is buried here. Soetonia Procula, his wife, and Babullius Thevestinus, his son, made this for their dutiful father.
EDCS-04001574.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Babullius Felix, a veteran, erected by his wife Soetonia Procula and his son Babullius Thevestinus.

EDCS-04001576.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavius Ia[---] Flavinus, optio principis, name SAT[---].
EDCS-04001576.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Ia[---] Flavinus, an 'optio principis,' a Roman military rank, with some parts of his name and profession preserved.

EDCS-04001584.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Aelius Marcianus Junior lived 2 years, 5 months, 7 days; he is buried here. Victorinus made this for his son.
EDCS-04001584.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the young Publius Aelius Marcianus Junior, erected by his father Victorinus.

EDCS-04001587.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Baebius Balbus lived 3 years; he is buried here. Quintus Baebius Bassinus, his father, had this made.
EDCS-04001587.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Quintus Baebius Balbus, commissioned by his father Quintus Baebius Bassinus.

EDCS-04001588.translation: To the Divine Shades. Publius Bo[---] Ser[---] lived 5 years, 4 months, 10 days. Sextus B[---] Felix, his father, made this for his most dutiful son; he is buried here.
EDCS-04001588.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Publius Bo[---] Ser[---], erected by his father Sextus B[---] Felix.

EDCS-04001589.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caecilius Felicianus lived 2 years. Felicior made this for his dutiful son.
EDCS-04001589.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilius Felicianus, a child who lived two years, set up by his father Felicior.

EDCS-04001590.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caecilia Saturnina lived 30 years. Felicior made this for his dutiful wife.
EDCS-04001590.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Saturnina, who lived 30 years, erected by her husband Felicior.

EDCS-04001596.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titus Decimius Speratus lived 55+ years... his sister... he is buried here.
EDCS-04001596.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Titus Decimius Speratus, indicating he lived at least 55 years, possibly set up by a sister or other family member.

EDCS-04001598.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Father. Daughter Euodia lived 5 years, 4 months.
EDCS-04001598.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Euodia, who lived 5 years and 4 months, likely set up by her father.

EDCS-04001600.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Flavia Ingenua lived for 45 years. Her husband Insteius Monitus placed this tomb for his most holy and devoted wife, as she requested.
EDCS-04001600.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a tombstone, dedicated by Insteius Monitus to his deceased wife, Flavia Ingenua, who lived to be 45 years old.

EDCS-04001601.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Folius Urbanus lived for [an unknown number of] years. Martialis made this for his devoted son.
EDCS-04001601.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Folius Urbanus, erected by Martialis, presumably his father, for his pious son.

EDCS-04001603.translation: Iulius [...]SME/DIAES[...] lived for 51 years. Curtia(?) [...] made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-04001603.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a man named Iulius [...], who lived 51 years, dedicated by his wife, Curtia, indicating a private funerary monument.

EDCS-04001608.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Memmius Victor lived for 50 years; he is buried here. His sons made this.
EDCS-04001608.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Memmius Victor, who lived 50 years, erected by his sons, indicating a private family tomb.

EDCS-04001609.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Milesia Silvana lived for 45 years. Her husband made this for his devoted wife.
EDCS-04001609.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Milesia Silvana, who lived 45 years, dedicated by her husband, indicating a private funerary monument.

EDCS-04001611.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Petronius Ianuarius lived for 40 years. His wife Flavina placed this for her well-deserving husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-04001611.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Petronius Ianuarius, who lived 40 years, erected by his wife, Flavina, indicating a private tomb.

EDCS-04001615.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Valeria Celsa, daughter of Gaius, a dutiful person, lived for 24 years, 4 months, and 27 days. She is buried here.
EDCS-04001615.summary: This is a detailed funerary inscription for Valeria Celsa, daughter of Gaius, who died at the age of 24, recording her precise lifespan and burial location, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-04001633.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Cornelius Donatus...
EDCS-04001633.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Cornelius Donatus, indicating a private funerary monument.

EDCS-04001634.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulius Saturninus lived for 31 years.
EDCS-04001634.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Saturninus, who lived 31 years, indicating a private funerary monument.

EDCS-04001639.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Valerius Communis, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe...
EDCS-04001639.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Communis, providing his full name and tribal affiliation, indicating a private funerary monument.

EDCS-04001643.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aufidius Messor lived 63 years; he is buried here. Aufidius Messor, his heir, made this for his dearest father.
EDCS-04001643.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely on a tombstone, dedicated by Aufidius Messor, the son, in memory of his father, also named Aufidius Messor, who lived 63 years.

EDCS-04001644.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Amabilis lived 35 years. Her most devoted family members made this for their sister.
EDCS-04001644.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Amabilis, who lived 35 years, erected by her devoted siblings.

EDCS-04001646.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. The children made this for their dearest mother Sabinia(?) Felicula, who lived 76(?) years, and for their father Marcus Julius Cupitus Pius(?), who lived 70 years.
EDCS-04001646.summary: This is a funerary inscription, erected by children, honoring their mother, Sabinia Felicula, and their father, Marcus Julius Cupitus Pius, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-04001647.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Laelia(?) Tertulla lived sixty years.
EDCS-04001647.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription commemorating Laelia Tertulla, noting that she lived for 60 years.

EDCS-04001758.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus Pius, [[Fortunate Augustus]], our lord Magnus Decentius, most noble Caesar, for the fourth time. To our lords Flavius Valentinianus and Valens Augusti, for the fifth time. To our lord Flavius Gratianus, always Augustus.
EDCS-04001758.summary: This inscription records multiple dedications or acclamations to successive Roman emperors: Constantius II (Flavius Claudius Constantinus Pius Felix Augustus, with a damnatio memoriae for the 'Felix Augustus' part), Magnentius as most noble Caesar, Valentinian I and Valens as Augusti, and Gratian as perpetual Augustus.

EDCS-04901012.text_edition: Marcus Aedius Marci filius Teretina Allifis vixit annis LVI militavit annis XXVII centuria Cosconi ossa de Risucade vecta hic situs est
EDCS-04901012.translation: Marcus Aedius, son of Marcus, of the Teretina tribe, from Allifae, lived 56 years, served 27 years as a soldier in the century of Cosconius. His bones, brought from Risucade, are buried here.
EDCS-04901012.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aedius, a Roman soldier from Allifae, detailing his life span, military service, and the relocation of his remains from Risucade.

EDCS-04901013.text_edition: Marti Augusto sacrum Publius Caelius Victor sacerdos et Furnia Donatula uxor votum solverunt libentes animo faciendos curaverunt gradus numero IIII
EDCS-04901013.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Publius Caelius Victor, priest, and Furnia Donatula, his wife, willingly fulfilled their vow. They ensured the making of four steps.
EDCS-04901013.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Mars Augustus by Publius Caelius Victor, a priest, and his wife Furnia Donatula, commemorating their fulfillment of a vow by having four steps constructed.

EDCS-04901014.text_edition: Marti Augusto sacrum pro salute Impperatorum Caessarum Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti et Marci Aureli Antonini Getae Caesaris et Iuliae Augustae matris castrorum Caius Rusticelius Proculus sacerdos pro salute mea et coniugis meae Faoniae Doniatulae et filiorum meorum votum solvi libens animo
EDCS-04901014.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, for the health of the Emperors Caesars Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Geta Caesar, and Julia Augusta, Mother of the Camps. Gaius Rusticelius Proculus, priest, willingly fulfilled his vow for my health and that of my wife Faonia Doniatula and my sons.
EDCS-04901014.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mars Augustus for the well-being of the imperial family—Septimius Severus, Caracalla, Geta, and Julia Domna—made by Gaius Rusticelius Proculus, a priest, also on behalf of his own family.

EDCS-04901015.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti eiusque domus divinae Augustae  Iani custodibus domino Saturno Augusto sacrum  Asidonius Pilatus Saracinus sacerdos pro salute mea et coniugis meae Cassiae Vinniolae et filiorum meorum in anniversali meo votum solvi libens animo
EDCS-04901015.translation: For the health of Emperor Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus and his divine Augustan house, to Janus Custos and lord Saturnus Augustus, sacred. Asidonius Pilatus Saracinus, priest, willingly fulfilled his vow for my health and that of my wife Cassia Vinniola and my sons on my anniversary.
EDCS-04901015.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription made by Asidonius Pilatus Saracinus, a priest, to Janus Custos and Saturnus Augustus on his anniversary, for the health of the emperor Septimius Severus and his imperial family, as well as his own family.

EDCS-04901020.text_edition: Votum sacro Saturno  Rufinus cum suis solvit
EDCS-04901020.translation: Rufinus, with his family, fulfilled his vow to sacred Saturn.
EDCS-04901020.summary: This is a brief dedicatory inscription recording that Rufinus and his family fulfilled a vow to the god Saturn.

EDCS-04901021.text_edition: Ennius Felix Marianus solum templo Telluris cum arboribus duabus copressi sic sacratis concessit
EDCS-04901021.translation: Ennius Felix Marianus granted the ground for the temple of Tellus, with two sacred cypress trees.
EDCS-04901021.summary: This inscription records the donation of land and sacred cypress trees for a temple of Tellus by Ennius Felix Marianus.

EDCS-04901036.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  itemque Quintus Sentius Saturninus equestri dignitate exornatus  hoc opus instituit et post vitam eius perpetuo pe
EDCS-04901036.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Also Quintus Sentius Saturninus, honored with equestrian dignity, initiated this work and after his life, perpetually...
EDCS-04901036.summary: This inscription is dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead and records Quintus Sentius Saturninus, an equestrian, initiating a perpetual work.

EDCS-04901037.text_edition: Marco Aelio Aurelio Caesari consuli II designato III Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate XXIII imperatoris II consulis IIII patris patriae filio Lucius Sempronius Paetus Nivis ob honorem flamonii perpetui ci filii sui HS III milia legitimae summae item
EDCS-04901037.translation: To Marcus Aelius Aurelius Caesar, consul for the second time, designated consul for the third time, son of Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 23rd time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Country. Lucius Sempronius Paetus Nivis, for the honor of his son's perpetual flamonium, [paid] 3,000 sesterces of the legitimate sum, likewise...
EDCS-04901037.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Aelius Aurelius Caesar, son of Antoninus Pius, by Lucius Sempronius Paetus Nivis, who paid 3,000 sesterces for his son's perpetual priesthood.

EDCS-04901038.text_edition: Marti Augusto sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filii divi Commodi fratris divi Pii nepotis divi Hadriani pronepotis divi Traiani Parthici abnepotis divi Nervae adnepotis Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi et Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti et Publi Septimi Getae nobilissimi Caesaris et Iuliae Domnae Augustae matris castrorum Selicius Saturninus Selici Felicis filius ob honorem XIprimatus quem ordo civitatis suae in loco patris eius suffragio detulit ex HS III milibus nummum legitimae summae honorariae adiectis a se HS |(mille) nummum item adiectis a se HS |(mille) D qui fiunt partis suae dimidium de HS III milibus XIprimatus summa honoraria Felicis patris sui defuncti insuper hoc amplius adiecta a se tertia eius summae id est HS D nummum statuae Martis dei quae est ultima pars taxationis HS VI |(milium) nummum sua pecunia fecit idemque dedicavit d(ecreto)? d(ecurionum)
EDCS-04901038.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, for the well-being of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, son of divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of divine Commodus, grandson of divine Pius, great-grandson of divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of divine Nerva, and Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, and Publius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar, and Julia Domna Augusta, Mother of the Camps. Selicius Saturninus, son of Selicius Felix, for the honor of the XI-primacy, which the order of his city bestowed upon him in his father's place by vote, from 3,000 sesterces of the legitimate honorarium sum, with an additional 1,000 sesterces added by himself, likewise an additional 1,500 sesterces added by himself, which amounts to half of his share of the 3,000 sesterces honorarium sum for his deceased father Felix's XI-primacy. Furthermore, he also added a third of that sum, that is, 500 sesterces, for the statue of the god Mars, which is the final part of the 6,000 sesterces taxation, he made at his own expense and dedicated it by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-04901038.summary: This complex inscription is a dedication to Mars Augustus for the imperial family (Septimius Severus and his sons) by Selicius Saturninus, who financed a statue of Mars from an honorarium and additional funds in honor of his father's XI-primacy.

EDCS-04901042.text_edition: Lucio Aelio Aurelio Commodo consuli Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae filio Lucius Manilius Felix ob magistratum ampliata legitima sufetatus summa ex HS milibus nummum posuit decreto decurionum
EDCS-04901042.translation: To Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus, consul, son of Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Father of the Country. Lucius Manilius Felix, for the magistracy, having increased the legitimate sum of the sufetate, erected this from thousands of sesterces by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-04901042.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus, son of Antoninus Pius, by Lucius Manilius Felix, who funded it for a magistracy with increased funds for a suffetate, by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-04901044.text_edition: Saturno Augusto sacrum civitas Chul sufetatus Caius Lepthini et Mani Senti anni LXXXXI
EDCS-04901044.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. The city of Chul [recorded] the suffetate of Gaius Lepthini and Mani Sentius, year 91.
EDCS-04901044.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Saturn Augustus and records the suffetate of Gaius Lepthini and Mani Sentius in the city of Chul in the 91st year.

EDCS-04901050.text_edition: Publio Septimio Getae Caesari Lucius Clodius Quirina Rufinus statuam quam dum adviveret ex HS IImilibus promiserat adiectis ex testamento eius HS IImilibus nummum Lucius Caecilius Quietus Rufinianus Quintus Caecilius Victor Quintianus Blandius flamines perpetui municipii heredes ampliata pecunia posuerunt idemque dedicaverunt
EDCS-04901050.translation: To Publius Septimius Geta Caesar. Lucius Clodius Quirina Rufinus, through his will, had promised a statue worth 2,000 sesterces while he was alive, and with an additional 2,000 sesterces from his will, his heirs, Lucius Caecilius Quietus Rufinianus, Quintus Caecilius Victor Quintianus Blandius, perpetual flamens of the municipality, erected it with increased funds and dedicated it.
EDCS-04901050.summary: This inscription records the erection and dedication of a statue to Publius Septimius Geta Caesar by the heirs of Lucius Clodius Quirina Rufinus, fulfilling his testamentary promise with additional funds.

EDCS-04901051.text_edition: Deo Silvano Augusto Genio civitatis pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici fili divi Pii nepotis divi Hadriani pronepotis divi Traiani Parthici abnepotis divi Nervae adnepotis Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici patris patriae pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate III imperatoris VII consulis II procons(ulis) Castricius Rogatianus Silvani SCI filius ornandae patriae causa fecit et in testamento suo HS II(milia) DV suis legavit ut ob dedicationem universis decurionibus sportulas praestarent
EDCS-04901051.translation: To the god Silvanus Augustus, Genius of the city, for the well-being of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, Father of the Country, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 3rd time, Imperator for the 7th time, Consul for the 2nd time, Proconsul, son of divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus, grandson of divine Pius, great-grandson of divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of divine Nerva. Castricius Rogatianus, son of Silvanus SCI, made this for the purpose of adorning his homeland and in his will bequeathed 2,500 sesterces to his people so that they might provide sportulae (distributions) to all the decurions for the dedication.
EDCS-04901051.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Silvanus Augustus and the Genius of the city, made by Castricius Rogatianus for the well-being of Emperor Septimius Severus and for the adornment of his homeland, with a testamentary provision for distributions to decurions upon dedication.

EDCS-04901052.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Venidius Repostus miles legionis III Augustae centuria Antoni Clementis vixit annis XXXXV hic situs est
EDCS-04901052.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Venidius Repostus, soldier of the III Augustan Legion, century of Antonius Clemens, lived for 45 years. He lies here.
EDCS-04901052.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Venidius Repostus, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, who lived for 45 years.

EDCS-04901053.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Cassius Iscoesius Valens miles cohortis II Flaviae equitatae pius vixit nnos XXXII Anicius Celer Andronicus commilito et contibernalis sic libens fecit hic situs est
EDCS-04901053.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Cassius Iscoesius Valens, a pious soldier of the II Flavian Cohort (mounted), lived for 32 years. Anicius Celer Andronicus, his fellow-soldier and tent-mate, willingly made this. He lies here.
EDCS-04901053.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cassius Iscoesius Valens, a soldier of the Cohors II Flavia Equitata, erected by his comrade Anicius Celer Andronicus.

EDCS-04901057.text_edition: Lucius Obulcius miles legionis III Augustae centuria Sallusti  vixit nnos
EDCS-04901057.translation: Lucius Obulcius, soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, from the century of Sallust, lived [X] years.
EDCS-04901057.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Obulcius, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, likely set up by his comrades or family, dating probably to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-04901059.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Volaseina Honoratus miles legionis III Augustae centuria Cressi pius vixit annos XXI hic situs est
EDCS-04901059.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Volaseina Honoratus, a devoted soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, from the century of Cressus, lived 21 years. He lies here.
EDCS-04901059.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Volaseina Honoratus, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, likely erected by his family or comrades, dating probably to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05000782.text_edition: Vanammoni Augusto sacrum Aurelius Varixen ordinarius qui ex fortia et suffragio vexillationi profecit ex voto a solo extruxit libens animo
EDCS-05000782.translation: Sacred to Vanammon Augustus. Aurelius Varixen, an ordinarius who advanced to the vexillation through bravery and suffrage, built this from its foundations as a vow, with a willing spirit.
EDCS-05000782.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Aurelius Varixen, an ordinarius, who built a monument for Vanammon Augustus after being promoted to a vexillation, probably dating to the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05000815.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Delius Felicissimus avunculus dulcissimus vixit annis LVI mense I diebus XVI
EDCS-05000815.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Delius Felicissimus, a most sweet uncle, lived 56 years, 1 month, and 16 days.
EDCS-05000815.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Delius Felicissimus, likely set up by a relative to honor his memory, dating probably to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05000823.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Alexandria pia vixit plus minus annos XXVIII quod fuit scriptum debitum communem reddidit cui Galata Augusti nostri verna dispensator marmorum Numidicorum alumnae merenti fecit
EDCS-05000823.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Alexandria, a devoted woman, lived more or less 28 years. What was written, a common debt, she repaid. To her, Galata, a slave and dispenser of Numidian marble for our Augustus, made this for his deserving foster-daughter.
EDCS-05000823.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Alexandria, erected by Galata, an imperial slave and marble dispenser, for his foster-daughter, dating probably to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-06000019.text_edition: Divae Faustinae Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii
EDCS-06000019.translation: To the deified Faustina, wife of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius.
EDCS-06000019.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diva Faustina, the deified wife of Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely erected after her death in 140 AD.

EDCS-06000024.text_edition: Marco Annio Vero Augusti filio  Lucio Aurelio Commodo Augusti filio  Lucillae Augustae
EDCS-06000024.translation: To Marcus Annius Verus, son of the Emperor; to Lucius Aurelius Commodus, son of the Emperor; to Lucilla Augusta.
EDCS-06000024.summary: This inscription is a dedication to three members of the imperial family, likely from the time of Marcus Aurelius, honoring his sons and daughter, dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-06000027.text_edition: Fadillae Augusti filiae  Cornificiae Augusti filiae  Faustinae Augusti filiae
EDCS-06000027.translation: To Fadilla, daughter of the Emperor; to Cornificia, daughter of the Emperor; to Faustina, daughter of the Emperor.
EDCS-06000027.summary: This inscription is a dedication to three imperial princesses, daughters of an unnamed emperor, likely from the Antonine or Severan dynasty, dating to the 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-06000051.text_edition: Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari Pio Felici Augusto principi iuventutis domino nostro Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Iuli Philippi Pii Felicis Augusti domini nostri filio Sabrathenses publice
EDCS-06000051.translation: To Marcus Iulius Philippus, most noble Caesar, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, prince of youth, our lord, son of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, our lord. The people of Sabratha publicly [erected this].
EDCS-06000051.summary: This is a public dedication from the city of Sabratha to Marcus Iulius Philippus (Philippus II), son of Emperor Philip the Arab, honoring him as Caesar and future emperor, dating to 247-249 AD.

EDCS-06000052.text_edition: Quinto Herennio Etrusco Messio Decio  filio  Augusti domini nostri tribunicia [potestate &
EDCS-06000052.translation: To Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, son of the Emperor, our lord, with tribunician power [and other titles].
EDCS-06000052.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, son of Emperor Decius, honoring him with imperial titles, dating to 251 AD, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-06000053.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Gallieno Pii Felici Augusto domino nostro pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XIIII consuli VI designato VII patri patriae proconsuli et Corneliae Saloninae Augustae coniugi Gallieni Augusti Sabrathenses publice
EDCS-06000053.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, our lord, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 14th time, consul for the 6th time, designated for the 7th, father of the country, proconsul, and to Cornelia Salonina Augusta, wife of Gallienus Augustus, the Sabrathenses officially [erected this].
EDCS-06000053.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the people of Sabratha to Emperor Gallienus and his wife Cornelia Salonina, detailing their imperial titles and honors, likely for a statue or public work, dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-06000058.text_edition: Aedem Liberi Patris quam antiqua ruina cum labe PIVS instaurationem EARISO ddominorum nnostrorum Flavi Iuli Constantii maximi et Flavi Iuli Constantis maximi triumphatorum semper Augustorum  amrimum praesidicium viri perfectissimi Flavi Victoris Calpurni servavit hanc Lucius Aemilius Caelestinus duovir quinquennalis flamen perpetuus amori patriae studiose respondita viro perfectissimo patrono provinciae dedicanteR fecit ex decreto ordinis
EDCS-06000058.translation: The temple of Liber Pater, which an ancient ruin with damage preserved with pious restoration during the reign of our lords Flavius Iulius Constantius Maximus and Flavius Iulius Constans Maximus, always triumphant Augusti, under the strong protection of the most perfect man Flavius Victor Calpurnius, this Lucius Aemilius Caelestinus, duovir quinquennalis, flamen perpetuus, zealously restored out of love for his homeland and dedicated through the most perfect man, patron of the province, acting on a decree of the council.
EDCS-06000058.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to Liber Pater by Lucius Aemilius Caelestinus, a local magistrate, dedicated under the patronage of Flavius Victor Calpurnius during the reign of Emperors Constantius and Constans, likely in the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-06000102.text_edition: Avitio Quinti filio Quirina (tribu) Rufo tribuno militum legionis VII geminae felicis praefecto cohortis I Augustae Thracum proximo tertiae militiae IIviro decreto ordinis ob merita eius erga rem publicam eximia Quintus Avitius Lucanus pater titulo et honore contentus sua pecunia posuit
EDCS-06000102.translation: To Avitius Rufus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, military tribune of the Seventh Legion Gemina Felix, prefect of the First Cohort Augusta Thracum, next in command for a third military service, duovir by decree of the council, on account of his outstanding merits towards the republic; Quintus Avitius Lucanus, his father, content with the title and honor, placed this at his own expense.
EDCS-06000102.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument or honorary dedication erected by Quintus Avitius Lucanus for his son, Avitius Rufus, detailing his military and civilian career, likely dating to the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-06000106.text_edition: DEM statuam co curante Calpurnio viro perfectissimo praeside provinciae Tripolitanae
EDCS-06000106.translation: [---] a statue, with the most perfect man Calpurnius, governor of the province of Tripolitana, overseeing the work.
EDCS-06000106.summary: This fragment likely records the dedication of a statue, with Calpurnius, the governor of Tripolitana, supervising its erection.

EDCS-06000109.text_edition: Flavi Vivi Benedicti viri perfectissimi totius integritatis moderationis iustitiae provisionis fidei benignitatis fortitudinis ac beneficentiae viro Flavio Vivio Benedicto viro perfectissimo praesidi provinciae Tripolitanae inter cetera beneficia sua quibus omnem provinciam conpendiis sic remediis et virtutibus fovit sublevabit erexit etiam ob ea quae sibi specialiter conlata sunt civitas Sabrathensis exsultans quod post ruinam et abnegatum thermarum populo exercitium citra ullius dispendium ornamentis patriae revocavit ordo populusque concinentibus omnibus votis statuam patrono praestantissimo gratanti studio conlocavit  Dedicata consulum dominorum nostrorum Valentis VI et Valentiniani II Augustorum die V Kalendas Augustas
EDCS-06000109.translation: To Flavius Vivius Benedictus, most perfect man of complete integrity, moderation, justice, foresight, faithfulness, kindness, fortitude, and beneficence; to Flavius Vivius Benedictus, most perfect man, governor of the province of Tripolitana, among his other good deeds by which he fostered, relieved, and also raised the whole province with gains, remedies, and virtues, for those things specifically bestowed upon it, the rejoicing city of Sabratha, because after its ruin and the denied use of the baths to the populace, he recalled them to the ornaments of the homeland without any cost to anyone, the council and people, with all votes concurring, placed this statue to their most excellent patron with grateful zeal. Dedicated on the 5th day before the Kalends of August (July 28) during the consulship of our lords Valens (for the 6th time) and Valentinian (for the 2nd time) Augusti.
EDCS-06000109.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue by the city of Sabratha to Flavius Vivius Benedictus, the governor of Tripolitana, in gratitude for his services, especially the restoration of the public baths, dated to July 28, 378 AD.

EDCS-06000114.text_edition: Dis manibus sacrum Varigata Lucius Munatius Dexter veteranus pius vixit annis LV ossa tibi bene quiescant terra tibi levis sit hic situs est
EDCS-06000114.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Varigata. Lucius Munatius Dexter, a pious veteran, lived for 55 years. May your bones rest well; may the earth be light upon you. He is buried here.
EDCS-06000114.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Munatius Dexter, a veteran who lived 55 years, expressing wishes for his peaceful rest, typical of Roman tombstones.

EDCS-06000115.text_edition: Ulpiano V sagittario natione  nus nnos plus minus  directo anni curante Flavio P
EDCS-06000115.translation: To Ulpianus V, archer, of the [---] nation, [---] years more or less, [---] directed in the year, with Flavius P. [---] overseeing.
EDCS-06000115.summary: This fragment is likely part of a dedication or record pertaining to an archer named Ulpianus, possibly military, with Flavius P. mentioned as an overseer, but too incomplete for a full understanding.

EDCS-06000119.text_edition: Publio o Publi filio Galeria (tribu) Maximo Numerio Avito V G C V rarissimo Sabrathenses publice patrono
EDCS-06000119.translation: To Publius Maximus Numerius Avitus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, Vir Clarissimus (most distinguished man), Sabrathenses publicly [dedicate this] to their most rare patron.
EDCS-06000119.summary: This inscription is an honorary dedication by the people of Sabratha to Publius Maximus Numerius Avitus, a high-ranking individual designated as Vir Clarissimus and their patron, likely for a statue.

EDCS-06000120.text_edition: Aurelius Flacianus P sacerdotalis scola felix et cum uiveret moreretur fuit fil  vixit annis L
EDCS-06000120.translation: Aurelius Flacianus P., a successful priest, both in life and death, was a son [---]. He lived for 50 years.
EDCS-06000120.summary: This appears to be a funerary inscription for Aurelius Flacianus P., a priest, noting his age and perhaps a familial relationship, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-06000121.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, mentioning a son of Cornelius and aediles, suggesting a public work or monument sponsored by local magistrates.

EDCS-06000168.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a name, with too little information to determine its specific purpose or date.

EDCS-06000169.text_edition: Dis Manibus Domiti simi tri V
EDCS-06000169.translation: To the Divine Shades of Domitius simi tri V.
EDCS-06000169.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the deceased, Domitius, whose full name or title is incomplete.

EDCS-06000174.text_edition: i iussit   et in singul  Proculus S
EDCS-06000174.translation: He ordered and in single Proculus S[...]
EDCS-06000174.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly from a public decree or building project, mentioning Proculus.

EDCS-06000175.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory monument, mentioning Rufus and his filiation.

EDCS-06000178.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely part of a name, with too little information to determine its specific purpose or date.

EDCS-06000202.text_edition: ΑΩ Bonae memoriae Angelas bixit sic in pace annos XIII plus minus requiebit die calendas Apriles
EDCS-06000202.translation: Alpha Omega. To the good memory of Angelas. He lived thus in peace for about 13 years. He rests on the day of the Kalends of April.
EDCS-06000202.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription commemorating Angelas, who died at approximately 13 years of age and was buried on April 1st, likely from the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-06000203.text_edition: spiritus Athica bonae memoriae dulcis nomen anima mellea petimus orationibus tuis exaudiat nos vixit annos LXVII
EDCS-06000203.translation: Spirit of Athica of good memory, sweet name, honeyed soul, we ask that you hear us in your prayers. She lived 67 years.
EDCS-06000203.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Athica, who lived 67 years, invoking her spirit to intercede in prayers, indicating a belief in the continued efficacy of the deceased in the afterlife.

EDCS-06000205.text_edition: Biocena bonae memoriae bixit sic nnos XXXV plus minus et in XRChristo requievit sub die XV Kalendas Maias indictione VII
EDCS-06000205.translation: To Biocena of good memory. She lived thus for about 35 years and rested in Christ on the 15th day before the Kalends of May, in the 7th indiction.
EDCS-06000205.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Biocena, who lived about 35 years, explicitly stating her death in Christ on April 17th, dated by the 7th indiction, likely from the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-06000206.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Bonus bixit sic in pake sic plus minus annos LXXX et requiebit sic in pace
EDCS-06000206.translation: To the good memory of Bonus. He lived thus in peace for about 80 years and rests thus in peace.
EDCS-06000206.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Bonus, who lived approximately 80 years, emphasizing his peaceful life and eternal rest.

EDCS-06000207.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Corcondiae bixit sic plus minus annos XII
EDCS-06000207.translation: To the good memory of Corcondia. She lived thus for about 12 years.
EDCS-06000207.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription commemorating Corcondia, who lived for approximately 12 years.

EDCS-06000208.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Crisomali bixit sic in pace annos LX plus minus
EDCS-06000208.translation: To the good memory of Crisomalus. He lived in peace for approximately 60 years.
EDCS-06000208.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, Crisomalus, who lived approximately 60 years.

EDCS-06000209.text_edition: Α Ω Hik requiebit sic bonae memoriae S Dominika sic bixit sic in pake sic annos decem plus minus sub die III mensis Maii indictione XI
EDCS-06000209.translation: Alpha Omega. Here rests to good memory Saint Dominika. She lived in peace for approximately ten years, on the 3rd day of the month of May, in the 11th indiction.
EDCS-06000209.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Saint Dominika, who died at approximately ten years of age on May 3rd of an 11th indiction year.

EDCS-06000210.text_edition: Bom(nae) memoriae Esidorus bixit sic in pace annos LXX plus minus sub die XI mensis Octobris indictione IIII
EDCS-06000210.translation: To the good memory of Esidorus. He lived in peace for approximately 70 years, dying on the 11th day of the month of October, in the 4th indiction.
EDCS-06000210.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Esidorus, who lived about 70 years and passed away on October 11th of a 4th indiction year.

EDCS-06000211.text_edition: Α Ω Bonae memoriae Ferrandus bixit in pace nnos XL plus minus sub die XII Kalendas Maias
EDCS-06000211.translation: Alpha Omega. To the good memory of Ferrandus. He lived in peace for approximately 40 years, dying on the 12th day before the Kalends of May (April 20th).
EDCS-06000211.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Ferrandus, who lived about 40 years and died on April 20th.

EDCS-06000212.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Fuzani bixit sic in pake sic annos XCV plus minus et requiebit sic in pake sic sub die VIII mensis Februari indictione XII
EDCS-06000212.translation: To the good memory of Fuzanus. He lived in peace for approximately 95 years, and he rests in peace on the 8th day of the month of February, in the 12th indiction.
EDCS-06000212.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fuzanus, who lived approximately 95 years and died on February 8th of a 12th indiction year.

EDCS-06000213.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Georgi bixit sic in pace annos plus minus XX sub die XXII mensis Aprilis indictione XI
EDCS-06000213.translation: To the good memory of Georgius. He lived in peace for approximately 20 years, dying on the 22nd day of the month of April, in the 11th indiction.
EDCS-06000213.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Georgius, who lived about 20 years and died on April 22nd of an 11th indiction year.

EDCS-06000214.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Ianacis bixit sic annos III bonae memoriae Iobris bixit sic in pace annos VI plus minus
EDCS-06000214.translation: To the good memory of Ianacis. He lived for 3 years. To the good memory of Iobris. He lived in peace for approximately 6 years.
EDCS-06000214.summary: This is a double funerary inscription commemorating Ianacis, who lived 3 years, and Iobris, who lived approximately 6 years.

EDCS-06000215.text_edition: Ioanes bonae me moriae bixit sic annos LXXXX
EDCS-06000215.translation: Ioanes, of good memory. He lived for 90 years.
