EDCS-06000215.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name of the deceased, Ioanes, and his age of 90 years.

EDCS-06000216.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Ioanes bixit sic in pace annos LXXXV plus minus suY(b)#su(b)#SY die XXVII mensis Ianuari indictione V
EDCS-06000216.translation: To the good memory of Ioanes. He lived in peace for approximately 85 years, dying on the 27th day of the month of January, in the 5th indiction.
EDCS-06000216.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ioanes, who lived about 85 years and died on January 27th of a 5th indiction year.

EDCS-06000217.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Iohannes in XPO requievit vixit nnm unum menses VIIII dies XX sub die XIII mensis Aprilis indictione VIII
EDCS-06000217.translation: To the good memory of Iohannes. He rested in Christ, having lived one year, nine months, and twenty days, dying on the 13th day of the month of April, in the 8th indiction.
EDCS-06000217.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iohannes, who lived for a year, nine months, and twenty days, dying on April 13th of an 8th indiction year.

EDCS-06000219.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Irene bixit sic in pace annos VIIII plus minus sub die XIII mensis Maii indictione XI
EDCS-06000219.translation: To the good memory of Irene, who lived in peace for nine years, more or less, on the 13th day of the month of May, in the 11th indiction.
EDCS-06000219.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Irene, noting her age and the date of her death, likely from late antiquity.

EDCS-06000221.text_edition: Indictione III Bonae memoriae Liberatae bixit sic annos XL plus minus pauci et mali et requiebit sic in Christo mense Iulio com VIII  Audias in illadie bocem Domini Iesu Christi dicentis remissa sunt tibi unibersa delicta
EDCS-06000221.translation: In the 3rd indiction, to the good memory of Liberata, who lived for 40 years, more or less, few and evil, and rested thus in Christ in the month of July, on the 8th day. May you hear on that day the voice of the Lord Jesus Christ saying, 'All your sins are forgiven.'
EDCS-06000221.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Liberata, recording her age, date of death, and including a plea for divine forgiveness, likely from late antiquity.

EDCS-06000223.text_edition: Α Ω Marcella bonae memoriae in XRChristo requievit die Kalendarum Ianuariarum indictione θ
EDCS-06000223.translation: Alpha Omega. Marcella, of good memory, rested in Christ on the Kalends of January, in the 9th indiction.
EDCS-06000223.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Marcella, noting her death date with an Alpha and Omega symbol, likely from late antiquity.

EDCS-06000225.text_edition: In nomine Domini (Iesus) Christi memoria Nadese filia Misekor bixit sic nnos VII dies LX  reqie
EDCS-06000225.translation: In the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, memory of Nades, daughter of Misekor, who lived for seven years, sixty days. She rests.
EDCS-06000225.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for a young girl named Nades, daughter of Misekor, stating her age and that she rests in Christ.

EDCS-06000227.text_edition: Ispiritus bonae memoriae tecum Segeddutis qui bixit sic annis LX plus minus
EDCS-06000227.translation: May the spirit of good memory be with you, Segeddus, who lived for 60 years, more or less.
EDCS-06000227.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Segeddus and stating his approximate age at death.

EDCS-06000228.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Stefanus bixit sic in pace annos LV indictione VIII
EDCS-06000228.translation: To the good memory of Stephanus, who lived in peace for 55 years, in the 8th indiction.
EDCS-06000228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Stephanus, recording his age and the indiction year of his death, likely from late antiquity.

EDCS-06000229.translation: Here rests Stidin, of good memory, a cleric. He lived in peace for nine years and rested in peace.
EDCS-06000229.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for a cleric named Stidin, noting his profession, age, and peaceful rest.

EDCS-06000230.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Teodora bixit sic in pace annos X plus minus
EDCS-06000230.translation: To the good memory of Theodora, who lived in peace for ten years, more or less.
EDCS-06000230.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Theodora, stating her approximate age and that she lived in peace.

EDCS-06000231.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Hteodorus vixit in pace nnos plus minus XX et requieuit cum patre BS mense Iulio  indictione quinta Ω Α
EDCS-06000231.translation: To the good memory of Theodorus, who lived in peace for twenty years, more or less, and rested with the father, BS, in the month of July, in the fifth indiction. Omega Alpha.
EDCS-06000231.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Theodorus, noting his approximate age, month of death, and an Omega and Alpha symbol, and possibly indicating he rested with a 'father BS'.

EDCS-06000247.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Aemilio Pacoro qui vixit annis L menses IIII dies X Aemilia Calliope coniux marito piissimo fecit
EDCS-06000247.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilia Calliope made this for her most devoted husband Lucius Aemilius Pacorus, who lived for 50 years, 4 months, 10 days.
EDCS-06000247.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aemilia Calliope to her very devoted husband, Lucius Aemilius Pacorus, detailing his precise age at death.

EDCS-06000261.text_edition: In ok tumulo positus est bonae memoriae Ianes biksit sic in pake sic plus minus annos XXX rekiebit sic sub karta decima mensis Aprilis

EDCS-06000262.text_edition: cia Narsis laris filia  vixit plus minus annos VIII  menses VI

EDCS-06000264.text_edition: Bonae memoriae Voilim  in pace bixit sic annos

EDCS-06000268.text_edition: Publius Cornelius Attax Marcianus Lucius Appius Amicus Rufinianus curratores refectionis thermarum tertium deo Aesculapio  votum solverunt

EDCS-06000273.text_edition: Cereri Augustae sacrum Caius Rubellius Blandus consul pontifex proconsul dedicavit Suphunibal ornatrix patriae Annobalis Rusonis de sua pecunia faciendum curavit

EDCS-06000274.text_edition: Cereri Luciferae Antoniae Quinti filiae Victorinae coniugi rarissimae QQuintus Iulius Iustus ex decreto ordinis

EDCS-06000277.text_edition: Dis Augustis Quintus Marcius Cai filius Barea consul XVvir sacris faciundis fetialis proconsul II patronus dedicavit Iddibal Magonis filius Tapapius Lepcitanus de sua pecunia fecit

EDCS-06000279.text_edition: Dibus Lepcis Magnae Marcus Vipsanius Clemens redemtor marmorarius templi Liberi Patris de sua pecunia votum solvit sub cura Quinti Servili Candidi amatoris patriae amatoris civium ornatoris patriae flaminis divi Vespasiani

EDCS-06000280.text_edition: Fortunae Iuliae Quinti filiae Iustae Iustus pater ex decreto ordinis

EDCS-06000281.text_edition: Fortunae Crescenti et Horae Bonae Hostiliae Quinti filiae Honestae Quintus Iulius Iustus avos ex decreto ordinis
EDCS-06000281.translation: To Fortuna Crescens and Hora Bona Honesta, daughter of Quintus, her grandfather Quintus Iulius Iustus (erected this) by decree of the town council.
EDCS-06000281.summary: This inscription records Quintus Iulius Iustus dedicating a monument to Fortuna Crescens and his granddaughter, Hora Bona Honesta, following a decree by the local council.

EDCS-06000284.text_edition: Genio coloniae Lepcis Magnae Ulpius Rogatus Pao structor dono dedit
EDCS-06000284.translation: To the Genius of the colony of Lepcis Magna, Ulpius Rogatus Pao, a builder, gave this gift.
EDCS-06000284.summary: This inscription records Ulpius Rogatus Pao, a builder, donating a gift to the protective spirit (Genius) of the colony of Lepcis Magna.

EDCS-06000294.text_edition: Iuliae Domnae Augustae deae Iunoni orbis terrae Quintus Fulvius Dida Bibulianus votum solvit
EDCS-06000294.translation: To Julia Domna Augusta, goddess Juno of the whole world, Quintus Fulvius Dida Bibulianus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-06000294.summary: This inscription records Quintus Fulvius Dida Bibulianus fulfilling a vow to Julia Domna Augusta, here identified with the goddess Juno of the world, likely in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000296.text_edition: Libero Patri sacrum Boncarth Muthumbalis filius Sydby sic IIIIvir macelli ex multis |(denariorum) LXII quibus adiecit de suo |(denarios) LIII
EDCS-06000296.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Boncarth, son of Muthumbal, Sydby, IVvir of the marketplace, (used) 62 denarii from many (sources), to which he added 53 denarii from his own funds.
EDCS-06000296.summary: This inscription records Boncarth, son of Muthumbal, a IVvir of the marketplace in Sydby, dedicating a sacred monument to Liber Pater, funding it partially from public money and partially from his own funds.

EDCS-06000297.text_edition: Iovigena Liber Pater votum quod destinaveram Lari Severi patrio Iovigenae solis mei Pudens pater pro filio ob tribunatus candidam et ob praeturam proximam tantamque in nos princippum conlatam indulgentiam conpos votorum omnium dentes duos Lucae bovis Indorum tuorum dico
EDCS-06000297.translation: To Jupiter-born Liber Pater, the vow I had destined for the ancestral Lar of Severus, my sun god Jupiter-born, I, Pudens the father, for my son, on account of his successful candidacy for the tribunate and his upcoming praetorship, and so great the indulgence bestowed upon us by the emperors, having obtained all my vows, I dedicate two teeth of your Indian bull, Lucas.
EDCS-06000297.summary: This inscription records Pudens dedicating two teeth of an Indian bull to Liber Pater, identified with Jupiter, to fulfill a vow made for his son's successful political career (tribunate and upcoming praetorship), acknowledging the emperors' favor.

EDCS-06000302.text_edition: Marti Augusto sacrum auspiciis Imperatoris Caesaris Augusti pontificis maxumi patris patriae ductu Cossi Lentuli consulis XVviri sacris faciundis proconsulis provincia Africa bello Gaetulico liberata civitas Lepcitana
EDCS-06000302.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Under the auspices of Imperator Caesar Augustus, pontifex maximus, father of the country, and the leadership of Cossus Lentulus, consul, quindecimvir sacris faciundis, proconsul, the city of Lepcis was freed in the province of Africa after the Gaetulian war.
EDCS-06000302.summary: This inscription records the city of Lepcis dedicating a monument to Mars Augustus in thanks for its liberation during the Gaetulian War, acknowledging the leadership of Augustus and Proconsul Cossus Lentulus, likely dating to 6 CE.

EDCS-06000308.text_edition: Augusta salutaris Caius Vibius Marsus pro consul III dedicavit
EDCS-06000308.translation: Augusta Salutaris. Gaius Vibius Marsus, proconsul for the third time, dedicated this.
EDCS-06000308.summary: This inscription records Gaius Vibius Marsus, serving his third term as proconsul, dedicating a monument to Augusta Salutaris.

EDCS-06000310.text_edition: Lucius Tettius Eutychus in suo sua pecunia cisternam Veneri et Leptitanis donui sic cura Luci Tetti Comi liberti faciendam curavit
EDCS-06000310.translation: Lucius Tettius Eutychus, on his own land and at his own expense, gave this cistern as a gift to Venus and the people of Leptis; Lucius Tettius Comus, his freedman, oversaw its construction.
EDCS-06000310.summary: This inscription records Lucius Tettius Eutychus donating a cistern, built on his own land and at his own expense, to Venus and the citizens of Leptis, with his freedman Lucius Tettius Comus supervising the construction.

EDCS-06000312.text_edition: Numini Veneris Adquisitricis Augustae sacrum Iucundus Augusti nostri verna vegtigalis sic IIII publicorum Africae vilicus Lepcis magnae terrestris de suo posuit
EDCS-06000312.translation: Sacred to the Divinity of Venus Acquisitrix Augusta. Iucundus, our Augustus's native slave and manager of the four public taxes of Africa, manager of the terrestrial Lepcis Magna, erected this from his own funds.
EDCS-06000312.summary: This inscription records Iucundus, a slave of the emperor and manager of taxes for Africa and Lepcis Magna, erecting a monument to the divinity of Venus Acquisitrix Augusta from his own funds.

EDCS-06000313.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Pii liberorumque eius Veneri Calchidicae Cupidinis statuam Caius Claudius Septimius Afer dono dedit dedicavit posuit
EDCS-06000313.translation: For the well-being of Imperator Antoninus Augustus Pius and his children, Gaius Claudius Septimius Afer gave, dedicated, and erected this statue of Cupid to Venus Chalcidica.
EDCS-06000313.summary: This inscription records Gaius Claudius Septimius Afer dedicating and erecting a statue of Cupid to Venus Chalcidica, on behalf of the well-being of Emperor Antoninus Pius and his children, likely in the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-06000319.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-06000319.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Divi filio Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XXIV consule XIII patre patriae Annobal Rufus ornator patriae amator concordiae flamen sufes praefectus sacrorum Himilchonis Tapapi filius de sua pecunia faciendum coeravit idemque dedicavit
EDCS-06000319.translation: To Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the Deified, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 24th time, consul for the 13th time, father of the country: Annobal Rufus, an adornment of his country, lover of concord, flamen, sufes, prefect of sacred rites, son of Himilcho Tapapi, took care to have this made from his own money and also dedicated it.
EDCS-06000319.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a public work, funded and dedicated by Annobal Rufus, a local magistrate of Leptis Magna, in honor of Emperor Augustus during his 24th tribunician power and 13th consulship (1 CE).

EDCS-06000320.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Divi filio Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XXIV consule XIII patre patriae Annobal ornator patriae amator concordiae flamen sufes praefectus sacrorum Himilchonis Tapapi filius Rufus de sua pecunia faciendum coeravit idemque dedicavit
EDCS-06000320.translation: To Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the Deified, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 24th time, consul for the 13th time, father of the country: Annobal Rufus, an adornment of his country, lover of concord, flamen, sufes, prefect of sacred rites, son of Himilcho Tapapi, took care to have this made from his own money and also dedicated it.
EDCS-06000320.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a public work by Annobal Rufus, a local official, funded by him and dedicated to Emperor Augustus, dated to 1 CE based on Augustus's imperial titles.

EDCS-06000321.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Divi filio Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XXIV consule XIII patre patriae Annobal ornator patriae amator concordiae flamen sufes praefectus sacrorum Himilchonis Tapapi filius Rufus de sua pecunia faciendum coeravit idemque dedicavit
EDCS-06000321.translation: To Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the Deified, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 24th time, consul for the 13th time, father of the country: Annobal Rufus, an adornment of his country, lover of concord, flamen, sufes, prefect of sacred rites, son of Himilcho Tapapi, took care to have this made from his own money and also dedicated it.
EDCS-06000321.summary: This inscription, similar to the preceding ones, details a public work commissioned and dedicated by Annobal Rufus in honor of Emperor Augustus around 1 CE.

EDCS-06000322.text_edition: Numini Imperatoris Caesaris divi fili Augusti pontificis maximi imperatoris XX consulis XIII tribunicia potestate XXXIIII calchidicum sic et porticus et porta et via ab XVviris sacrorum dedicata est  Iddibal Himilis filius Caphada Aemilius de sua pecunia faciendum curavit calchidicum sic et porticus et portam et viam  Iddibal Himilis filius Caphada Aemilius de sua pecunia faciendum curavit calchidicum sic et porticus et porta sic et via sic
EDCS-06000322.translation: To the Divinity of Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the Deified, pontifex maximus, imperator for the 20th time, consul for the 13th time, holding tribunician power for the 34th time: the Chalcidicum, thus also the porticoes, and the gate, and the road were dedicated by the Quindecimviri Sacris Faciundis. Iddibal Caphada Aemilius, son of Himilis, took care to have the Chalcidicum, thus also the porticoes, and the gate, and the road made from his own money. Iddibal Caphada Aemilius, son of Himilis, took care to have the Chalcidicum, thus also the porticoes, and the gate, and the road made from his own money.
EDCS-06000322.summary: This inscription commemorates the dedication of a chalcidicum, porticoes, a gate, and a road by the Quindecimviri Sacris Faciundis to the divinity of Emperor Augustus (dated to 13 CE by his 34th tribunician power), with Iddibal Caphada Aemilius funding the construction from his private resources.

EDCS-06000328.text_edition: Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Augusto Divi Iuli nepoti pontifici maximo consuli V imperatori VIII tribunicia potestate XXXVII Caius Rubellius Blandus quaestor divi Augusti tribunus plebis praetor consul proconsul pontifex patronus ex reditibus agrorum quos Lepcitanis restituit vias omnis civitatis Lepcitanae sternendas silice curavit Marcus Etrilius Lupercus legatus pro praetore patronus sub hasta faciendum locavit  Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Augusto Divi Iuli nepoti pontifici maximo consuli V imperatori VIII tribunicia potestate XXXVII Caius Rubellius Blandus quaestor divi Augusti tribunus plebis praetor consul proconsul pontifex patronus ex reditibus agrorum quos Lepcitanis restituit vias omnis civitatis Lepcitanae sternendas silice curavit Marcus Etrilius Lupercus legatus pro praetore patronus sub hasta faciendum locavit
EDCS-06000328.translation: To Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the Deified Augustus, grandson of the Deified Julius, pontifex maximus, consul for the 5th time, imperator for the 8th time, holding tribunician power for the 37th time: Gaius Rubellius Blandus, quaestor of the Deified Augustus, tribune of the plebs, praetor, consul, proconsul, pontifex, patron, had all the streets of the city of Leptis paved with flint from the revenues of the lands he restored to the Lepcitani. Marcus Etrilius Lupercus, legate pro praetore, patron, contracted for the work to be done by public auction. To Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the Deified Augustus, grandson of the Deified Julius, pontifex maximus, consul for the 5th time, imperator for the 8th time, holding tribunician power for the 37th time: Gaius Rubellius Blandus, quaestor of the Deified Augustus, tribune of the plebs, praetor, consul, proconsul, pontifex, patron, had all the streets of the city of Leptis paved with flint from the revenues of the lands he restored to the Lepcitani. Marcus Etrilius Lupercus, legate pro praetore, patron, contracted for the work to be done by public auction.
EDCS-06000328.summary: This inscription records the paving of all streets in Leptis Magna, funded by Gaius Rubellius Blandus from revenues of restored lands and contracted by Marcus Etrilius Lupercus, dedicated to Emperor Tiberius during his 37th tribunician power (36 CE).

EDCS-06000329.text_edition: Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Augusto divi Iuli nepoti pontifici maximo consuli V imperatori VIII tribunicia potestate XXXVII Caius Rubellius Blandus quaestor divi Augusti tribunus plebis praetor consul proconsul pontifex patronus ex reditibus agrorum quos Lepcitanis restituit vias omnis civitatis Lepcitanae sternendas silice curavit Marcus Etrilius Lupercus legatus pro praetore patronus sub hasta faciendum locavit
EDCS-06000329.translation: To Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the Deified Augustus, grandson of the Deified Julius, pontifex maximus, consul for the 5th time, imperator for the 8th time, holding tribunician power for the 37th time: Gaius Rubellius Blandus, quaestor of the Deified Augustus, tribune of the plebs, praetor, consul, proconsul, pontifex, patron, had all the streets of the city of Leptis paved with flint from the revenues of the lands he restored to the Lepcitani. Marcus Etrilius Lupercus, legate pro praetore, patron, contracted for the work to be done by public auction.
EDCS-06000329.summary: This inscription documents the refurbishment of all roads in Leptis Magna, financed by Gaius Rubellius Blandus and overseen by Marcus Etrilius Lupercus, in honor of Emperor Tiberius, likely in 36 CE.

EDCS-06000331.text_edition: Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Augusto
EDCS-06000331.translation: To Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the Deified Augustus.
EDCS-06000331.summary: This is a simple dedicatory inscription to Emperor Tiberius, son of the deified Augustus, providing no further details about the monument or dedicant.

EDCS-06000333.text_edition: Druso Caesari Imperatoris Tiberi Caesaris Augusti filio divi Augusti nepoti Perperna Lepcitanus
EDCS-06000333.translation: To Drusus Caesar, son of Emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus, grandson of the Deified Augustus, by Perperna Lepcitanus.
EDCS-06000333.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, by an individual named Perperna Lepcitanus, likely from Leptis Magna.

EDCS-06000335.text_edition: Tiberio Claudio Caesari Augusto Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate V imperatori XI patri patriae consuli designato IIII
EDCS-06000335.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator for the 11th time, father of the country, designated consul for the 4th time.
EDCS-06000335.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Emperor Claudius, detailing his full titulature and dating to around 45 CE (5th tribunician power).

EDCS-06000336.text_edition: Tiberio Claudio Drusi filio Caesari Augusto Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XIII imperatori XXVII consuli V censori patri patriae Marcus Pompeius Silvanus consul XVvir sacris faciundis proconsul patronus dedicavit Quinto Cassio Grato praetore proconsule Cretae et Cyrenarum legato pro praetore Africae Caius Annonis filius nomine Cai Annonis filii nepotis sui columnas cum superficie et forum de sua pecunia dedit Balitho Annonis Macri filius Commodus testamento adoptatus faciendum curavit
EDCS-06000336.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of Drusus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 13th time, imperator for the 27th time, consul for the 5th time, censor, father of the country: Marcus Pompeius Silvanus, consul, Quindecimvir Sacris Faciundis, proconsul, patron, dedicated this. With Quintus Cassius Gratus as praetor, proconsul of Crete and Cyrene, legate pro praetore of Africa: Gaius Annonis, son of Annon, in the name of Gaius Annonis, his grandson, gave the columns with their superstructure and the forum from his own money. Balitho Commodus, son of Annon Macer, adopted by testament, took care to have it made.
EDCS-06000336.summary: This inscription records the dedication of columns and a forum, funded by Gaius Annonis for his grandson and overseen by Balitho Commodus, to Emperor Claudius (dated to 53 CE by his 13th tribunician power and 5th consulship), with Marcus Pompeius Silvanus acting as dedicant and Quintus Cassius Gratus as provincial governor.

EDCS-06000342.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare divi Vespasiani filio Domitiano Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate II imperatore III patre patriae consule VIIII Lucius Nonius Luci filius Pomptina Asprenas Luci Noni Asprenatis VIIviri epulonum proconsulis provinciae Africae III nepos triumvir aere argento auro flando feriundo sevir Salius Palatinus quaestor Caesaris Augusti centurio equitum Romanorum hastis honoratus octo vexillis IIII coronis muralibus duabus vallaribus duabus aurea una praetor inter cives et peregrinos legatus pro praetore provinciae Galateae Paphlagoniae Pamphyliae Pisidiae consul VIIvir epulonum proconsul provinciae Africae patronus municipii dedicavit legato pro praetore Marco Cornelio Firmo
EDCS-06000342.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus, son of the divine Vespasian, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, acclaimed Imperator for the third time, Father of the Country, consul for the ninth time. Lucius Nonius, son of Lucius, of the Pomptine tribe, Asprenas, grandson of Lucius Nonius Asprenas, Septemvir epulonum, three times proconsul of the province of Africa, triumvir for coining bronze, silver, and gold, sevir, Salian Palatine priest, quaestor of Caesar Augustus, centurion of Roman knights, honored with eight spears, four vexilla, two mural crowns, two vallar crowns, one golden crown, praetor between citizens and foreigners, propraetorian legate of the province of Galatia, Paphlagonia, Pamphylia, Pisidia, consul, Septemvir epulonum, proconsul of the province of Africa, patron of the municipality, dedicated this to the propraetorian legate Marcus Cornelius Firmus.
EDCS-06000342.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Nonius Asprenas to the Emperor Domitian, detailing Nonius's extensive public career and honors, likely dating to the early years of Domitian's reign (around 83 CE).

EDCS-06000343.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare divi Vespasiani filio Domitiano Augusto Germanico pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XI imperatore XXI consule XVI censore perpetuo patre patriae Tiberius Claudius Quirina Sestius Tiberi Claudi Sesti filius praefectus sacrorum flamen divi Vespasiani sufes flamen perpetuus amator patriae amator civium ornator patriae amator concordiae cui primo ordo et populus ob merita maiorum eius et ipsius lato clavo semper uti concessit podium et aram de sua pecunia facienda curavit
EDCS-06000343.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus Germanicus, son of the divine Vespasian, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the eleventh time, acclaimed Imperator for the twenty-first time, consul for the sixteenth time, perpetual censor, Father of the Country. Tiberius Claudius Sestius, of the Quirina tribe, son of Tiberius Claudius Sestius, prefect of sacred rites, flamen of the divine Vespasian, sufes, perpetual flamen, lover of his country, lover of citizens, adornment of his country, lover of concord, to whom the order and the people first granted the right to always wear the broad stripe on account of the merits of his ancestors and himself, had a podium and altar built at his own expense.
EDCS-06000343.summary: This inscription commemorates Tiberius Claudius Sestius, who, due to his and his ancestors' merits, funded the construction of a podium and an altar, dedicated to Emperor Domitian Germanicus, likely dating to 91/92 CE.

EDCS-06000345.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a larger dedication, identifying Domitian as the son of Vespasian.

EDCS-06000350.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII consul III aquae aeternitati consuluit Quintus Servilius Candidus pecunia sua perduxit
EDCS-06000350.translation: The Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the third time, consulted for the eternity of the water. Quintus Servilius Candidus brought it through with his own money.
EDCS-06000350.summary: This inscription records the construction or restoration of an aqueduct, undertaken by Quintus Servilius Candidus at his own expense, for the benefit of Emperor Hadrian, dating to around 120 CE.

EDCS-06000354.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVII consuli III patri patriae Lepcitani publice per omnia conseruatori suo
EDCS-06000354.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventeenth time, consul for the third time, Father of the Country. The people of Lepcis (publicly) to their preserver in all things.
EDCS-06000354.summary: This inscription is a public dedication from the citizens of Lepcis to Emperor Hadrian, acknowledging him as their preserver, dating to around 133 CE.

EDCS-06000356.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely mentioning the emperor Trajan and the people of Lepcis.

EDCS-06000358.translation: To Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of Augustus.
EDCS-06000358.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Aelius Caesar, adoptive son and heir-designate of Emperor Hadrian, likely dating to 136-138 CE.

EDCS-06000360.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XVI imperatore II consule IIII patre patriae templum Augusti Calpurnia Honesta sua pecunia fecit
EDCS-06000360.translation: With Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrian Antoninus Augustus Pius, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the sixteenth time, acclaimed Imperator for the second time, consul for the fourth time, Father of the Country, Calpurnia Honesta built the Temple of Augustus with her own money.
EDCS-06000360.summary: This inscription records Calpurnia Honesta funding the construction of a Temple of Augustus during the reign of Emperor Antoninus Pius, dating to 153 CE.

EDCS-06000366.text_edition: Imperatori Antonino Augusto Pio Quintus Pompeius Quirina Saturninus statuam quam pro honore aedilitatis promisit posuit
EDCS-06000366.translation: To Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius. Quintus Pompeius Saturninus, of the Quirina tribe, erected the statue which he promised for the honor of the aedileship.
EDCS-06000366.summary: This inscription records Quintus Pompeius Saturninus fulfilling a promise to erect a statue to Emperor Antoninus Pius, made in return for holding the office of aedile, dating to between 138-161 CE.

EDCS-06000368.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadr[iano Antonino Augusto Pio &
EDCS-06000368.translation: To Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadr[ian Antoninus Augustus Pius &
EDCS-06000368.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely part of a larger honorific monument.

EDCS-06000370.translation: To the Divine Faustina, the people of Lepcis (set this up) publicly.
EDCS-06000370.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the citizens of Lepcis to the deified empress Faustina, likely Faustina the Elder, honoring her memory after her death.

EDCS-06000371.text_edition: Lucio Aelio Caesaris Antonini Augusti filio consuli II Lepticani publice
EDCS-06000371.translation: To Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of Antoninus Augustus, consul for the second time, the people of Leptis (set this up) publicly.
EDCS-06000371.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the citizens of Leptis to Lucius Aelius Caesar, the adopted son and intended heir of Emperor Hadrian, who was also consul for the second time, dating to around 137 AD.

EDCS-06000373.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a larger dedication to an emperor, possibly Antoninus Pius or another ruler bearing the cognomen Pius.

EDCS-06000377.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto patri patriae tribunicia potestate V consuli II imperatori VIII conservatori orbis ob publicam et in se privatam pietatem Lepcitani publice
EDCS-06000377.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Father of the Fatherland, holding tribunician power for the fifth time, consul for the second time, acclaimed imperator for the eighth time, conservator of the world, for his public and private devotion to them, the people of Lepcis (set this up) publicly.
EDCS-06000377.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the citizens of Lepcis to Emperor Septimius Severus, commemorating his fifth year of tribunician power and expressing gratitude for his benevolence, dating to around 197 AD.

EDCS-06000378.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VI imperatori XI consuli II patri patriae Marcus Ulpius Cerealis procurator Auggustorum devotissimus numini eius posuit
EDCS-06000378.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, greatest pontiff, holding tribunician power for the sixth time, acclaimed imperator for the eleventh time, consul for the second time, Father of the Fatherland, Marcus Ulpius Cerealis, most devoted procurator of the Augusti, set this up to his divinity.
EDCS-06000378.summary: This inscription is a private dedication by Marcus Ulpius Cerealis, a procurator, to Emperor Septimius Severus, acknowledging his military victories and divine status, dating to around 198 AD.

EDCS-06000379.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filio divi Commodi fratri divi Antonini Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiani Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae adnepoti Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo tribunicia potestate VII imperatori XI consuli II patri patriae proconsuli Lepcitani publice
EDCS-06000379.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, greatest, son of the divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the divine Commodus, grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, holding tribunician power for the seventh time, acclaimed imperator for the eleventh time, consul for the second time, Father of the Fatherland, proconsul, the people of Lepcis (set this up) publicly.
EDCS-06000379.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the citizens of Lepcis to Emperor Septimius Severus, emphasizing his illustrious imperial lineage and listing his extensive titles and powers, dating to around 199 AD.

EDCS-06000380.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XIII imperatori XI consuli II patri patriae proconsuli Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus et Marcus Calpurnius Attianus filius numini eorum devotissimi posuerunt
EDCS-06000380.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, greatest pontiff, holding tribunician power for the thirteenth time, acclaimed imperator for the eleventh time, consul for the second time, Father of the Fatherland, proconsul, Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus and his son Marcus Calpurnius Attianus, most devoted to their divinity, set this up.
EDCS-06000380.summary: This inscription is a private dedication by Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus and his son to Emperor Septimius Severus, expressing their profound devotion and acknowledging his numerous imperial titles, dating to around 205 AD.

EDCS-06000382.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VIIII imperatori XI consuli II patri patriae proconsuli Marcus Iunius Punicus procurator sexagenarius provinciae Thraciae centenarius Alexandriae ad Mercurium
EDCS-06000382.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, greatest pontiff, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, acclaimed imperator for the eleventh time, consul for the second time, Father of the Fatherland, proconsul, Marcus Iunius Punicus, sexagenarian procurator of the province of Thrace, centenarian (procurator) at Alexandria ad Mercurium.
EDCS-06000382.summary: This inscription is a private dedication by Marcus Iunius Punicus, an imperial procurator, to Emperor Septimius Severus, detailing his own career and the emperor's extensive titles and powers, dating to around 201 AD.

EDCS-06000383.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate X imperatori XI patri patriae proconsuli consuli III Lepcitani Septimiani publice ob eximiam ac divinam in se indulgentiam
EDCS-06000383.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, greatest pontiff, holding tribunician power for the tenth time, acclaimed imperator for the eleventh time, Father of the Fatherland, proconsul, consul for the third time, the Septimian people of Lepcis (set this up) publicly, on account of his exceptional and divine indulgence towards them.
EDCS-06000383.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the citizens of Lepcis to Emperor Septimius Severus, thanking him for his benevolence and listing his updated imperial titles and powers, dating to around 202 AD.

EDCS-06000385.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filio divi Commodi fratri divi Antonini Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiani Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae adnepoti Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XII imperatori XI consuli III patri patriae proconsuli propagatori imperi ob caelestem in se indulgentiam eius Decimus Clodius Galba procurator regionis Thevestinae et Hipponensis et procurator rationis privatae per Flaminiam Aemiliam Liguriam
EDCS-06000385.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, greatest pontiff, son of the divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the divine Commodus, grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, holding tribunician power for the twelfth time, acclaimed imperator for the eleventh time, consul for the third time, Father of the Fatherland, proconsul, propagator of the empire, on account of his heavenly indulgence towards them, Decimus Clodius Galba, procurator of the Thevestine and Hipponensian region and procurator of the privy purse throughout Flaminia, Aemilia, and Liguria (set this up).
EDCS-06000385.summary: This inscription is a private dedication by Decimus Clodius Galba, an imperial procurator, to Emperor Septimius Severus, highlighting his illustrious ancestry, numerous imperial titles, and his role in expanding the empire, dating to around 204 AD.

EDCS-06000390.text_edition: Septimius Severus Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Publius Septimius Geta
EDCS-06000390.translation: Septimius Severus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Publius Septimius Geta.
EDCS-06000390.summary: This inscription records the names of the emperors Septimius Severus and his sons Caracalla and Geta, likely from a dedication to the imperial family.

EDCS-06000392.text_edition: Divo Severo patri Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aurelli sic Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Parthici maximi Brittanici maximi Germanici maximi pontificis maximi tribuniciae potestatis XVIII imperatori sic III consulis IIII patris patriae Lepcitani Septimiani
EDCS-06000392.translation: To the Divine Severus, father of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Parthicus, greatest Britannicus, greatest Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 18th time, acclaimed imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, a Leptitanian Septimian.
EDCS-06000392.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified emperor Septimius Severus by his son Caracalla, highlighting Caracalla's titles and the connection to Leptis Magna (Leptitani Septimiani), likely dating to 215 AD.

EDCS-06000393.text_edition: Divo Pio Severo Augusto Quintus Marcius Dioga praefectus annonae sacrae urbis
EDCS-06000393.translation: To the Divine Pius Severus Augustus, Quintus Marcius Dioga, prefect of the sacred grain supply of the city.
EDCS-06000393.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified emperor Septimius Severus by Quintus Marcius Dioga, who served as the prefect of the annona in Rome.

EDCS-06000394.text_edition: Iulia Augustae matri castrorum matri Augustorum coniugi invicti Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus et Marcus Calpurnius Attianus filius numini eorum devotissimi posuerunt
EDCS-06000394.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, mother of the Augusti, wife of the unconquered Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus and Marcus Calpurnius Attianus, his son, most devoted to their divinity, placed this.
EDCS-06000394.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Julia Augusta (Julia Domna) as mother of the camps, mother of the emperors, and wife of Septimius Severus, erected by Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus and his son, expressing their devotion to the imperial family around the turn of the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-06000395.text_edition: Iuliae Augustae matris sic castrorum matri Aug[gustorum] coniugi invicti Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Marcus Iunius Punicus procurator sexagenarius provinciae Thraciae centenarius Alexandriae ad Mercurium
EDCS-06000395.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, mother of the Augusti, wife of the unconquered Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, Marcus Junius Punicus, sexagenarian procurator of the province of Thrace, centenarian at Alexandria for Mercury.
EDCS-06000395.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Julia Augusta (Julia Domna) by Marcus Junius Punicus, a procurator with various administrative roles, celebrating her status as mother of the emperors and wife of Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000396.text_edition: Iuliae Augustae matri Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aurelli sic Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et castrorum senatus et patriae Lepcitani Septimiani
EDCS-06000396.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and mother of the camps, the senate, and the Leptitanian Septimian fatherland.
EDCS-06000396.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Julia Augusta (Julia Domna) by the senate and the 'Leptitanian Septimian fatherland' (Leptis Magna), honoring her as mother of Caracalla and mother of the camps, likely dating to Caracalla's sole reign (after 212 AD).

EDCS-06000397.text_edition: Iuliae Augustae matri castrorum matri Aug[gustorum] coniugi invicti Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi curia Augusta numini eius devotissima posuit
EDCS-06000397.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, mother of the Augusti, wife of the unconquered Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, the Curia Augusta, most devoted to her divinity, placed this.
EDCS-06000397.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Julia Augusta (Julia Domna) by the Curia Augusta, emphasizing her imperial titles and her role as wife of Septimius Severus and mother of the emperors, reflecting civic devotion.

EDCS-06000398.text_edition: Iuliae Augustae matri castrorum matri Auggus[torum] coniugi invicti Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi curia Iulia numini eius devotissima posuit
EDCS-06000398.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, mother of the Augusti, wife of the unconquered Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, the Curia Iulia, most devoted to her divinity, placed this.
EDCS-06000398.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Julia Augusta (Julia Domna) by the Curia Iulia, highlighting her prominent position as mother of the emperors and wife of Septimius Severus, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-06000399.text_edition: Iuliae Augustae matri Augusto[rum] et castrorum Decimus Clodius Galba procurator
EDCS-06000399.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the Augusti and of the camps, Decimus Clodius Galba, procurator.
EDCS-06000399.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Julia Augusta (Julia Domna) by Decimus Clodius Galba, a procurator, honoring her as mother of the emperors and mother of the camps.

EDCS-06000400.text_edition: Iuliae Augustae matri Auggustorum et castrorum Messius Atticus centurio cohortis VII praetoriae piae vindicis votum solvit
EDCS-06000400.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the Augusti and of the camps, Messius Atticus, centurion of the VII Praetorian Cohort, Pious and Vindicator, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-06000400.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Julia Augusta (Julia Domna) by Messius Atticus, a centurion of the Praetorian Guard, honoring her as mother of the emperors and mother of the camps.

EDCS-06000401.summary: This fragment likely records a dedication to an empress, indicated by the title 'Augusta'.

EDCS-06000402.text_edition: Pacciae Marcianae quondam uxoris sic domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Lepcitani Septimiani publice ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-06000402.translation: To Paccia Marciana, formerly the wife, thus, of our lord, Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, a Leptitanian Septimian, they publicly placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-06000402.summary: This inscription records a public dedication, made in fulfillment of a vow, to Paccia Marciana, the deceased wife of Emperor Septimius Severus, by the city of Lepcis Magna (Leptis Magna).

EDCS-06000403.text_edition: Pacciae Marcianae quondam uxoris domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi curiae tres Plotina et Nervia et Matidia posuerunt
EDCS-06000403.translation: To Paccia Marciana, formerly the wife of our lord, Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, three curiae – Plotina, Nervia, and Matidia – placed this.
EDCS-06000403.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Paccia Marciana, the former wife of Emperor Septimius Severus, made by three municipal subdivisions (curiae) named Plotina, Nervia, and Matidia.

EDCS-06000404.text_edition: Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi tribunicia potestate X imperatoris XI consulis III patris patriae proconsulis avo domini nostri Lucio Septimio Severo sufeti praefecto publice creato cum primum civitas Romana adacta est duumviro flamini perpetuo in decuriis et inter selectos Romae iudicavit Lepcitani publice
EDCS-06000404.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, holding tribunician power for the tenth time, acclaimed imperator for the eleventh time, consul for the third time, father of his country, proconsul, and to the grandfather of our lord, Lucius Septimius Severus, suffete, publicly created prefect when Roman citizenship was first introduced, duumvir, flamen perpetuus, who judged in the decuriae and among the selected men in Rome, the people of Leptis Magna publicly (dedicate this).
EDCS-06000404.summary: This inscription is a public dedication from Lepcis Magna honoring Emperor Septimius Severus and commemorating his grandfather, Lucius Septimius Severus, who held various important local and Roman magistracies.

EDCS-06000405.text_edition: Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi tribunicia potestate XI imperatoris XII consulis III proconsulis Lucio Septimio Severo flamini perpetuo avo curiae duae Traiana Dacica ex voto statuerunt
EDCS-06000405.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, holding tribunician power for the eleventh time, acclaimed imperator for the twelfth time, consul for the third time, proconsul, and to Lucius Septimius Severus, flamen perpetuus, grandfather, two curiae – Traiana and Dacica – erected this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-06000405.summary: This inscription records a dedication, made in fulfillment of a vow by two municipal subdivisions (curiae) named Traiana and Dacica, to Emperor Septimius Severus and his grandfather, Lucius Septimius Severus, who served as a perpetual flamen.

EDCS-06000406.text_edition: Publio Septimio Getae patri domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate VIIII imperatoris XI consulis II patris patriae proconsulis curia Nervia ex voto statuit
EDCS-06000406.translation: To Publius Septimius Geta, father of our lord, Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, acclaimed imperator for the eleventh time, consul for the second time, father of his country, proconsul, the curia Nervia erected this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-06000406.summary: This inscription records a dedication, made in fulfillment of a vow by the curia Nervia, to Publius Septimius Geta, the father of Emperor Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000407.text_edition: Fulviae Piae matris sic domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Lepcitani Septimiani publice ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-06000407.translation: To Fulvia Pia, the mother, thus, of our lord, Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, a Leptitanian Septimian, they publicly placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-06000407.summary: This inscription records a public dedication, made in fulfillment of a vow, to Fulvia Pia, the mother of Emperor Septimius Severus, by the city of Lepcis Magna (Leptis Magna).

EDCS-06000408.text_edition: Fulviae Piae matri domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi curiae tres Severae Pia et Ulpia et Augusta ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-06000408.translation: To Fulvia Pia, the mother of our lord, Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, three curiae – Severa, Pia, and Ulpia, and Augusta – placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-06000408.summary: This inscription records a dedication, made in fulfillment of a vow by four municipal subdivisions (curiae) named Severa, Pia, Ulpia, and Augusta, to Fulvia Pia, the mother of Emperor Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000409.text_edition: Septimiae Octavillae sorori Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi clarissimae memoriae feminae curiae tres Iulia et Germanica et Marciana posuerunt
EDCS-06000409.translation: To Septimia Octavilla, sister of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, a woman of most distinguished memory, three curiae – Iulia, Germanica, and Marciana – placed this.
EDCS-06000409.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Septimia Octavilla, the sister of Emperor Septimius Severus, made by three municipal subdivisions (curiae) named Iulia, Germanica, and Marciana.

EDCS-06000410.text_edition: Marco Aurellio sic Antonino Caesari imperatori destinato Imperatoris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici filio Lepcitani publice
EDCS-06000410.translation: To Marcus Aurelius, thus, Antoninus Caesar, designated imperator, son of Imperator Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus, the people of Leptis Magna publicly (dedicate this).
EDCS-06000410.summary: This inscription is a public dedication from Lepcis Magna honoring Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla) as designated imperator and son of Emperor Septimius Severus, likely dating to the early 200s AD.

EDCS-06000411.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Augusto tribunicia potestate III Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus et Marcus Calpurnius Attianus filius numini eorum devotissimi posuerunt
EDCS-06000411.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, with tribunician power for the third time, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country; Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus and Marcus Calpurnius Attianus, his son, most devoted to their divine will, erected this.
EDCS-06000411.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus and his son to the emperor Caracalla, likely dating to around 200 CE based on his tribunician power.

EDCS-06000412.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio tribuniciae potestatis IIII proconsuli Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio curia Pia Severiana ex voto statuit
EDCS-06000412.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, with tribunician power for the fourth time, proconsul, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country; the Pia Severiana curia erected this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-06000412.summary: This inscription commemorates the dedication of a statue by the Pia Severiana curia to Emperor Caracalla, fulfilling a vow, likely around 201 CE based on his tribunician power.

EDCS-06000413.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio tribuniciae potestatis IIII procons(uli) Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio curia Ulpia ex voto statuit
EDCS-06000413.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, with tribunician power for the fourth time, proconsul, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country; the Ulpia curia erected this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-06000413.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue by the Ulpia curia to Emperor Caracalla, fulfilling a vow, likely around 201 CE based on his tribunician power.

EDCS-06000414.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio tribuniciae potestatis IIII proconsuli Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio Marcus Iunius Punicus procurator sexagenarius provinciae Thraciae centenarius Alexandriae ad Mercurium
EDCS-06000414.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, with tribunician power for the fourth time, proconsul, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country; Marcus Iunius Punicus, procurator sexagenarius of the province of Thrace, centenarius at Alexandria ad Mercurium, (dedicated this).
EDCS-06000414.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Iunius Punicus, an imperial administrator, to Emperor Caracalla, likely dating to around 201 CE based on his tribunician power.

EDCS-06000416.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Severi Augusti filio divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nep(oti) divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti diui Traiani Parthici et divi Neruae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici tribunicia potestate VII consuli designato II proconsuli propagatori imperi ob caelestem in se indulgentiam eius Decimus Clodius Galba procurator regionis Thevestinae et Hipponensis et procurator rationis privatae per Flaminiam Aemiliam Liguriam
EDCS-06000416.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix, son of Severus Augustus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, with tribunician power for the seventh time, consul designate for the second time, proconsul, propagator of the empire, on account of his heavenly indulgence towards him; Decimus Clodius Galba, procurator of the region of Thevestina and Hipponensis, and procurator of the private treasury through Flaminia, Aemilia, and Liguria, (erected this).
EDCS-06000416.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Decimus Clodius Galba, an imperial procurator, to Emperor Caracalla, celebrating his lineage and imperial achievements, likely dating to around 204 CE based on his tribunician power.

EDCS-06000418.text_edition: Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio   Clementis viri egregi filio a militiis civi patrono ordo splendidissimus Lepcimagnensium
EDCS-06000418.translation: To the son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country, ... Clement, a man of outstanding rank, son of military service, citizen, patron; the most splendid order of the Lepcitani Magni (dedicated this).
EDCS-06000418.summary: This inscription honors an unnamed son of Emperor Septimius Severus, likely Caracalla, by the splendid order of Lepcis Magna, recognizing him as a patron and citizen.

EDCS-06000424.text_edition: Publio Septimio Getae Caesari Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio Marcus Iunius Punicus procurator sexagenarius provinciae Thraciae centenarius Alexandriae ad Mercurium
EDCS-06000424.translation: To Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country; Marcus Iunius Punicus, procurator sexagenarius of the province of Thrace, centenarius at Alexandria ad Mercurium, (dedicated this).
EDCS-06000424.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Iunius Punicus, an imperial administrator, to Publius Septimius Geta Caesar.

EDCS-06000425.text_edition: Publio Septimio Getae Caesari Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio Lepcitani Septimiani publice ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-06000425.translation: To Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country; the Septimian Lepcitani publicly erected this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-06000425.summary: This inscription records the public dedication of a monument by the Septimian citizens of Lepcis to Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-06000426.text_edition: Publio Septimio Getae Caesari Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio curia Matidia ex voto statuit
EDCS-06000426.translation: To Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country; the Matidia curia erected this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-06000426.summary: This inscription commemorates the dedication of a statue by the Matidia curia to Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-06000428.text_edition: Lucio Septimio Getae Caesari Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filio et Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aurelli sic Antonini Augusti fratri Messius Atticus primus sacerdos votum solvit
EDCS-06000428.translation: To Lucius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, and brother of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus; Messius Atticus, the chief priest, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-06000428.summary: This inscription records Messius Atticus, the chief priest, fulfilling a vow to Lucius Septimius Geta Caesar, recognizing his relation to both Emperor Septimius Severus and Emperor Caracalla.

EDCS-06000429.text_edition: Publio Septimio [Getae] Caesari Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi fortissimi felicissimi patris patriae filio et Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aurelli sic Antonino sic Augusti tribunicia potestate proconsulis fratri Messius Atticus centurio cohortis X urbanae votum solvit
EDCS-06000429.translation: To Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, greatest Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus, most brave, most fortunate, father of his country, and brother of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, holding tribunician power and proconsul; Messius Atticus, centurion of the X urban cohort, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-06000429.summary: This inscription records Messius Atticus, a centurion, dedicating a monument to Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Septimius Severus and brother of Caracalla, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000434.text_edition: Publio Septimio Getae Augusto Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae [filio] Quintus Pompeius Cerealis Felix procurator Augggustorum devotissimus numini eius posuit
EDCS-06000434.translation: To Publius Septimius Geta Augustus, son of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, greatest Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus, most fortunate, father of his country; Quintus Pompeius Cerealis Felix, most devoted procurator of the Augusti, placed this in honor of his divinity.
EDCS-06000434.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Quintus Pompeius Cerealis Felix, a procurator, to Publius Septimius Geta Augustus, son of Septimius Severus, honoring his divinity, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000443.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Iulio Vero Maximino Pio Felici Augusto patri patriae tribuniciae potestatis II consuli proconsuli conservatori orbis Lepcitani Septimiani publice
EDCS-06000443.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, father of his country, holding tribunician power for the second time, consul, proconsul, preserver of the Septimian Leptis, by public decree.
EDCS-06000443.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by the city of Leptis to Emperor Maximinus Thrax, celebrating him as a preserver, dating to his reign (235-238 CE).

EDCS-06000445.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate I  Lepcitani publice  Domino nostro invicto ac nobilissimo Caesari Flavio Valerio Severo Lepcitani devoti numini maiestatique eius publice
EDCS-06000445.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the first time, by public decree of the people of Leptis. To our unconquered and most noble Caesar Flavius Valerius Severus, the devoted people of Leptis, by public decree, in honor of his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-06000445.summary: This inscription records two public dedications from the people of Leptis, one to Emperor Gordian III and another, later, to Caesar Flavius Valerius Severus, honoring their power and majesty.

EDCS-06000447.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Gallieno Germanico Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo Germanico maximo tribunicia potestate XIII imperatori XII consuli VI patri patriae proconsuli et Corneliae Saloninae sanctissimae Augustae nostrae coniugi Gallieni Augusti nostri Lepcitani Septimiani Saloniniani publice
EDCS-06000447.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Germanicus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, greatest Germanicus, holding tribunician power for the thirteenth time, imperator for the twelfth time, consul for the sixth time, father of his country, proconsul; and to Cornelia Salonina, our most holy Augusta, wife of our Augustus Gallienus; by public decree of the Septimian Saloninian Leptis.
EDCS-06000447.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by the city of Leptis to Emperor Gallienus and his wife, Augusta Cornelia Salonina, likely from the latter part of Gallienus's reign (around 265 CE).

EDCS-06000451.text_edition: Publio Cornelio Licinio Valeriano nobilissimo Caesari Lepcitani Septimiani publice
EDCS-06000451.translation: To Publius Cornelius Licinius Valerianus, most noble Caesar, by public decree of the Septimian Leptis.
EDCS-06000451.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by the city of Leptis to Publius Cornelius Licinius Valerianus, who was designated Caesar, likely dating to the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000452.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to a Publius Co[rnelio..., likely part of a public dedication to an imperial figure or senator from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000453.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Caro Pio Felici invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli II patri patriae proconsuli Lucius Iulius Paulinus vir clarissimus proconsul
EDCS-06000453.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus Pius Felix, unconquered Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time, father of his country, proconsul; Lucius Iulius Paulinus, most distinguished man, proconsul, (erected this).
EDCS-06000453.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Proconsul Lucius Iulius Paulinus to Emperor Carus, acknowledging his titles and virtues, dating to Carus's reign (282-283 CE).

EDCS-06000454.text_edition: Fortissimis et invictissimis imperatoribus ddominis nnostris Marco Flavio Valerio Constantio Pio Felici invicto Augusto et Caio Galerio Valerio Maximiano Pio Felici invicto Augusto Lepcitani devoti numini maiestatique eorum publice
EDCS-06000454.translation: To the bravest and most unconquered emperors, our lords Marcus Flavius Valerius Constantius Pius Felix, unconquered Augustus, and Gaius Galerius Valerius Maximianus Pius Felix, unconquered Augustus; the people of Leptis, devoted to their divinity and majesty, by public decree.
EDCS-06000454.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by the people of Leptis to the co-emperors Constantius Chlorus and Galerius, honoring their might and majesty, likely from the early 4th century CE during the Tetrarchy.

EDCS-06000456.text_edition: Indulgentissimo ac libertatis restitutori victoriosissimoque imperatori domino nostro Maxentio Pio Felici invicto Augusto Valerius Alexander vir perfectissimus agens vices praefecti praetorio numini maiestatique eius dicatissimus
EDCS-06000456.translation: To the most indulgent and victorious emperor, restorer of liberty, our lord Maxentius Pius Felix, unconquered Augustus; Valerius Alexander, most perfect man, acting as praetorian prefect, most dedicated to his divinity and majesty, (erected this).
EDCS-06000456.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Valerius Alexander, acting praetorian prefect, to Emperor Maxentius, praising him as a benevolent restorer of liberty, dating to Maxentius's reign (306-312 CE).

EDCS-06000457.text_edition: Indulgentissimo ac libertatis restitutori victorioriosissimoque Imperatori domino nostro Maxentio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto Volusius Donatianus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Tripolitanae numini maiestatique eius dicatissimus
EDCS-06000457.translation: To the most indulgent and restorer of liberty, and most victorious Emperor, our lord Maxentius, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible, Augustus, Volusius Donatianus, a most perfect man, governor of the province of Tripolitana, most devoted to his divine power and majesty.
EDCS-06000457.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Maxentius by Volusius Donatianus, the governor of Tripolitana, praising him as a restorer of liberty and a victor, likely dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-06000459.text_edition: Cum basilica vetus ex maxima parte ruina esset deformata conlabsu sic ac spatio sui breviasset aream forensem   divino icta conflagrarat incendio adque is locus saeculo fortunatissimo meliora deposceret tantae stragis labe sublata tripertita porticus magnitudine sui ac Troadensium columnarum adornata operis provincialium ac sumptu publico disponente Laenatio Romulo viro perfectissimo rectore provinciae intra anni spatium perfecta ac dedicata est adque ad sempiternam memoriam statuam marmoream suo numine radiantem domino nostro Constantino maximo victori semper Augusto idem vir perfectissimus dicata mente constituit curante Claudio Aurelio Generoso viro egregio curatore rei publicae et splendidissimo ordine coloniae Lepcimagnensium
EDCS-06000459.translation: Since the old basilica was largely disfigured by ruinous collapse, and thus had narrowed the forum area, having been struck and consumed by divine fire, and since that place in this most fortunate age demanded better, with such a great ruin removed, a tripartite portico, adorned by its magnitude and with columns of Troas, was completed and dedicated within the space of a year by the work of provincials and public expense, under the direction of Laenatius Romulus, a most perfect man, governor of the province. And for eternal memory, the same most perfect man, with a dedicated mind, erected a marble statue radiating with his divine power to our lord Constantine, the greatest, always victorious Augustus, with the care of Claudius Aurelius Generosus, an excellent man, curator of public affairs, and the most splendid order of the colony of Lepcis Magna.
EDCS-06000459.summary: This inscription records the reconstruction of a basilica and the dedication of a statue to Emperor Constantine in Lepcis Magna, following a fire and collapse, undertaken by provincial workers and public funds under the direction of Governor Laenatius Romulus and curator Claudius Aurelius Generosus, dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-06000485.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely commemorating or dedicating something to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, probably dating to the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-06000486.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning an Emperor Caesar Antoninus, likely referring to one of the Antonine emperors of the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-06000490.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly referring to the divine Marcus (likely Marcus Aurelius) as emperor, with other illegible text, dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-06000494.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription possibly referring to a divine Nerva, likely Emperor Nerva, dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-06000502.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning 'Augustus our,' likely part of a dedication to an emperor, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-06000503.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription using imperial epithets 'Pious, Fortunate, greatest,' suggesting a dedication to an emperor, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-06000505.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly referring to a son of an Augustus, likely a member of the imperial family, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-06000506.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription using epithets 'Pious, Fortunate' for 'our lord,' likely part of a dedication to an emperor, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-06000507.text_edition: VS  Liberatori orbis ac    RLepcitani devoti numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-06000507.translation: To the Liberator of the world, and the citizens of Leptis Magna devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-06000507.summary: This inscription is a dedication to an emperor, likely for his role as 'liberator of the world,' set up by the citizens of Leptis Magna, probably in the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-06000509.text_edition: Marcus Annius Messalla adlectus in senatum a divo a divo Vespasiano legatus Africae
EDCS-06000509.translation: Marcus Annius Messalla, adlected into the Senate by the deified Vespasian, legate of Africa.
EDCS-06000509.summary: This is a record of the career of Marcus Annius Messalla, highlighting his senatorial adlection by Emperor Vespasian and his governorship of the province of Africa, likely dating to the Flavian period.

EDCS-06000510.text_edition: Marcus Annius Messalla adlectus in senatum a divo a divo Vespasiano legatus Africae
EDCS-06000510.translation: Marcus Annius Messalla, adlected into the Senate by the deified Vespasian, legate of Africa.
EDCS-06000510.summary: This is a record of the career of Marcus Annius Messalla, highlighting his senatorial adlection by Emperor Vespasian and his governorship of the province of Africa, likely dating to the Flavian period.

EDCS-06000511.text_edition: Marco Atilio Metilio Braduae Caucidio Tertullo Claudio Attico Vibullio Pollioni Gavidio Latiari Atrio Basso proconsuli Decimus Iunius Crescens Decimus Iunius Galba Quintus Calpurnius Capito Lucius Plautius Octavianus patrono
EDCS-06000511.translation: To Marcus Atilius Metilius Bradua Caucidius Tertullus Claudius Atticus Vibullius Pollio Gavidius Latiarius Atrius Bassus, proconsul. Decimus Iunius Crescens, Decimus Iunius Galba, Quintus Calpurnius Capito, Lucius Plautius Octavianus, to their patron.
EDCS-06000511.summary: This inscription is a dedication by four individuals, Decimus Iunius Crescens, Decimus Iunius Galba, Quintus Calpurnius Capito, and Lucius Plautius Octavianus, to Marcus Atilius Metilius Bradua Caucidius Tertullus Claudius Atticus Vibullius Pollio Gavidius Latiarius Atrius Bassus, honoring him as their patron and former proconsul, likely dating to the early-to-mid 2nd century AD.

EDCS-06000513.text_edition: Elpidii  Omnium virtutum praedicabili viro et supra documenta bonitatis insigni adque magnifico Caecilio Severo viro clarissimo agenti vices praeffectorum praetorio ob multiformem iudiciorum eius in se moderationem ex decreto et sufragio M  Lepcitani publice
EDCS-06000513.translation: To Elpidius, a man commendable for all virtues and exceptionally distinguished and magnificent beyond all evidence of goodness, and to Caecilius Severus, a most distinguished man acting as vice-prefect of the praetorium, on account of the many forms of his moderation in his judgments concerning them, by decree and vote of the people of Leptis Magna, publicly.
EDCS-06000513.summary: This is a public dedication by the community of Leptis Magna, honoring Caecilius Severus, a clarissimus vir acting as vice-prefect of the praetorium, for his virtuous and moderate judicial conduct, likely dating to the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-06000514.text_edition: Cnaeus Calpurnius Cnaei filius Piso consul pontufex sic proconsul
EDCS-06000514.translation: Gnaeus Calpurnius Piso, son of Gnaeus, consul, pontiff, proconsul.
EDCS-06000514.summary: This inscription records the public offices held by Gnaeus Calpurnius Piso, including consul, pontiff, and proconsul, likely dating to the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-06000515.text_edition: Lucius Caninius Luci filius Gallus XVvir sacris faciundis consul proconsul patronus dedicavit
EDCS-06000515.translation: Lucius Caninius Gallus, son of Lucius, quindecimvir for performing sacred rites, consul, proconsul, patron, dedicated this.
EDCS-06000515.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Caninius Gallus, detailing his distinguished public career as a quindecimvir, consul, proconsul, and patron, likely dating to the Augustan period.

EDCS-06000516.text_edition: Claudio A  proconsuli provinciae Africae omnium virtutum viro innocentis integritatis vicoratae lenitatis sublimis moderationis laudavilis sic iustitiae Lepcimagnenses ex decreto ordinis patrono perpetuo
EDCS-06000516.translation: To Claudius A[---], proconsul of the province of Africa, a man of all virtues, of innocent integrity, of restrained clemency, of sublime moderation, of praiseworthy justice, the citizens of Leptis Magna by decree of the ordo to their perpetual patron.
EDCS-06000516.summary: This is a public dedication by the citizens of Leptis Magna, honoring Claudius A[---] as their perpetual patron and former proconsul of Africa, commending his virtues and justice, likely dating to the Antonine or Severan period.

EDCS-06000518.text_edition: Caio Fulvio Plautiano praefecto praet[orio] Marcus Cornelius Bassus Servianus egregiae memoriae vir fieri iussit Cornelia Servianilla clarissima femina filia et heres posuit
EDCS-06000518.translation: For Gaius Fulvius Plautianus, praetorian prefect. Marcus Cornelius Bassus Servianus, a man of outstanding memory, ordered this to be made. Cornelia Servianilla, a most distinguished woman, his daughter and heir, erected this.
EDCS-06000518.summary: This is a funerary monument or honorary dedication erected by Cornelia Servianilla, daughter and heir of Marcus Cornelius Bassus Servianus, in honor of Gaius Fulvius Plautianus, the powerful praetorian prefect, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-06000524.text_edition: Dimittendus in splendidissimam coloniam Leptim Magnam iussu Fulvi Plautiani clarissimi viri praefecti praetorio ac necessari dominorum nostrorum
EDCS-06000524.translation: To be dismissed into the most splendid colony of Leptis Magna by order of Fulvius Plautianus, most distinguished man, praetorian prefect, and close associate of our masters.
EDCS-06000524.summary: This inscription records the dismissal of an individual to the colony of Leptis Magna, a command issued by Fulvius Plautianus, the praetorian prefect, emphasizing his high status and connection to the emperors, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-06000526.text_edition: Gaio Gavio Macro legato pro praetore III Lepcitani publice
EDCS-06000526.translation: To Gaius Gavius Macer, legatus pro praetore III. The people of Lepcis publicly honor him.
EDCS-06000526.summary: This inscription is a public dedication from Lepcis to Gaius Gavius Macer, likely a provincial governor, acknowledging his service.
