EDCS-10300871.translation: To the Genius of the Trajanic waters, Marcus Ulpius Menophoon, freedman of Augustus, assistant to Licinius Maximus and Felicior, procurators of Augustus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10300871.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Marcus Ulpius Menophoon, an imperial freedman and assistant to imperial procurators, to the Genius (protective spirit) of the Trajanic aqueduct, fulfilling a vow he made.

EDCS-10301005.translation: Of our Augusti. Properties of F.S., most distinguished man.
EDCS-10301005.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely marking imperial properties or land belonging to a 'clarissimus vir' (senator or high-ranking individual) and the current emperors.

EDCS-10301035.translation: For the well-being of the Emperors Caesars Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, father of the country, and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and Publius Septimius Severus Geta Augustus, and Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the Augusti and of the camps, and of their entire divine house, the Republic of the municipality Septimius Aurelius Liber Thuggensis [dedicates this].
EDCS-10301035.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by the Republic of the municipality of Septimius Aurelius Liber Thuggensis for the well-being of the imperial family, including Emperor Septimius Severus, Caracalla, Geta, and Julia Domna, dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10900709.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aemilia Decimi filia Aprulla pia vixit annis LXXXXIII mensibus II hic sita est  Dis Manibus sacrum Nabor Felicis Balsillecis Maraxae pius vixit nnos LXXX hic situs est
EDCS-10900709.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aemilia Aprulla, daughter of Decimus, pious, lived 93 years, 2 months. She is buried here. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Nabor, son of Felix, of Balsillecis, Maraxa, pious, lived 80 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-10900709.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Aemilia Aprulla, who lived 93 years and 2 months, and Nabor, son of Felix, who lived 80 years, marking their burial sites.

EDCS-10900710.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Iulius Sanus Maximinus vixit annum menses III horas IIII caementarius hic situs
EDCS-10900710.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Publius Julius Sanus Maximinus, a mason, lived 1 year, 3 months, 4 hours. He is buried here.
EDCS-10900710.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Julius Sanus Maximinus, a mason, who died at a very young age, likely placed by his family.

EDCS-10900738.text_edition: Clodius Quartus filius ex Taeis vixit annis XXV posuit pietatis causa ex |(centurione) Caius Iulius hic situs est
EDCS-10900738.translation: Clodius Quartus, son from the Taei, lived 25 years. Gaius Julius, a centurion, placed this on account of piety. He is buried here.
EDCS-10900738.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodius Quartus, who lived 25 years, placed by Gaius Julius, a centurion, out of piety.

EDCS-11300842.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a larger dedication or monument, identifying Cornelius Bassus Surus.

EDCS-11300844.translation: Son of a most distinguished man.
EDCS-11300844.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely from a funerary monument or statue base, identifying the deceased or dedicatee as the son of a clarissimus vir, a high-ranking senator.

EDCS-11300858.translation: Of Lucius Silius Amicus, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-11300858.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely from a monument, identifying Lucius Silius Amicus as a clarissimus vir, a high-ranking senator.

EDCS-11300863.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a larger monument, identifying Marcus Iunius Crescens.

EDCS-11300865.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Geminius Orfitianus miles cohortis XI urbanae vixit annis XXXVIIII hic situs est Geminius Restutus filio pio posuit  Dis Manibus sacrum Sornia Maxima vixit annis XXXVI hic sita est Geminius Restutus matri pie posuit  Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Geminius Pusissus vixit annis LVII hic situs est Geminius Restutus patri posuit  Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Geminius Salfenius Quirina vixit annis L filii patri optimo pio posuerunt hic situs est
EDCS-11300865.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Geminius Orfitianus, a soldier of the 11th Urban Cohort, lived 39 years. He is buried here. Geminius Restutus placed this for his pious son. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sornia Maxima lived 36 years. She is buried here. Geminius Restutus placed this piously for his mother. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Geminius Pusissus lived 57 years. He is buried here. Geminius Restutus placed this for his father. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Geminius Salfenius Quirina lived 50 years. His sons placed this for their excellent, pious father. He is buried here.
EDCS-11300865.summary: This is a collection of four funerary inscriptions, primarily set up by Geminius Restutus for his son Geminius Orfitianus (a soldier), his mother Sornia Maxima, and his father Lucius Geminius Pusissus, and an additional one by sons for Quintus Geminius Salfenius Quirina.

EDCS-11300870.text_edition: Cal Fol  Dis Manibus sacrum Mevius Ingenuus vixit nnos XXXIII hic situs est Marcus Mevius Campesis veteranus legionis III Augustae piae vindicis fratri pio posuit  Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Mevius Campesis veteranus legionis III Augustae piae vindicis vixit nnos  ipse se vivo fecit
EDCS-11300870.translation: Cal. Fol. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Mevius Ingenuus lived 33 years. He is buried here. Marcus Mevius Campesis, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, pious and avenging, placed this for his pious brother. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Mevius Campesis, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, pious and avenging, lived [---] years. He made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-11300870.summary: This inscription records two separate funerary dedications: one by Marcus Mevius Campesis, a veteran of the Legio III Augusta, for his brother Mevius Ingenuus, and another by Marcus Mevius Campesis himself, prepared during his lifetime.

EDCS-11300873.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Fabia Dativa vixit annis XXXX hic sita est Marcus Mevius Campesis veteranus legionis III Augustae piae vindicis uxori piae fecit
EDCS-11300873.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Fabia Dativa lived for 40 years; she lies here. Marcus Mevius Campesis, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, Pious and Vengeful, made this for his devoted wife.
EDCS-11300873.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a veteran of Legio III Augusta, Marcus Mevius Campesis, dedicated to his deceased wife, Fabia Dativa, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-11300875.text_edition: Lucius Pacuvius Servatus veteranus legionis III Augustae vixit nnos LXXV militavit nnos hic situs est Pacuvii Felix et Claudia filii patri poserunt
EDCS-11300875.translation: Lucius Pacuvius Servatus, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, lived for 75 years and served for [---] years; he lies here. His children, Pacuvius Felix and Claudia, placed this for their father.
EDCS-11300875.summary: This funerary inscription was erected by Pacuvius Felix and Claudia for their father, Lucius Pacuvius Servatus, a veteran of Legio III Augusta who lived to be 75.

EDCS-11300876.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Allius Iucundinus vixit annos XLV hic situs est Ianuaria uxor marito amantissimo pio posuit  Dis Manibus sacrum Petilia Romana vixit annos LXV hic sita est Allius Fortunatio uxori piae posuit IVVIT  Dis Manibus sacrum Petilius Rusticus miles praetorianus |(duplicarius qui vixit annos XXVIII Allius Fortunatio pater filio p(osuit)
EDCS-11300876.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Allius Iucundinus lived for 45 years; he lies here. Ianuaria, his wife, placed this for her most loving and devoted husband. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Petilia Romana lived for 65 years; she lies here. Allius Fortunatio placed this for his devoted wife. [---]. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Petilius Rusticus, a praetorian soldier, a duplicarius, who lived for 28 years. Allius Fortunatio, his father, placed this for his son.
EDCS-11300876.summary: This monument contains three separate funerary inscriptions: one from Ianuaria to her husband Allius Iucundinus, another from Allius Fortunatio to his wife Petilia Romana, and a third from Allius Fortunatio to his son Petilius Rusticus, a praetorian soldier, suggesting a family plot or monument.

EDCS-11300880.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Octaviae Sterceiae Lucius Sempronius Liberalis signifer legionis III Augustae post annos XVI stipendiorum matri posuit
EDCS-11300880.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Sempronius Liberalis, standard-bearer of the Third Augustan Legion, placed this for his mother, Octavia Sterceia, after sixteen years of service.
EDCS-11300880.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Sempronius Liberalis, a standard-bearer of Legio III Augusta, to his mother, Octavia Sterceia.

EDCS-11300881.text_edition: miles legionis III Augustae centuria Volusi hic situs est militauit annos XVI vixit annos XXXV Caius Iulius Rufus comanupularis de suo curavit
EDCS-11300881.translation: A soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, from the centuria of Volusius, lies here. He served for 16 years and lived for 35 years. Gaius Iulius Rufus, his tent-mate, took care of this at his own expense.
EDCS-11300881.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates an unnamed soldier of Legio III Augusta, erected by his tent-mate, Gaius Iulius Rufus, noting his service and lifespan.

EDCS-11300884.text_edition: Gaius Iulius Africanus i C vixsit sic nnos LI Gaius Iulius Dux |(centurio)  pater
EDCS-11300884.translation: Gaius Iulius Africanus, [---] lived for 51 years. Gaius Iulius Dux, centurion, his father.
EDCS-11300884.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Africanus, who lived 51 years, placed by his father, Gaius Iulius Dux, a centurion.

EDCS-11600235.text_edition: Mercurio Augusto sacrum Lucius Liconius Rogatianus
EDCS-11600235.translation: Sacred to Augustus Mercury. Lucius Liconius Rogatianus.
EDCS-11600235.summary: This is a votive dedication to Augustus Mercury by Lucius Liconius Rogatianus.

EDCS-11600240.text_edition: Aulus Gabinius Quirina Datus patronus pagi et civitatis Thuggensis Marcus Gabinius Quirina Bassus flamen Augusti perpetuus patronus pagi et civitatis nomine suo et Auli Gabini Dati filii in quinque decuriis ab Imperatore Caesare Traiano Hadriano Augusto adlecto sic patroni pagi et civitatis templa solo suo a fundamentis sua pecunia struxerunt itemque dedicaverunt  Aulus Gabinius Quirina Datus patronus pagi et civitatis Thuggensis Marcus Gabinius Quirina Bassus flamen Augusti perpetuus patronus pagi et civitatis templa Concordiae Frugiferi Liberi Patris cum reliquis templis et xystis solo suo a fundamentis sua pecunia struxerunt in quod opus cum HS Lmilia promisissent multiplicata pecunia consummaverunt
EDCS-11600240.translation: Aulus Gabinius Quirina Datus, patron of the pagus and civitas of Thugga, and Marcus Gabinius Quirina Bassus, perpetual flamen of Augustus, patron of the pagus and civitas, in their own name and that of Aulus Gabinius Datus, son, who was adlected into the five decuriae by the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, as patrons of the pagus and civitas, built temples from their own funds from the foundations on their own land and likewise dedicated them. Aulus Gabinius Quirina Datus, patron of the pagus and civitas of Thugga, and Marcus Gabinius Quirina Bassus, perpetual flamen of Augustus, patron of the pagus and civitas, built the temples of Concordia, Frugifer Pater Liber, along with other temples and porticoes, from their own funds from the foundations on their own land. For this work, when they had promised 50,000 sesterces, they completed it with multiplied funds.
EDCS-11600240.summary: This inscription records the extensive patronage of Aulus Gabinius Quirina Datus and Marcus Gabinius Quirina Bassus, who, as patrons of Thugga, funded and dedicated the construction of several temples, including those to Concordia and Liber Pater, on their own land.

EDCS-11600241.text_edition: Lucio Tadio Luci filio Horatia Vero Caeciliano praefecto iure dicundo aedili quaestori equo publico et in quinque decurias adlecto sacerdoti nni CLXXX Uchitani Maiores patrono ob merita
EDCS-11600241.translation: To Lucius Tadius, son of Lucius, of the Horatian tribe, Verus Caecilianus, prefect for administering justice, aedile, quaestor, granted a public horse and adlected into the five decuriae, priest for 180 years. The Greater Uchitani to their patron for his merits.
EDCS-11600241.summary: This is an honorary inscription dedicated by the Greater Uchitani to Lucius Tadius Verus Caecilianus, recognizing his various magistracies and priesthood and his merits as their patron.

EDCS-11600249.summary: This is a fragmentary votive dedication to the goddess Laverna.

EDCS-11600252.text_edition: Augusto s(acrum)  Cai filius Felix et Mucia   instar decurionatus amplius pecunia a se adiecta
EDCS-11600252.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Gaius Felix, son of Gaius, and Mucia [dedicate this] in the manner of a decurionate, with additional money contributed by themselves.
EDCS-11600252.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Augustus by Gaius Felix and Mucia, indicating a contribution made as if for a decurionate, possibly a public work or gift to the community, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-11600253.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Allius Victorinus vixit annos XI
EDCS-11600253.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Allius Victorinus lived for 11 years.
EDCS-11600253.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Allius Victorinus, who died at the age of 11, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600254.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Ambia Fortunata pia vixit annis
EDCS-11600254.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Ambia Fortunata, pious, lived for [---] years.
EDCS-11600254.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ambia Fortunata, described as pious, with her age missing, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600259.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Durvius Felix pius vixit annos XXXVII hic situs est
EDCS-11600259.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Durvius Felix, pious, lived for 37 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-11600259.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Durvius Felix, who was 37 years old and described as pious, indicating his burial place and likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600260.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Durvius Fortunatus pius vixit annis XII
EDCS-11600260.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Durvius Fortunatus, pious, lived for 12 years.
EDCS-11600260.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Durvius Fortunatus, a pious individual who lived for 12 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600261.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Erennia Casta pia vixit annos LXXXXI hic sita est hic sita est
EDCS-11600261.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Erennia Casta, pious, lived for 91 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-11600261.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Erennia Casta, a pious woman who lived to the age of 91, indicating her burial place and likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600263.text_edition: Caius Licinius Beryllus pius vixit annos
EDCS-11600263.translation: Gaius Licinius Beryllus, pious, lived for [---] years.
EDCS-11600263.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Licinius Beryllus, described as pious, with his age missing, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600265.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Manlius Manlianus vixit annos LXX hic situs est
EDCS-11600265.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Manlius Manlianus lived for 70 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-11600265.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Manlius Manlianus, who lived for 70 years, indicating his burial place and likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600291.translation: Expedita, in peace. So be it.
EDCS-11600291.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for a woman named Expedita, expressing a wish for her to be in peace, likely dating from Late Antiquity.

EDCS-11600295.summary: This is a simple Christian funerary inscription for Sorica, expressing a wish for her to be in peace, likely dating from Late Antiquity.

EDCS-11600296.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for a person named Gratiosus, wishing them peace in death, likely dating to the early Christian period due to the phrase 'in pace'.

EDCS-11600297.text_edition: Haec memoria quondam M Nicasiae scolasticae quae vixit annis XX mensibus histt
EDCS-11600297.translation: This is the memorial of the once M. Nicasia, a teacher, who lived for 20 years and [an unknown number of] months.
EDCS-11600297.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument for a female teacher, M. Nicasia, noting her profession and age at death.

EDCS-12500357.text_edition: Vol Calassini kentenaria sic X Caprorenses kentenaria sic DCVII fieri kentenaria sic CCCXIIII R CCLIIII et erunt in conditorio in die XIIII Kalendas Martias centenaria DCCCCXXXI fieri in conditorio |(centenaria) |milleCLXXX  XIII Kalendas Martias ad Octobrem |(centenarius) Maurus pondo libras XXXV |(centenarius) Ianuarius pondo libras LV |(centenarius) Felix pondo libras VIIICCXXVI |(centenarius) Vindemialis pondo libras CCC |(centenarius) Victor Mercurialis pondo libras |(duo milia)XCVII |(centenarius) Augustalis pondo libras |milleCCCXCVIII |(centenarius) Maioricus pondo libras |(duo milia)XCVII |(centenarius) Marianus pondo libras IIIIDCLXXVII |(centenarius) Primus pondo libras IIIIDCLX |(centenarius) Murc pondo libras IIICCCCXXV |(centenarius) Evasius pondo libras |milleDCCCLXXII |(centenarius) Restutus pondo libras IIIIXXII |(centenarius) Victorinus pondo libras |(mille) DCCXCIIII fieri numero CCCL pondo libras LXXVIII Vol Calassini kentenaria sic LXXVI
EDCS-12500357.translation: Vol. Calassini hundredweights: 10. Caprorenses hundredweights: 607. To be made, hundredweights: 314. R: 254. And there will be in the storeroom on the 14th day before the Kalends of March, hundredweights: 931. To be made in the storeroom: 1180 hundredweights. 13th day before the Kalends of March until October: Maurus, 35 pounds of hundredweight; Ianuarius, 55 pounds of hundredweight; Felix, 8826 pounds of hundredweight; Vindemialis, 300 pounds of hundredweight; Victor Mercurialis, 2097 pounds of hundredweight; Augustalis, 1398 pounds of hundredweight; Maioricus, 2097 pounds of hundredweight; Marianus, 4677 pounds of hundredweight; Primus, 4660 pounds of hundredweight; Murc, 3425 pounds of hundredweight; Evasius, 1872 pounds of hundredweight; Restutus, 822 pounds of hundredweight; Victorinus, 1794 pounds of hundredweight. To be made: 350 in number, 78 pounds. Vol. Calassini hundredweights: 76.
EDCS-12500357.summary: This is an administrative record, likely from a public or private granary or storage facility, detailing quantities of goods (specified in hundredweights and pounds), their origin, and the names of individuals associated with their handling or provision, spanning from the 14th day before the Kalends of March until October.

EDCS-12500358.text_edition: VII Idus Martias ad Octobrem |(centenarius) Felix XXXIII pondo libras LXIIII |(summa) XXXIII pondo libras LXIIII inpletur Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero XXI |(centenaria) XXXIIII fieri in conditorio Zeugitano amphorae gemellariae numero CLXVII ascopae a Byzace(na numero VI |(summa) CCCCXIII pondo libras XX
EDCS-12500358.translation: 7th day before the Ides of March until October: Felix 33 hundredweight, 64 pounds. Total: 33 hundredweight, 64 pounds. Completed: Vol. (of) askopa from Byzacena, number 21 hundredweight 34. To be made in the Zeugitanian storeroom, amphorae of twin type: number 167. Askopa from Byzacena: number 6. Total: 413, 20 pounds.
EDCS-12500358.summary: This inscription is an administrative record of shipments and storage, detailing quantities of 'askopa' (likely liquid containers or their contents) from Byzacena and amphorae for a Zeugitanian storeroom, dated from the 7th day before the Ides of March until October.

EDCS-12500359.text_edition: VII Kalendas Maias ad Octobrem |(centenarius) Quintus |(centenaria) V pondo libras LX |(centenarius) Victorianus |(centenaria) XX pondo libras LXVII |(centenarius) Bilossus ab Occhi sic |(centenaria) V pondo |(centenarius) Tzelica |(centenaria) X pondo libras XX |(summa) XXXVIII pondo libras XLVII inpletur Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero XXII |(centenaria) XXXVIII pondo libras LXXV fieri in conditorio Zeugitano amphorae gemellariae numero CLXVII et kentenaria sic LXII fisci et Tebelbucitan QZ ascopae a Byzacena numero CCCCXLVI et Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero |(duo milia)CLVI olei Romae canon ascopae numero DCCLVII kentenaria sic |milleCCXXXII |(summa) VIIDCCCLII pondo libras LXXV
EDCS-12500359.translation: 7th day before the Kalends of May until October: Quintus, 5 hundredweight, 60 pounds; Victorianus, 20 hundredweight, 67 pounds; Bilossus from Occhi, 5 hundredweight; Tzelica, 10 hundredweight, 20 pounds. Total: 38 hundredweight, 47 pounds. Completed: Vol. (of) askopa from Byzacena, number 22 hundredweight, 38 hundredweight, 75 pounds. To be made in the Zeugitanian storeroom: amphorae of twin type, number 167; and 62 hundredweight from the fiscus; and from Tebelbucitan QZ, askopa from Byzacena, number 446; and Vol. (of) askopa from Byzacena, number 2156. Oil for the Roman canon: askopa number 757, 1232 hundredweight. Total: 7852, 75 pounds.
EDCS-12500359.summary: This detailed administrative record, dated from the 7th day before the Kalends of May until October, itemizes quantities of goods (including 'askopa' and oil for the Roman canon) from various named individuals and places like Byzacena and Tebelbucitan QZ, destined for a Zeugitanian storeroom, and notes contributions to the imperial treasury (fiscus).

EDCS-12500360.text_edition: VII Idus Apriles ad Octobrem |(centenarius) Flabianus sic |(centenaria) X Felix ab Ucubi |(centenaria) VII pondo libras XL |(centenarius) Peregrinus |(centenaria) XIIII pondo libras XC |(centenarius) Felix ab C |(centenaria) X pondo libras XCII |(centenarius) Doianus |(centenaria) pondo libras XXXV |(summa) CLIIII pondo libras LVII inpletur Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero XXI et olei Romae canon ascopae numero CLXI et Macrinenses ascopae a Byzacena numero VI |(centenaria) CLVI fieri in conditorio Zeugitano amphorae gemellariae numero CLXVII et kentenaria sic L fisci et de numero Tebelbucitan QZ ascopae a Byzacena numero CCC et kentenaria sic L de numero Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero |milleDXIIII et olei Romae canon ascopae numero CCLXXII et kentenaria sic |milleXCI |(summa) VCCLXV pondo libras L
EDCS-12500360.translation: 7th day before the Ides of April until October: Flavianus, 10 hundredweight; Felix from Ucubi, 7 hundredweight, 40 pounds; Peregrinus, 14 hundredweight, 90 pounds; Felix from C., 10 hundredweight, 92 pounds; Doianus, hundredweight, 35 pounds. Total: 154 hundredweight, 57 pounds. Completed: Vol. (of) askopa from Byzacena, number 21; and oil for the Roman canon, askopa number 161; and Macrinenses askopa from Byzacena, number 6 hundredweight, 156. To be made in the Zeugitanian storeroom: amphorae of twin type, number 167; and 50 hundredweight from the fiscus; and from the number of Tebelbucitan QZ, askopa from Byzacena, number 300; and 50 hundredweight from the number of Vol. (of) askopa from Byzacena, number 1514; and oil for the Roman canon, askopa number 272; and 1091 hundredweight. Total: 5265, 50 pounds.
EDCS-12500360.summary: This administrative record, dated from the 7th day before the Ides of April until October, meticulously lists individuals, their contributions in hundredweights and pounds, and the quantities of 'askopa' (likely containers of liquid, possibly oil) and amphorae designated for a Zeugitanian storeroom, alongside fiscal contributions and oil for the Roman canon from various locations including Byzacena and Tebelbucitan QZ.

EDCS-12500361.text_edition: VI Idus Apriles ad Octobrem |(centenarius) Donatilus |(centenaria) XII pondo libras L |(centenarius) Victorianus |(centenaria) VII pondo libras XV |(centenarius) Vanosus |(centenaria) XIIII pondo libras XIIII |(centenarius) Fir |(centenaria) XLVII pondo libras LXV |(centenarius) Kalius |(centenaria) XXIIII |(centenarius) Arus |(centenaria) XXIII pondo libras LIII |(centenarius) Victor |(centenaria) XLVI pondo libras LX |(centenarius) Romanus |(centenaria) LVII pondo libras X |(summa) CCXXXII pondo libras LXVII inpletur Macrinenses ascopae a Byzacena numero XLVIIII et kentenaria sic CXX AFR |(summa) CCXXXV fieri in conditorio Zeugitano amphorae gemellariae numero CLXVII et kentenaria sic L fisci et Tebelbucitan QZ ascopae a Byzacena numero CCC et kentenaria sic L et Macrinenses ascopae a Byzacena numero VI et kentenaria sic CXX olei Romae canon ascopae numero CCCXCI et kentenaria sic |(mille)XCI |(summa) VCCCCI pondo libras XXXV
EDCS-12500361.translation: 6th day before the Ides of April until October: Donatilus, 12 hundredweight, 50 pounds; Victorianus, 7 hundredweight, 15 pounds; Vanosus, 14 hundredweight, 14 pounds; Fir, 47 hundredweight, 65 pounds; Kalius, 24 hundredweight; Arus, 23 hundredweight, 53 pounds; Victor, 46 hundredweight, 60 pounds; Romanus, 57 hundredweight, 10 pounds. Total: 232 hundredweight, 67 pounds. Completed: Macrinenses askopa from Byzacena, number 49; and 120 hundredweight AFR. Total: 235. To be made in the Zeugitanian storeroom: amphorae of twin type, number 167; and 50 hundredweight from the fiscus; and from Tebelbucitan QZ, askopa from Byzacena, number 300; and 50 hundredweight; and Macrinenses askopa from Byzacena, number 6; and 120 hundredweight. Oil for the Roman canon: askopa number 391; and 1091 hundredweight. Total: 5901, 35 pounds.
EDCS-12500361.summary: This inscription is a detailed administrative record, dated from the 6th day before the Ides of April until October, itemizing various consignments of goods, particularly 'askopa' and oil for the Roman canon, from named individuals and locations like Byzacena and Tebelbucitan QZ, intended for a Zeugitanian storeroom, and includes contributions to the fiscus.

EDCS-12500362.text_edition: Pridie Nonas Maias ad Octobrem |(centenarius) inus |(centenaria) VIIII pondo libras XCIIII |(centenarius)  |(centenaria) XIII pondo libras XLII |(centenarius)  |(centenaria) VII pondo libras LXVI |(centenarius) nus |(centenaria) IIII pondo libras LXXXVII |(centenarius) Victor a Serue |(centenaria) V pondo libras XVIII |(centenarius) Felix |(centenaria) V pondo libras XIIII |(centenarius) Inventus |(centenaria) XXXV pondo libras LXII |(centenarius) Victor a Nobis sic |(centenaria) XXVII pondo libras XVIII |(summa) CXI pondo libras XI inpletur Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero LXVIII |(centenaria) CXI pondo libras X fieri in conditorio Zeugitano ψ numero CLXXXIIII et amphorae gemellariae numero CLXVII kentenaria LXII fisci et Tebelbucitan--- ascopae a Byzacena numero CCCCXLVI et kentenaria sic LXV Macrinenses ascopae a Byzacena LV et kentenaria sic CCLXXXV Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero |(duo milia)CCLXXV olei Romae canon ascopae numero DCCLVII kentenaria sic |milleCCXXXII |(summa) VIIIDCCCII pondo libras LXXV
EDCS-12500362.translation: Day before the Nones of May until October: [---]inus, 9 hundredweight, 94 pounds; [---], 13 hundredweight, 42 pounds; [---], 7 hundredweight, 66 pounds; [---]nus, 4 hundredweight, 87 pounds; Victor from Serue, 5 hundredweight, 18 pounds; Felix, 5 hundredweight, 14 pounds; Inventus, 35 hundredweight, 62 pounds; Victor from Nobis, 27 hundredweight, 18 pounds. Total: 111 hundredweight, 11 pounds. Completed: Vol. (of) askopa from Byzacena, number 68 hundredweight, 111 hundredweight, 10 pounds. To be made in the Zeugitanian storeroom: number 184 [unspecified units]; and amphorae of twin type, number 167; 62 hundredweight from the fiscus; and from Tebelbucitan--- askopa from Byzacena, number 446; and 65 hundredweight; Macrinenses askopa from Byzacena, 55; and 285 hundredweight. Vol. (of) askopa from Byzacena, number 2275. Oil for the Roman canon: askopa number 757, 1232 hundredweight. Total: 8802, 75 pounds.
EDCS-12500362.summary: This extensive administrative record, spanning from the day before the Nones of May until October, details contributions and movements of goods, specifically 'askopa' and oil for the Roman canon, from numerous individuals and locations, including Byzacena and Tebelbucitan, designated for a Zeugitanian storeroom, and includes fiscal allotments.

EDCS-12600043.text_edition: Invicto numini deae Caelestis pro salute et aeternitate imperii domini nostri Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Augustae matris Augusti et castrorum et senatus et patriae totiusque domus divinae
EDCS-12600043.translation: To the unconquered divinity of the goddess Caelestis, for the welfare and eternal existence of the empire of our lord Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and of Julia Augusta, mother of the Augustus, and of the camps, and of the Senate, and of the fatherland, and of the entire divine household.
EDCS-12600043.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the goddess Caelestis, made for the health and eternal rule of the reigning emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus (Caracalla), his mother Julia Domna, and the Roman state, dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12600056.text_edition: XI Kalendas Augustas navicula Felicis Xeri I Byzacenae  VI Kalendas Augustas navicula  Byzacenae CXLIIII reprobo IIII
EDCS-12600056.translation: 11th day before the Kalends of August: the small boat of Felix Xerus I from Byzacena. 6th day before the Kalends of August: the small boat from Byzacena, 144. Rejected: 4.
EDCS-12600056.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary administrative record, detailing shipments or arrivals of goods by small boats (naviculae) from Byzacena, likely for commercial or logistical purposes, on two specific dates in July, noting a quantity of rejected items.

EDCS-12700167.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aelius Publius veteranus militavit ala Britannica bis torquata pius vixit annos LV Numpidia sic fecit viro pientissimo et carissimo marito eius sit tibi terra levis VSAL  OQB
EDCS-12700167.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aelius Publius, a veteran, served in the double-torqued British cavalry wing, lived piously for 55 years. Numpidia thus made this for her most devoted and dearest husband. May the earth be light for you. VSAL OQB.
EDCS-12700167.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Numpidia to her husband, Aelius Publius, a veteran of the British cavalry, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-12700228.text_edition: Numini sancto inferno deo votum monitus solvit Cissus
EDCS-12700228.translation: To the holy infernal deity, Cissus, having been warned, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-12700228.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Cissus fulfilled a vow to an infernal deity after receiving a warning.

EDCS-12700229.text_edition: Diis Mauris Augustis sacrum pro salute Augusti Caesaris Marci Aureli Commodi Antonini toris procurator marmorum Numidicorum io
EDCS-12700229.translation: Sacred to the August Maurish Gods for the well-being of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus, Toris, procurator of Numidian marbles. Io!
EDCS-12700229.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription made by Toris, a procurator of Numidian marbles, to the August Maurish Gods for the health of Emperor Commodus, dating to his reign (late 2nd century AD).

EDCS-12800687.text_edition: Caius Acrasius Publi filius militavit legione III Augusta vixsit sic nnis XXXV O militavit annis XV heres Helvius rius Quirina H
EDCS-12800687.translation: Gaius Acrasius, son of Publius, served in the Third Augustan Legion. He lived for 35 years and served for 15 years. His heir, Helvius Rius Quirina, [made this].
EDCS-12800687.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Acrasius, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his heir, Helvius Rius Quirina.

EDCS-12800688.text_edition: Marcus Cornelius Marci filius Maecia Longus Neapole miles legionis III Augustae centuria Sextili vixit nnos LIX militavit nnos XXV hic situs est heredes posuerunt
EDCS-12800688.translation: Marcus Cornelius Longus, son of Marcus, of the Maecia tribe, from Neapolis, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, in the centuria of Sextilius, lived for 59 years and served for 25 years. He lies here. His heirs placed this.
EDCS-12800688.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Cornelius Longus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-12800689.text_edition: Clodius Arnensis Qu miles legionis  III AugustaeIFMC
EDCS-12800689.translation: Clodius Arnensis, of the Quirina tribe, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion. IFMC.
EDCS-12800689.summary: This is an incomplete inscription, likely funerary, for Clodius Arnensis, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-12800690.text_edition: Caius Iulius Augusti libertus Crestus Samianus
EDCS-12800690.translation: Gaius Julius Crestus, freedman of Augustus, from Samia.
EDCS-12800690.summary: This is an inscription identifying Gaius Julius Crestus, an imperial freedman from Samia, likely part of a larger monument or dedication.

EDCS-12800691.text_edition: Diis Manibus sacrum Tiberius Claudius Felix Cilicinenus  vixit nnos LI menses hic situs est
EDCS-12800691.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Tiberius Claudius Felix Cilicinenus lived for 51 years and [some] months. He lies here.
EDCS-12800691.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Felix Cilicinenus, recording his age and place of burial.

EDCS-13000608.text_edition: Iovi Augusto sacrum dedicante Cnaeo Suellio Flacco legato Augusti pro praetore Quintus Mantius Quinti filius Camilia  Alba Pompeia  legio III Augusta de suo fecit
EDCS-13000608.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Augustus, dedicated by Gnaeus Suellius Flaccus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power. Quintus Mantius, son of Quintus, of the Camilia tribe, from Alba Pompeia, of the Third Augustan Legion, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-13000608.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Augustus, made at personal expense by Quintus Mantius, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, under the authority of legate Gnaeus Suellius Flaccus, likely dating to the early-to-mid 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13000622.translation: To the Divine Pious Severus, father of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, conqueror of Arabs, Adiabenians, greatest conqueror of Parthians, greatest conqueror of Britons, greatest conqueror of Germans, greatest pontiff, with tribunician power for the 17th time, proclaimed Imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, proconsul, father of his country.
EDCS-13000622.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the deified Emperor Septimius Severus (Divo Pio Severo), set up by his son Caracalla, providing Caracalla's extensive imperial titles and dating the inscription to around 214 AD based on the tribunician power and consular dates.

EDCS-13000625.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-13000625.text_edition: Ex testamento Cai Corneli Egriliani praefecti legionis XIIII geminae quo testamento ex HS CCL milibus nummum arcum cum statuis Auggustorum nnostrorum item tetrastylis duobus cum statuis et Minervae quae in foro fieri praecepit praeter alia HS CCL milia nummum quae rei publicae ita ut certis diebus gymnasia populo publice in thermis praeberentur legavit et ad Kapitolinum argenti libras CLXX id est lances IIII auri libras XIIII id est phialas III scyphos II haece omnia secundum voluntatem eius in contione populi Corneli Fortunatus et Quinta fratres et heredes eius adsignaverunt et opus perfecerunt
EDCS-13000625.translation: By the will of Gaius Cornelius Egrilianus, prefect of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, by which will he ordered an arch with statues of our Augusti, likewise two tetrastyles with statues, and a statue of Minerva to be made in the forum, from 250,000 sesterces, besides another 250,000 sesterces which he bequeathed to the public, so that on certain days public gymnastic exercises might be provided for the people in the baths, and 170 pounds of silver for the Capitoline, that is, 4 platters, and 14 pounds of gold, that is, 3 bowls and 2 cups. All these things, according to his will, in an assembly of the people, Cornelius Fortunatus and Quinta, his siblings and heirs, assigned and completed the work.
EDCS-13000625.summary: This inscription records the execution of the will of Gaius Cornelius Egrilianus, a military prefect, who posthumously funded public works in the forum, provided for public baths, and donated precious metals to the Capitoline temple.

EDCS-13000642.translation: ] of the [third] Claudia Legion, lived X[+] years, served V years, is buried here. Lucius Annaeus [
EDCS-13000642.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier of the Claudia Legion, indicating his age, years of service, and the name of the dedicant.

EDCS-13000643.translation: Flavius Januarius, assistant in the office of the chief clerks, Homullius Saturninus, soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, made this for his comrade.
EDCS-13000643.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Homullius Saturninus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, erected a monument for his comrade Flavius Januarius, an assistant in the chief clerks' office.

EDCS-13000644.translation: Quintus Julius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Dioratus, from Autricum, soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, in the century of Lucius Julius, lived 30 years, served 5 years, is buried here. Titus Flavius Martialis, in the century of Nasidius, his heir, placed this monument.
EDCS-13000644.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Julius Dioratus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his heir Titus Flavius Martialis.

EDCS-13000646.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Repentinus, imperial letter-carrier, lived 25 years. His colleagues, the letter-carriers, made this.
EDCS-13000646.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by colleagues to Repentinus, an imperial letter-carrier, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-13000654.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Audasius, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, Succedens, duumvir, prefect for administering justice, quaestor, lived 70 years. Audasius Pomptinus, Primitius, and Januaria, his freedmen and heirs, made this for their best and most devoted patron. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000654.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Audasius Succedens, a Roman official who held several civic positions, erected by his freedmen and heirs.

EDCS-13000656.text_edition: Publi Saturnini Saturniani flaminis perpetui filio nepoti Cai Iuli Romuleani equitis Romani pontificis qui primus a condita civitate sua ob honorem flamoni annui munus Idibus omnibus senis paribus curiae suae dedit universae curiae et Augustales pecunia sua locus datus ex decreto ordinis
EDCS-13000656.translation: To the son and grandson of Publius Saturninus Saturnianus, perpetual flamen, and of Gaius Julius Romuleanus, Roman knight and pontiff, who was the first from the founding of his city, in honor of his annual flamenship, to give a gift of six pairs of gladiators on every Ides to his curia; the entire curia and the Augustales erected this at their own expense. The location was granted by decree of the council.
EDCS-13000656.summary: This inscription commemorates the son and grandson of prominent citizens, including a perpetual flamen and a pontiff who made a significant gladiatorial offering, and was erected by the entire curia and the Augustales with a plot granted by senatorial decree.

EDCS-13000665.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Felicitas lived 3 years; she is buried here. Hilarus, freedman of the Augusti, made this for his native-born girl.
EDCS-13000665.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Felicitas, erected by Hilarus, an imperial freedman, likely her father.

EDCS-13000667.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Victoria Augusta lived 50 years; she is buried here. Flavius Primus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-13000667.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavius Primus to his beloved wife, Victoria Augusta, who lived to be 50 years old.

EDCS-13000670.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius Viator [lived X years], he is buried here. Publius Aelius Felix made this for his devoted father.
EDCS-13000670.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Publius Aelius Felix for his devoted father, Publius Aelius Viator.

EDCS-13000674.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Apuleius Gudullus lived 65 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000674.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Apuleius Gudullus, indicating he lived 65 years and is buried at this spot, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000675.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Apuleius Miggin lived 26 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000675.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Apuleius Miggin, who lived for 26 years and is interred at this location, typical of Roman epitaphs.

EDCS-13000676.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Attia Hilaritas lived 29 years, 5 months, 6 days. Iulius Nervalis made this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-13000676.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Iulius Nervalis to his most devoted wife, Attia Hilaritas, who lived for 29 years, 5 months, and 6 days, typical of Roman marital epitaphs.

EDCS-13000677.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Audasius Primitivus and Audasia Ianuaria made this for Audasia Fortunata, their excellent and most devoted daughter, who deserved well of us. She lived 16 years, 3 months. She is buried here.
EDCS-13000677.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Audasius Primitivus and Audasia Ianuaria for their beloved daughter, Audasia Fortunata, who died at the age of 16 years and 3 months.

EDCS-13000678.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Audasia Charitu lived 30 years. Pomptinus, her husband, made this for his most devoted wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-13000678.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Pomptinus to his most devoted wife, Audasia Charitu, who lived for 30 years, marking her burial place.

EDCS-13000680.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelius Fortunatus set this up for Aurelius Victorinus, his dearest and well-deserving father, and for Aurelia Cara, his sweetest and well-deserving mother. The mother lived 20 years. The father lived 80 years.
EDCS-13000680.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Fortunatus in honor of his dearest father, Aurelius Victorinus, who lived 80 years, and his sweetest mother, Aurelia Cara, who lived 20 years.

EDCS-13000681.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Caecilius Ianuarius lived 62 years, 11 months, 25 days. Marcus Caecilius Ianuarius made this for his dearest father.
EDCS-13000681.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Marcus Caecilius Ianuarius for his dearest father, Marcus Caecilius Ianuarius, who lived 62 years, 11 months, and 25 days.

EDCS-13000682.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Calpurnia Caesita lived 25 years. She is buried here. Calpurnius Felix, father, and Hirria Rogata, mother, made this for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-13000682.summary: This is a funerary monument for Calpurnia Caesita, who lived 25 years, set up by her parents, Calpurnius Felix and Hirria Rogata, for their sweetest daughter.

EDCS-13000686.translation: To the Divine Shades of Claudia Aphrodisia.
EDCS-13000686.summary: This is the fragmentary beginning of a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades of Claudia Aphrodisia.

EDCS-13000691.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cornificia Fortunata lived for [unknown number of years].
EDCS-13000691.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornificia Fortunata, with the age and some text missing due to damage.

EDCS-13000692.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Domitia Primitiva lived for 32 years; she lies here. Gaius Domitius Pudens made this for his dearest and most faithful freedwoman.
EDCS-13000692.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Gaius Domitius Pudens for his freedwoman, Domitia Primitiva, who lived to be 32 years old.

EDCS-13000693.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Faonius Marcellus, son of Gaius, lived for [an uncertain number of] years. He made this in his lifetime.
EDCS-13000693.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Faonius Marcellus, son of Gaius, which he had prepared for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-13000694.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Flavius Agilis lived for 80 years. Flavius, his son, made this for his dutiful father.
EDCS-13000694.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavius for his dutiful father, Titus Flavius Agilis, who lived to be 80 years old.

EDCS-13000696.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Flavius Ianuarius lived for 20 years. His heirs made this. He is laid to rest here.
EDCS-13000696.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Ianuarius, who lived for 20 years, set up by his heirs.

EDCS-13000699.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Flaviana lived for 17 years. Demetrianus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-13000699.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Demetrianus for his dearest wife, Flaviana, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-13000700.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Folius Felix lived for 12 years. Martialis made this for his dutiful son.
EDCS-13000700.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Martialis for his dutiful son, Quintus Folius Felix, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-13000703.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Fusca lived for 10 years, 1 month, 5 days; she lies here. Gaius Caecilius Cinna made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-13000703.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fusca, who lived for 10 years, 1 month, and 5 days, dedicated by Gaius Caecilius Cinna for his dearest wife.

EDCS-13000706.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Ianuarius lived for 36 years. His father(?) made this.
EDCS-13000706.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ianuarius, who lived for 36 years, set up by his father.

EDCS-13000707.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Ianuarius Halconius(?), dutiful, lived for 70 years.
EDCS-13000707.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ianuarius Halconius, described as dutiful, who lived for 70 years.

EDCS-13000711.translation: Iulius Gududio lived for 8 years; he is buried here. Iulia Saturnina made this for her sweetest son.
EDCS-13000711.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Iulius Gududio, set up by his mother Iulia Saturnina for her son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000713.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Iulius Minervalis lived for 70 years, 7 months, and 7 days; he is buried here. Tullius Pistus made this.
EDCS-13000713.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Iulius Minervalis, erected by Tullius Pistus, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000714.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Iulius Niger lived for 65 years. Caecilia Candida made this for her sweetest husband.
EDCS-13000714.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Niger, set up by his wife Caecilia Candida, probably dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-13000716.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Speratus made this for his sweetest daughter Ierinaniu, who lived for 2 years, 8 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-13000716.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Speratus for his daughter Ierinaniu, recording her short life, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000718.translation: Gaius Iulius Victorinus lived for 60 years; he is buried here. Iulii Vitalis and Arnobius, sons of Triturrii, made this for their dearest father.
EDCS-13000718.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Victorinus, set up by his sons Iulii Vitalis and Arnobius, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000720.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iuia Eutyche lived for 35 years, 10 months, and 12 days; she is buried here. Amantius made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-13000720.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iuia Eutyche, erected by her husband Amantius, probably dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-13000721.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Fortunata lived piously for 60 years, chaste, good, inviolate. This rare gift, Fortunata, from your husband, pleases you so. Hardalio, worthy of my name, because you, dear, are survived by your children's children, and I flourished while you were safe.
EDCS-13000721.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Fortunata, celebrating her virtues and her legacy through her children and grandchildren, set up by her husband Hardalio, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000722.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Fortunata lived for 40 years. Maximus made this for his pious mother.
EDCS-13000722.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Fortunata, erected by her son Maximus for his pious mother, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000724.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Verecunda Tritae lived for 2 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13000724.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Iulia Verecunda Tritae, who lived for two years, probably dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-13000729.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Maronica lived for 50 years. Upius Rugosus lived for [---] years, 6 months, 2 days. Made by her peers.
EDCS-13000729.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maronica and Upius Rugosus, erected by their peers, likely from the Imperial period, though Upius's age is partly illegible.

EDCS-13000731.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Minucius Saturus lived 127 years. Lucius Minucius Felix made this for his excellent father.
EDCS-13000731.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Minucius Saturus, who lived to an exceptional age, erected by his son Lucius Minucius Felix, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000734.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Occia Auge lived 37 years; she is buried here. Pomponius Gaetulus, her husband, made this.
EDCS-13000734.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Occia Auge, aged 37, erected by her husband Pomponius Gaetulus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000736.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription commemorating Gabia Fausta, who lived 19 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000737.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Publius Paetius Abascantius, pious, who lived 25 years, 11 months, 22 days; he is buried here. Publius Paetius Abascantius, his father, made this.
EDCS-13000737.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Paetius Abascantius, who died young, erected by his father, also named Publius Paetius Abascantius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000738.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Perellia Fortunula lived 12 years. Her parent made this.
EDCS-13000738.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Perellia Fortunula, aged 12, dedicated by her parent, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000739.translation: Publius Petronius Festus, son of Decimus, pious, lived 7 years, 5 months, and a half hour; he is buried here.
EDCS-13000739.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Petronius Festus, son of Decimus, who died at a very young age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000741.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Plotius Maximus lived 65 years. Labenia Glauce made this for her pious husband; he is buried here.
EDCS-13000741.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Plotius Maximus, aged 65, erected by his wife Labenia Glauce, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000746.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Pomponius Adiutor lived 62 years, 1 month, 17 days.
EDCS-13000746.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Marcus Pomponius Adiutor, detailing his age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000747.translation: Marcus Pomponius Albinus lived 63 years, [X] months, 16 days.
EDCS-13000747.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Marcus Pomponius Albinus, detailing his age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000750.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Poposa lived 16 years. Hermophilus made this for his dearest daughter; she is buried here.
EDCS-13000750.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Poposa, aged 16, dedicated by her father Hermophilus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000751.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Rufrius(?)... Pompeia(?) Inuena(?) made this for her most beloved husband. He is laid to rest here.
EDCS-13000751.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Pompeia Inuena's dedication of a tombstone to her husband, Rufrius(?).

EDCS-13000754.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Sempronius Amaranthus lived 60 years. His son made this for his dutiful father.
EDCS-13000754.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a son, Marcus Sempronius Amaranthus, to his dutiful father, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-13000755.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Sergia Rustica lived 36 years, 7 months. Claudius(?) Titius(?) Crescens...
EDCS-13000755.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sergia Rustica, who lived 36 years and 7 months, likely set up by Claudius(?) Titius(?) Crescens.

EDCS-13000757.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Speratus lived 34 years.
EDCS-13000757.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription commemorating Speratus, who lived 34 years.

EDCS-13000759.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Tadius Cotronius(?) lived 3 years. His father made this. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Tadius Victorinus lived...
EDCS-13000759.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, with one part dedicated by a father to his son, Tadius Cotronius, who lived 3 years, and the other for Gaius Tadius Victorinus.

EDCS-13000760.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Tanonnius Felix, son of Publius, lived 27 years. O most dutiful son, he is laid to rest here.
EDCS-13000760.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Tanonnius Felix, son of Publius, who lived 27 years, likely set up by his family lamenting his passing as a dutiful son.

EDCS-13000761.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Valerius Sisa made this while alive for himself and his parents. He lived [number of] years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Homullia Secundilla lived 40 years. Sisa [made this] for his mother. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Valerius Natalis lived 75 years. Sisa [made this] for his father. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Valerius Eupraxius lived [number of] years, and Iulia Tertia lived [number of] years. Sisa made this. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Banasius lived [number of] years, and Pretiosa lived [number of] years.
EDCS-13000761.summary: This is a series of interconnected funerary inscriptions, primarily recording Valerius Sisa dedicating monuments for himself, his parents (Homullia Secundilla and Valerius Natalis), and other family members (Valerius Eupraxius, Iulia Tertia, Banasius, and Pretiosa).

EDCS-13000763.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Vettius Rufus, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, a dutiful man, lived 34 years. Marcus Vettius Rufus, his father, and Lucius Vettius Tenax, his dutiful brother, took care of having this made.
EDCS-13000763.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Vettius Rufus, who lived 34 years, erected by his father, Marcus Vettius Rufus, and his brother, Lucius Vettius Tenax.

EDCS-13000767.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Vindemia lived 35 years. Ispiculus made this for his most beloved wife. She is laid to rest here.
EDCS-13000767.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Ispiculus dedicates a tombstone to his most beloved wife, Vindemia, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-13000768.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Ulpius Liberalis lived 15 years, 11 months, 7 days. He is laid to rest here. Marcus Ulpius Vibianus, his father, and Aurelia Victoria, his mother, made this for their incomparable son.
EDCS-13000768.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Marcus Ulpius Vibianus and Aurelia Victoria for their incomparable son, Ulpius Liberalis, who died at the young age of 15 years, 11 months, and 7 days.

EDCS-13000769.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Ulpia Quinta lived for 40 years. Hortensius Geminius made this for his dearest daughter-in-law.
EDCS-13000769.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Quinta, erected by her father-in-law, Hortensius Geminius, likely dating to the Imperial period given the formulaic D.M.S.

EDCS-13000772.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. She was good in her manners, urbane, and holy, worthy wife with her Gaius. I adore you. Your memory, for both of us, sought, rare are the titles for your memory. We, of our goodness, go cheerful towards you and in good spirits. For many years may we celebrate this, and may we return after you, dearest, unless your good, chaste, innocent fame... You were noble... Brea, hail, woman. Farewell, and this sacred monument. Of rare chastity.
EDCS-13000772.summary: This is a lengthy and highly emotional funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated to a woman named Brea by her husband, Gaius, praising her virtues and expressing his enduring love and grief, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000800.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Margarita the dutiful lived for 75 years; she is buried here. Gudullus, adjutant of our Augustus's record office, made this for his most uncommon mother-in-law.
EDCS-13000800.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Margarita, erected by her son-in-law, Gudullus, who held an administrative position under the emperor (Augustus), indicating an Imperial date for this monument.

EDCS-13000801.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Attius Mattius lived for 47 years. His sons made this.
EDCS-13000801.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Attius Mattius, erected by his sons, typical of Roman funerary monuments from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000803.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Perellius Felix lived for [unknown] years. Caecilia Zaba(?) made this for her husband.
EDCS-13000803.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Perellius Felix, set up by his wife Caecilia Zaba (or a similar name), consistent with a Roman tombstone from the Imperial era.

EDCS-13000805.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Septimius Iulianus lived for 90 years. Caecilia Paulina made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-13000805.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Septimius Iulianus, erected by his wife Caecilia Paulina, a common type of Roman tombstone from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000867.translation: Decimus Seius Iuvenis, son of Decimus, of the Quirina tribe, from Bordeaux, soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, centuria of Nepos. He lived for 46 years, served for 23 years. He is buried here. Gisatia Seiana made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-13000867.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a Roman soldier, Decimus Seius Iuvenis, of Legio III Augusta, indicating a military tombstone from the Imperial period, erected by his wife Gisatia Seiana.

EDCS-13000869.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Domitius Saturninus lived for 25 years and 13 days. Domitia Crescentina arranged for this to be made for her dearest brother.
EDCS-13000869.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitius Saturninus, commissioned by his sister Domitia Crescentina, consistent with a Roman tombstone from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000870.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius M. Rogatus lived for 95 years. His sons made this.
EDCS-13000870.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius M. Rogatus, erected by his sons, a common form of Roman tombstone from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000934.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Aelia Nina lived for 85 years; she is buried here. A. S. and P. made this.
EDCS-13000934.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Nina, erected by individuals whose names are abbreviated (A. S. and P.), likely a tombstone from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-13000935.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Apuleius Martis lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000935.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Apuleius Martis, who lived 60 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000939.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Numerius Petronius Successus, 85 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-13000939.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Numerius Petronius Successus, aged 85, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000941.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Tutia Vitalis, daughter of Publius, lived 67 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13000941.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tutia Vitalis, daughter of Publius, who lived 67 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000994.text_edition: Ex auctoritate Imperatoris Nervae Traiani Caesaris Augusti Germanici Dacici Lucius Minicius Natalis legatus Augusti pro praetore inter Madaurenses et Musulamios
EDCS-13000994.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, Lucius Minicius Natalis, legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power, [acted] between the Madaurenses and the Musulamii.
EDCS-13000994.summary: This inscription records an official act or decision made by Lucius Minicius Natalis, a legate of Emperor Trajan, concerning a dispute or boundary between two groups, the Madaurenses and the Musulamii, dating to the reign of Trajan (98-117 AD).

EDCS-13001069.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Scribonius Celsinus, of the Quirina tribe, pious, lived 49 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13001069.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Scribonius Celsinus, a pious man of the Quirina tribe, who lived 49 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13001088.text_edition: no nex sacro praecepto domini nostri Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et consensum sic utrarumque partium decernente Claudio Iuliano proconsule clarissimo viro
EDCS-13001088.translation: not by sacred precept of our lord Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus and by consent of both parties, thus decreeing Claudius Julianus, proconsul, most distinguished man.
EDCS-13001088.summary: This inscription fragment records a legal or administrative decision made by the proconsul Claudius Julianus, a man of senatorial rank, not by imperial decree but with the consent of both parties, likely during the reign of Antoninus Pius (138-161 AD) or later emperors sharing similar titles.

EDCS-13001089.translation: To the Great Idaean Mother of the Gods, most holy, for the health and safety of our lord Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, and Julia Mamaea Augusta, and their entire divine house, Publius Caecilius Felix, priest from the order of pontiffs, undertook a criobolium and a taurobolium.
EDCS-13001089.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Publius Caecilius Felix, a priest, to the Magna Mater (Great Mother of the Gods) for the well-being of Emperor Severus Alexander, Julia Mamaea, and the imperial house, specifically mentioning his performance of the criobolium and taurobolium rituals, dating to the reign of Severus Alexander (222-235 AD).

EDCS-13001538.translation: Sacred to Victory Augusta. In the year of the suffetship of Asmunis Muthumbalis, son of Mutthumba, and Urbanus Auchusor, son of Auchusor; under the principate of Pudens Auchusor, son of Auchusor; with the care of Clemens Saphiris, P.A.D.P.P. (exact meaning of abbreviation unclear).
EDCS-13001538.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Victoria Augusta, dated by local Punic magistracies (suffetes and principate) and overseen by Clemens Saphiris, likely from an African province during the Imperial period.

EDCS-13001588.translation: ...a vow... he vaporized... who granted hours... by a furious storm... enriched forever... to the most distinguished man, the proconsul, rightly... ...Optatus and Paulinus being consuls.
EDCS-13001588.summary: This fragmented inscription, potentially a poetic or dedicatory text, mentions a vow, a storm, and a proconsul, and can be dated to 273 AD based on the consuls Optatus and Paulinus.

EDCS-13002447.translation: Marcus Canteius, soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, in the century of Perpetuus, lived 30 years, served 5 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002447.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Canteius, a soldier of Legio III Augusta, who lived 30 years and served 5 years, likely from the Imperial period and possibly from North Africa where this legion was stationed.

EDCS-13002449.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Stercula lived 5 years, 2 months, 7 days. Liberalis, adjutant to our Augustus's tabularium, and Felicia made this for their dearest daughter. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002449.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Liberalis, an imperial adjutant, and Felicia to their young daughter, Stercula, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-13002450.translation: Marcus Iulius Maternus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, from Augustonemetum, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, in Crispinus's century, lived 27 years, served 5 years. He is buried here. Sextus Valerius Atticinus, standard-bearer of the cavalry of the Third Augustan Legion.
EDCS-13002450.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Maternus, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, likely erected by his comrade, Sextus Valerius Atticinus, a vexillarius of the same legion's cavalry.

EDCS-13002451.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Marcus Aurelius Ursio, freedman of the younger Augustus, who lived 90 years, 3 months, 5 days.
EDCS-13002451.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Aurelius Ursio, a freedman of an Augustus, who lived to an advanced age.

EDCS-13002452.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Celsina, also known as Lampadaria, who lived 6 years, 8 months. She is buried here. Iulia Celsina made this for her sweetest daughter.
EDCS-13002452.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Celsina to her young daughter, Iulia Celsina (also called Lampadaria), who died at the age of six.

EDCS-13002453.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Secura, pious, lived 64 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002453.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Secura, a woman remembered for her piety, who lived 64 years.

EDCS-13002485.text_edition: Ex auctoritate Imperatoris Caesaris Traiani Augusti Germanici Dacici Lucius Munatius Gallus legatus pro praetore finibus Musulamiorum ectis vetustatis tam abolevit
EDCS-13002485.translation: By the authority of Imperator Caesar Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, Lucius Munatius Gallus, propraetorian legate, boundaries of the Musulamii extended, old things so abolished.
EDCS-13002485.summary: This inscription records the extension of the Musulamian territory and the abolition of old customs, carried out by Lucius Munatius Gallus, a propraetorian legate, under the authority of Emperor Trajan.

EDCS-13002527.translation: Publius Messius Melissus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, from Augustodunum, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, in Lucilius's century, pious, lived 30 years, served 4 years. He is buried here. Decimus Clodius, arms-keeper, from the same century, placed this monument.
EDCS-13002527.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Messius Melissus, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, erected by his comrade Decimus Clodius, an arms-keeper from the same century.

EDCS-13002528.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Valerius Vitalis, a soldier from the unit of the Martenses, from Gallia ESS, lived 55 years.
EDCS-13002528.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Vitalis, a soldier from a unit of Martenses (possibly Martians) from Gaul, who died at 55 years old.

EDCS-13002530.translation: Centurion, armorer of the Second Adiutrix Legion, promoted into the Third Augustan Legion, with 26 years of service, who lived 46 years, 2 months, 20 days. He is buried here. Aurelia Dio[---] [---] for her husband [---] made this.
EDCS-13002530.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a centurion and armorer who served in both Legio II Adiutrix and Legio III Augusta, set up by his wife Aurelia Dio[---].

EDCS-13002538.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Fortunatus, adjutant to our Augustus from the record office, lived 32 years, 1 month, 17 days. He is buried here. [---] and Venustula, his dearest wife, made this for him. Vegethi.
EDCS-13002538.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fortunatus, an imperial adjutant from the commentarii office, erected by his wife Venustula and another individual.

EDCS-13002539.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Maxima, our Emperor's slave, lived for 5 years. She lies here. Victor made this for his most devoted daughter.
EDCS-13002539.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Maxima, a five-year-old imperial slave, erected by her father Victor. It likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002540.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sallustia Amnubiti(?) of our Emperors, lived for 18 years, 3 months, 5 days. Januaria, freedwoman of the Emperors, her mother, made this for her most unique daughter.
EDCS-13002540.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sallustia Amnubiti(?), an 18-year-old, erected by her mother Januaria, a freedwoman of the Emperors, suggesting a date during the Imperial period with co-emperors.

EDCS-13002541.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cornelius Vincentius, alumnus of the arena, lived for [?] years, 3 months, 10 days. Baberius, his father, made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-13002541.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelius Vincentius, an 'alumnus' (ward or foster child) of the arena, erected by his father Baberius. It likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002545.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Aelius Salvianus lived for 60 years. He lies here. Aelii Saturninus, Messianus, Octavianus, Sedatus [made this] for their father.
EDCS-13002545.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Aelius Salvianus, aged 60, erected by his sons Saturninus, Messianus, Octavianus, and Sedatus. It likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002546.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aemilius Processianus lived for 13 years, 2 months, 16 days. He lies here. Manilius Restutus, his grandfather, and Aemilius Felicissimus made this for their sweetest son/grandson.
EDCS-13002546.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Processianus, a 13-year-old boy, erected by his grandfather Manilius Restutus and Aemilius Felicissimus (likely his father). It likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002547.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aemilia Donativa lived for 1 year, 4 months, 13 days. She lies here, who lived sweeter than a rose. Turbo, her father, and Designata, her mother, made this for their daughter.
EDCS-13002547.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Donativa, a young child, erected by her parents, Turbo and Designata. It likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002548.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aemilia Processa lived for 59 years. Manilius Restutus, her husband, and the Aemilii, her sons, made this for their dearest mother.
EDCS-13002548.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Processa, aged 59, erected by her husband Manilius Restutus and her sons. It likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002553.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Apponius Hilarus Tonantius lived for 37 years, 4 months, 9 days. He lies here. Licinia Montana made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-13002553.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Apponius Hilarus Tonantius, aged 37, erected by his wife Licinia Montana. It likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002555.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Lucius Atellius Terminalis, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, who lived for 80 years. He lies here. While I live, I took care to have this made.
EDCS-13002555.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Atellius Terminalis, aged 80, which he commissioned himself during his lifetime. It indicates a Roman citizen from the Papirian tribe and likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002559.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caelia Primula lived for 7 months, 8 days. She lies here. Lucius Caelius Primulus and Julia Dativa [made this] for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-13002559.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caelia Primula, an infant, erected by her parents Lucius Caelius Primulus and Julia Dativa. It likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002560.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caelia Victoria lived for 3 years, 4 months. Her father, Caelius Maximus, made this for his daughter. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002560.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Caelia Victoria, erected by her father, Caelius Maximus, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002561.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Claudius Abigeus, a freedman, lived for 8 years, 6 months, 27 days. Claudius Orion made this for his dearest foster-son. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002561.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudius Orion to his deceased foster-son, Claudius Abigeus, a freedman, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002563.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cornelius Dativus lived for [an unknown number of] years. He is buried here. After the death of Cornelius Lupercus, a most exceptional father, as a comfort.
EDCS-13002563.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelius Dativus, which also mentions the deceased's father, Cornelius Lupercus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002568.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius F. Felix lived for 50 years.
EDCS-13002568.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Gaius F. Felix, recording his age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002572.translation: Fortunatus lived for 55 years. Petronia Quarta took care to have this made for her dearest husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002572.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fortunatus, commissioned by his wife Petronia Quarta, probably from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002575.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gavilia Felicula lived for 15 years. Her father, Flavius Ianuarius, made the tomb.
EDCS-13002575.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gavilia Felicula, erected by her father, Flavius Ianuarius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002576.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gentia Prima lived for 50 years, 2 months. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002576.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age and burial of Gentia Prima, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002577.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Iulius Donatianus lived for 32 years, 4 months, 6 days. Gaius Iulius Felix made this for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-13002577.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Iulius Felix to his deceased brother, Gaius Iulius Donatianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002578.summary: This extremely brief inscription appears to be a name or a label, possibly for a personal possession or a marker, with Sisatius possibly being a name and Mensa perhaps referring to a table or an offering plate, from an uncertain date.

EDCS-13002581.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Castula made this for her husband Donatus. He lived for 72 years.
EDCS-13002581.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Donatus, erected by his wife Iulia Castula, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13002582.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Ianuaria lived for 23 years, 2 months, 25 days. Gaius Roius Emeritus made this for his most devoted wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002582.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the life span of Iulia Ianuaria and was set up by her husband, Gaius Roius Emeritus.

EDCS-13002584.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Vitalis lived for 45 years. She is buried here. Her children made this for their most devoted mother.
EDCS-13002584.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the children of Iulia Vitalis to their mother, noting her age at death and burial place.

EDCS-13002586.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lepidius Congius(?) lived for 75 years. His devoted wife made this. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002586.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lepidius Congius(?), erected by his wife, detailing his age at death and his burial location.

EDCS-13002587.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Liberalis lived for 11 years.
EDCS-13002587.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Liberalis, stating only his age at death.

EDCS-13002588.translation: To the Divine Shades. Licinia Claudia lived for 12 months. Lucius Licinius Speratus made this for his devoted daughter.
EDCS-13002588.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Licinius Speratus to his young daughter, Licinia Claudia.

EDCS-13002589.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Licinia Quartula(?) lived for 43 years. Her daughter made this for her mother.
EDCS-13002589.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a daughter for her mother, Licinia Quartula(?), noting her mother's age at death.

EDCS-13002590.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marius Felix, also known as Cucio(?)...
EDCS-13002590.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Marius Felix, who also went by the name Cucio(?).

EDCS-13002592.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Petronius Victor lived for 60 years. His son made this willingly and with good will.
EDCS-13002592.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Petronius Victor, set up by his son, indicating the deceased's age at death.

EDCS-13002594.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Pomponia Firmana Mascaradiana lived for 33 years and 4 months.
EDCS-13002594.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pomponia Firmana Mascaradiana, providing her age at death.
