EDCS-15100374.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Trebius Ianuarius lived 15 years; he is buried here. Annia Maxima erected this for her dutiful son. // Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Trebius Maximus lived 45 years; he is buried here. His dutiful wife erected this.
EDCS-15100374.summary: This is a double funerary monument, with the first inscription dedicated by Annia Maxima to her son Gaius Trebius Ianuarius, and the second by an unnamed dutiful wife to Gaius Trebius Maximus.

EDCS-15100375.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Valerius Institor lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-15100375.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Marcus Valerius Institor, who died at 55 years of age.

EDCS-15100376.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. F. Valeria Dubitata lived for 5 years and 20 days. Her father placed this for his daughter.
EDCS-15100376.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by a father to his young daughter, F. Valeria Dubitata, who died at the age of five years and twenty days.

EDCS-15100377.translation: Here lies Varius, surnamed Frontonianus, whom his charming wife Cornelia Galla placed, restoring the sweet comforts of old life. She set up marble visages, eyes, and spirit, so that she might longer be sated by his dear form. This will be the solace of sight, for the pledge of love is kept in her heart with the sweet memory of her mind, lest her lip should perish in easy oblivion. While she lives, her husband is in her whole heart. Nor is it a wonder, since what woman would have such character... He, pious... indulgent... and that... happy... she snatched away... for...
EDCS-15100377.summary: This is a poetic and deeply personal funerary inscription placed by Cornelia Galla for her husband, Varius Frontonianus, expressing her enduring love and grief, likely dating to the imperial period given its elaborate nature.

EDCS-15100378.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Venustus the pious lived for 80 years; he is buried here. [Someone] placed this for his most pious father.
EDCS-15100378.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Venustus, who lived to the age of 80, placed by an unnamed individual (likely a son or daughter) for their pious father.

EDCS-15100381.translation: Caia Vetutia, a matron, lived for [an unknown number of] years.
EDCS-15100381.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Caia Vetutia, a matron, recording her name and status but with her age incomplete.

EDCS-15100382.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Volussena Ianuaria lived for 38 years; she is buried here. Lucius Granius Victor placed this for his excellent wife. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Volussena Gududia lived for 96 years; she is buried here. Lucius Granius Victor placed this for his daughter.
EDCS-15100382.summary: This is a double funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Granius Victor to his wife, Volussena Ianuaria (who died at 38), and to his daughter, Volussena Gududia (who died at 96), likely on a single monument or adjacent stelae.

EDCS-15100411.translation: Rufus, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, lived for 94 years; he is buried here. Marcus Nunnius Secundinus placed this for his pious maternal uncle.
EDCS-15100411.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rufus, a veteran of Legio III Augusta, who lived to a remarkable 94 years, dedicated by his nephew Marcus Nunnius Secundinus.

EDCS-15100412.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Caelius Felix, a priest, lived for 60 years. Quintus Caelius Felix the son placed this for his pious father.
EDCS-15100412.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Caelius Felix, a priest, who lived to 60 years, dedicated by his son, also named Quintus Caelius Felix.

EDCS-15100415.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Atilia Quartilla lived for 35 years; she is buried here. Cornelius Saxo placed this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-15100415.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atilia Quartilla, who died at 35, dedicated by her husband Cornelius Saxo, expressing his affection.

EDCS-15100416.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caecilia Concordia the pious lived for 32 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-15100416.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Concordia, described as pious, who died at 32 years of age.

EDCS-15100424.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavia Seundina, a holy virgin, lived for 19 years and 7 months; she is buried here. [The remainder is mostly illegible, possibly referring to who made the monument].
EDCS-15100424.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Seundina, described as a holy virgin, who died young at 19 years and 7 months.

EDCS-15100427.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Nonia Prima lived 23 years. Iulius Donatus placed this for his devoted wife.
EDCS-15100427.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a tombstone, dedicated by Iulius Donatus to his deceased wife, Nonia Prima, who lived 23 years, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-15100428.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Postumia Mustacia, freedwoman of Gaius, lived 41 years; she is buried here. Pullimer placed this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-15100428.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Postumia Mustacia, a freedwoman, erected by her husband Pullimer, commemorating her 41 years of life and burial at the site, likely from the imperial era.

EDCS-15100436.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Fructuosa lived 24 years and 5 months; she is buried here. Manilius Messallinus placed this for his devoted daughter.
EDCS-15100436.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely on a tombstone, dedicated by Manilius Messallinus to his deceased daughter Fructuosa, who lived 24 years and 5 months, probably from the imperial period.

EDCS-15100439.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Plotia, a chaste and modest woman, who lived 54 years; she is buried here. Iulius Generosus placed this for his most rare wife.
EDCS-15100439.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Plotia, a chaste and modest woman who lived 54 years, erected by her husband Iulius Generosus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-15100440.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Aemilia Fortunata lived 43 years. Agrius Rufinus made this for his most dear mother; she is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Agrius Quartus lived 57 years. She placed this for her most dear husband; he is buried here.
EDCS-15100440.summary: This double funerary inscription records the deaths of Aemilia Fortunata, commemorated by her son Agrius Rufinus, and Marcus Agrius Quartus, commemorated by his wife, indicating a family burial plot from the imperial period.

EDCS-15100441.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Gabinius Gentilis lived 65 years, who while still living arranged this tomb for himself and his wife Ianuaria; he is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Pompeia Ianuaria lived 60 years; she is buried here. Gabinia Adauctula arranged for this to be inscribed for her excessively devoted parents.
EDCS-15100441.summary: This double funerary inscription records the lives and burial of Gaius Gabinius Gentilis and Pompeia Ianuaria, with Gabinius pre-arranging his own tomb and their daughter Gabinia Adauctula overseeing the inscription for her devoted parents, likely dating from the imperial period.

EDCS-15100443.translation: Lucius Calpurnius, son of Marcus, of the Tromentina tribe, from Aquae Statiellae, lived 35 years, served 10+ years as a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion; he lies buried here. His freedmen Philmusus, Eutychus, Amaranthus, [and his] heir.
EDCS-15100443.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Calpurnius, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, detailing his origin, age, and length of service, and listing his freedmen and heir, typical of a military tombstone from the Roman imperial era.

EDCS-15100444.translation: Publius Vettius, son of Publius, of the Velina tribe, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, a trumpeter; he is buried here.
EDCS-15100444.summary: This is a military funerary inscription for Publius Vettius, a trumpeter in the Third Augustan Legion, detailing his name, filiation, tribe, and military role, likely from a tombstone of the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-15100445.translation: Titus Pompeius Macrinus, son of Titus, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 85 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-15100445.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Pompeius Macrinus, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, recording his name, filiation, military status, age, and burial, likely from a tombstone of the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-15100446.translation: ]lia Amata, high priestess, lived 100 years; she is buried here. II Felix, III supervised.
EDCS-15100446.summary: This partial funerary inscription commemorates ]lia Amata, a high priestess who lived to 100 years, and includes references to individuals named Felix and another whose name is lost, who 'supervised' (suerunt), likely indicating the overseers of the monument, possibly from the imperial period.

EDCS-15100450.translation: Antonia Amia, pious, lived 90 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-15100450.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonia Amia, commemorating her age and piety.

EDCS-15300049.translation: Pompeia Kariota lived 21 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-15300049.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pompeia Kariota, noting her young age at death.

EDCS-15300051.translation: Berec Balyuri lived [---] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-15300051.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Berec Balyuri, indicating his burial place, with his age at death missing due to damage.

EDCS-15300052.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Caelius Tuscus, remarkable for the very advanced age he reached.

EDCS-15300053.translation: Gaius Calpurnius Caprarius lived 50 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-15300053.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Calpurnius Caprarius, recording his age and burial location.

EDCS-15300054.translation: Camuria Secunda lived 100 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-15300054.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Camuria Secunda, highlighting her exceptional longevity.

EDCS-15300055.translation: Lucius Carmeius Rogatus lived 12 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-15300055.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Carmeius Rogatus, commemorating a child who died at a young age.

EDCS-15300060.translation: Publius Eppius Felix lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-15300060.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Eppius Felix, noting his age at death and burial place.

EDCS-15300077.translation: Gaius Manilius Titulus lived 90 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-15300077.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Manilius Titulus, celebrating his long life.

EDCS-15300082.translation: Sextus Munniatius Communus, son of Primigenius, lived [---] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-15300082.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Munniatius Communus, identifying him by his father and marking his burial spot, with his exact age at death partially lost.

EDCS-15300084.translation: Munniatia Satura, daughter of Sextus, lived 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-15300084.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Munniatia Satura, placed by an unmentioned dedicant, likely a family member, and dates to an unknown period.

EDCS-15300090.translation: Cubelia Prima, daughter of Gaius, lived 20 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-15300090.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cubelia Prima, placed by an unmentioned dedicant, likely a family member, and dates to an unknown period.

EDCS-15300160.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano optimo Augusto Germanico Parthico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XX imperatori XII consuli Vl  Caecilius Faustinus proconsul dedicavit decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-15300160.translation: To the Imperator Caesar, son of the divine Nerva, Nerva Traianus Optimus Augustus Germanicus Parthicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 20th time, imperator for the 12th time, consul for the 6th time. Caecilius Faustinus, proconsul, dedicated this by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-15300160.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument, possibly a statue or building, to Emperor Trajan by the proconsul Caecilius Faustinus, funded publicly by decree of the decurions, likely around 116-117 CE given Trajan's titles.

EDCS-15400826.translation: Iulius Ingenus died in Gaul. His wife Virula commits his body to the embrace of his homeland. He served in Germania as a speculator and cornicularius of the legion. The beginning of the vine was the end of his life.
EDCS-15400826.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Ingenus, a former soldier who died in Gaul, dedicated by his wife Virula, and makes a poetic reference to his military career and death, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15600892.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Gadaeus Felicis filius pius vixit annis LXVI hic situs est Secunda Secundi filia vixit annos  Saturio et Gadaeus patri piissimo posuerunt
EDCS-15600892.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gadaeus, son of Felix, a dutiful man, lived 66 years. He is buried here. Secunda, daughter of Secundus, lived [number] years. Saturio and Gadaeus placed this for their most dutiful father.
EDCS-15600892.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gadaeus, son of Felix, placed by his sons Saturio and Gadaeus, and also mentions a Secunda, whose relationship is unclear, likely dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-15600922.text_edition: Caio Vettio Grato Attico Sabiniano clarissimo puero filio Cai Vetti Grati Sabiniani clarissimi viri tribuni militum legionis VII Claudiae quaestoris kandidati nepotis sic Cai Vetti Sabiniani Hospitis clarissimae memoriae viri proconsulis municipium Aurelium Cpatrono decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-15600922.translation: To Gaius Vettius Gratus Atticus Sabinianus, a most distinguished boy, son of Gaius Vettius Gratus Sabinianus, a most distinguished man, military tribune of the VII Claudia legion, quaestor candidatus, grandson of Gaius Vettius Sabinianus Hospes, a man of most distinguished memory, proconsul. The municipium Aurelium [---] as patron, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-15600922.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument, likely a statue, to Gaius Vettius Gratus Atticus Sabinianus, a distinguished youth from an important family, by the municipium Aurelium [---], funded publicly by decree of the decurions, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15600925.translation: To Quintus Considius Namphamo's son Repostus Areianus, aedile, duumvir, for his exceptional administration as duumvir and singular love towards the citizens, (dedicated by) the entire curia. Dedicated on the 3rd or 4th Ides of March, with Agricola and Clementianus as consuls.
EDCS-15600925.summary: This is a public honorific inscription for Quintus Considius Repostus Areianus, recognizing his excellent service as a duumvir and love for citizens, dedicated by the entire curia and funded publicly, precisely dated to March 12th or 13th in 230 CE (when Agricola and Clementianus were consuls).

EDCS-15600970.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aebutius Fortunatus, a dutiful man, lived 70 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-15600972.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Aelius Felix, a dutiful man, lived 81 years. He is buried here. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulia Celsina, a dutiful woman, lived 71 years. She is buried here. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Aelius Fortunatus, a dutiful man, lived 52 years. He is buried here. Sacred to the Divine Manes. [---]A lived [---] months. He is buried here. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Aelius Laresis, a dutiful man, lived 69 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-15600972.summary: This inscription likely commemorates multiple family members: Lucius Aelius Felix, Iulia Celsina, Marcus Aelius Fortunatus, an unnamed individual, and Marcus Aelius Laresis, suggesting a shared monument or burial plot, dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-15900341.text_edition: In hok sic tumulo iaket sic korpus bonae memoriae RV pectore carus qui per gladium indomitem sic okisum sic bixit sic in pake sic plus minus annos XXV rekessit sic de hok sic saekulo sic sub die VI mensis Dekember sic indictione XIII amen
EDCS-15900341.translation: In this tomb lies this body of good memory, dear to the heart, who was slain by the untamed sword. He lived in peace for more or less 25 years. He departed from this world on the 6th day of the month of December, in the 13th indiction. Amen.
EDCS-15900341.summary: This is a late Roman or early medieval funerary inscription commemorating an unnamed individual, beloved by a relative, who died by the sword at approximately 25 years old, and records the exact date of death in terms of the indiction cycle.

EDCS-16100561.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Crispina, daughter of Felicissima, lived 50 years. She lies here.
EDCS-16100561.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Crispina, possibly the daughter of Felicissima, stating she lived for 50 years and is buried here.

EDCS-16200840.text_edition: Ἀγαθῇ τύχῃ τῶν κυρίων Ἀσσκληπιάδης θεῷ Ἀσσκληπιῷ εὐχαριστήριον  Pro  victoria  dominorum  nostrorum  Aretes causa dio sic Aesculapio Asclepiades Asclepiadis filius marmoArarius#marmorarius#MARMARARI Nicomediensis
EDCS-16200840.translation: For good fortune of our lords. Asclepiades to the god Asclepius as a thank offering. For the victory of our lords, on behalf of Aretes, thus to the god Asclepius, Asclepiades son of Asclepiades, a marble-worker from Nicomedia.
EDCS-16200840.summary: This is a bilingual (Greek and Latin) dedicatory inscription by Asclepiades, a marble-worker from Nicomedia, to the god Asclepius, offered in gratitude for the victory of the emperors and on behalf of Aretes, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201257.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Marius Clodius, dutiful, who lived 51 years. He is laid here.

EDCS-16201260.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Mattius Pullaenus Bellicus, dutiful, lived 55 years. He is laid here.

EDCS-16201261.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Lucceius Gududianus, dutiful, lived 12 years. He is laid here. // Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Augustalis, son of Lucceius, dutiful, lived 7 years. He is laid here. // Sacred to the Divine Shades. Furia Faustina, dutiful, lived 85 years. She is laid here. // Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Lucceius Fortunatus, dutiful, lived 87 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-16201261.summary: This is a series of four funerary inscriptions, likely from a family tomb or multiple graves in close proximity, for Lucius Lucceius Gududianus, Lucius Augustalis, Furia Faustina, and Lucius Lucceius Fortunatus, ranging in age from 7 to 87 years.

EDCS-16201262.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Iulius Messor, dutiful grandson, lived 2 years. He is laid here. // Sacred to the Divine Shades. Tonneia Venusta, dutiful, lived 65 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-16201262.summary: This is a pair of funerary inscriptions, likely from a shared monument, for Lucius Iulius Messor, a young grandson who lived 2 years, and Tonneia Venusta, a woman who lived 65 years.

EDCS-16201264.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [---] Labennius Faustinus, pious, lived 80 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-16201264.summary: This is a funerary inscription for [---] Labennius Faustinus, who lived to be 80 years old.

EDCS-16201273.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Iulius Aratorianus, pious, lived 26 years. He is laid here.

EDCS-16201276.publication: CIL 08, 26933 = Dougga 00113 = ILTun 01465 = CLE 01873 = MAD 00542 = MAD 00631 = CLEAfrique 00071 = AntAfr-1973-133
EDCS-16201276.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Iulius Castus, pious, lived 75 years. As a father, I prepared the ground and established it for my family, having been active for seventy-five years. I have left behind a son to always honor the household god. He is laid here. // Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Namphamina, pious, lived 61 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-16201276.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, with the first part dedicated by Quintus Iulius Castus, who lived 75 years and explicitly states he prepared his own burial site, and the second part dedicated to Iulia Namphamina, who lived 61 years.

EDCS-16201281.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Nartialus also made this for Iulius Felix, his best father and genitor, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-16201282.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. The same Nartialis made this for Iulia Prima, his best mother and genitrix, who lived [---]25 years.

EDCS-16201360.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two young brothers, Lucius Magnius Minnus Fortunatianus and Lucius Magnius Ingenuus Marcianus, who died in childhood.

EDCS-16201389.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Minicius Salvidenus, son of Primus, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-16201391.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Modius Firmus, a dutiful man, lived for 62 years. He lies here.

EDCS-16201398.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Marius Candidus, a dutiful man, lived for 80 years. He lies here.

EDCS-16201859.text_edition: Minervae Victoriae Augustae sacrum pro salute Auggg(ustorum) Imppp(eratorum) Severi et Antonini et Getae et Iuliae Domnae Augustae coloni Gamoniensis sua pecunia fecerunt
EDCS-16201859.translation: Sacred to Minerva Victoria Augusta. For the well-being of the Augusti, the Emperors Severus and Antoninus and Geta, and Julia Domna Augusta, the colonists of Gamon did this at their own expense.
EDCS-16201859.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by the colonists of Gamon to Minerva Victoria Augusta, for the health of Emperors Severus, Caracalla, Geta, and Empress Julia Domna, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16201867.text_edition: Hygiae Augustae sacrum pro salute et victoriis Impperatorum Caessarum Luci Septimii Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti et Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et Luci Septimii Getae nobilissimi Caesaris et Iuliae Domnae Augustae matris Aug(ustorum) et castrorum totiusque divinae domus eorum Lucius Baebius Secundus et Iulia Victorina uxor eius votum solverunt
EDCS-16201867.translation: Sacred to Hygia Augusta. For the well-being and victories of the Emperors Caesars Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and Lucius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar, and Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the Augusti and of the camps, and of their entire divine house, Lucius Baebius Secundus and Julia Victorina his wife fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-16201867.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hygia Augusta by Lucius Baebius Secundus and Julia Victorina for the health and victories of the Severan imperial family (Septimius Severus, Caracalla, Geta, and Julia Domna), dating to between AD 198-209.

EDCS-16201878.text_edition: Publio Attio Deciano Felici Matutino clarissimo puero civi et patrono
EDCS-16201878.translation: To Publius Attius Decianus Felix Matutinus, most distinguished boy, citizen and patron.
EDCS-16201878.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Publius Attius Decianus Felix Matutinus, a prominent young citizen and patron, possibly erected by a community or a grateful individual.

EDCS-16201883.text_edition: Splendonii Lucio Clodio Luci filio Quirina Titiano qui in IANI et IIviratu admodum floret  IIOR cultoribusque ium ediderunt
EDCS-16201883.translation: The Splendonii to Lucius Clodius Titianus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, who greatly flourishes in the office of IANI and as duumvir, and to the worshipers [---] they dedicated this.
EDCS-16201883.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from the Splendonii to Lucius Clodius Titianus, honoring his public offices as a duumvir, and his devotion to his cult, likely made by a group of worshipers.

EDCS-16201905.text_edition: Pro salute Augusti Quintus Bennius Felix Palus Victor filius Aesculapio vota reddiderunt
EDCS-16201905.translation: For the well-being of the Augustus, Quintus Bennius Felix Palus Victor and his son rendered vows to Aesculapius.
EDCS-16201905.summary: This is a votive inscription to Aesculapius, offered by Quintus Bennius Felix Palus Victor and his son for the health of the emperor.

EDCS-16201908.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Peticius Ianuarius vixit annis LXVII et Peticia Procula uxor eius vixit annis Publius Peticius Martialis filius parentibus praestantissimis fecit et dedicavit ex officina Cittini
EDCS-16201908.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Peticius Ianuarius lived for 67 years, and Peticia Procula, his wife, lived for [---] years. Publius Peticius Martialis, their son, made and dedicated this to his most excellent parents, from the workshop of Cittinus.
EDCS-16201908.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Publius Peticius Martialis for his parents, Publius Peticius Ianuarius and Peticia Procula, indicating that it was carved in the workshop of Cittinus.

EDCS-16201909.text_edition: Quirino deo Patri Genio municipii Augusto sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate  consulis  patris patriae proconsulis et Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae matris Augusti et castrorum et senatus et patriae totiusque domus divinae Lucius Turius Martialis et Quintus Cornelius Felix Emeritus aediles inlatis rei publicae summis honorariis aedilitatis suae amplius statuam aeream ex SS VIIIImilibus nummum et basim patriae dono dederunt et dedicaverunt ob quam dedicationem ludos scaenicos sic ediderunt et gymnasium biduo dederunt
EDCS-16201909.translation: Sacred to Quirinus, god the Father, the Genius of the municipality, Augustus. For the well-being of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, in tribunician power [---], consul [---], Father of the Country, proconsul, and of Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of the Augustus and of the camps and of the Senate and of the Country and of the entire divine house, Lucius Turius Martialis and Quintus Cornelius Felix Emeritus, aediles, having paid the considerable honoraria of their aedileship to the republic, additionally gave and dedicated a bronze statue worth eight thousand sesterces and its base as a gift to the country. For this dedication, they presented theatrical games and provided a gymnasium for two days.
EDCS-16201909.summary: This is a civic dedication by aediles Lucius Turius Martialis and Quintus Cornelius Felix Emeritus to Quirinus, the Genius of the municipality, and the imperial family (Severus Alexander and Julia Mamaea), marking their donation of a bronze statue and its base, and their presentation of theatrical games and a gymnasium, dating to the reign of Severus Alexander (AD 222-235).

EDCS-16201910.text_edition: Bonifatius  Ianuarius  Montius  Eurialus  Candurius  Victor  Eulogius  Anfio  Ninficus  Fortunio  Preiectus  Anastasus  Amor  Nymficus
EDCS-16201910.translation: Bonifatius, Ianuarius, Montius, Eurialus, Candurius, Victor, Eulogius, Anfio, Ninficus, Fortunio, Preiectus, Anastasus, Amor, Nymficus.
EDCS-16201910.summary: This inscription is a simple list of fourteen names, likely recording members of a group or a community, possibly from the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-16201927.text_edition: Caelesti sacrum Decimus Valerius Phoenix libens animo votum solvit
EDCS-16201927.translation: Sacred to Caelestis. Decimus Valerius Phoenix willingly and with a grateful heart fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16201927.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Caelestis by Decimus Valerius Phoenix, indicating gratitude and the fulfillment of a religious promise.

EDCS-16201951.text_edition: Divo Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci patri Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Parthici Arabici maximi Britannici maximi pontificis maximi patris patriae ordo Mustitanus Pacatori deo decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-16201951.translation: To the deified Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax, father of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Parthian, greatest Britannic, greatest Arab, pontifex maximus, father of his country; the Mustitanian order [dedicated this] to the deified Pacator, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-16201951.summary: This inscription is an official dedication by the city council of Musti to the deified Emperor Septimius Severus and the local deity Pacator, commissioned by the decurions using public funds, likely erected during the reign of Caracalla (early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-16201959.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris divi Severi Pii nepotis divi Magni Antonini Pii filii Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate VIIII consulis III patris patriae proconsulis Caius Timuleius Honoratus Felix Petronianus eques Romanus  in urbe Roma Imperatoris domini nostri Alexandri Augusti sacerdos bidentalis iussus fecit
EDCS-16201959.translation: For the health of the Emperor Caesar, grandson of the deified Severus Pius, son of the deified Great Antoninus Pius, Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, consul for the third time, father of his country, proconsul; Gaius Timuleius Honoratus Felix Petronianus, Roman eques, bidental priest in the city of Rome of our lord Emperor Alexander Augustus, made this by command.
EDCS-16201959.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the well-being of Emperor Severus Alexander, commissioned by Gaius Timuleius Honoratus Felix Petronianus, a Roman eques and priest, likely dating to 230 AD based on the imperial titles.

EDCS-16300643.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Nahania Saturnina, daughter of Ianuarius, a dutiful person, lived for 80 [or 80+] years. She is laid to rest here.
EDCS-16300643.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nahania Saturnina, daughter of Ianuarius, who died at an advanced age, indicating a personal memorial.

EDCS-16300644.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Nahania Saturnina, daughter of Honoratus, a dutiful person, lived for 67 years. She is laid to rest here.
EDCS-16300644.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nahania Saturnina, daughter of Honoratus, who lived to be 67, marking her burial site.

EDCS-16300645.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Nampame, daughter of Diophantus, a dutiful person, lived for 41 years. She is laid to rest here.

EDCS-16300646.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Octavia Victoria, daughter of Zabullus, a dutiful person, lived for 21 years. She is laid to rest here.
EDCS-16300646.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Octavia Victoria, daughter of Zabullus, who passed away at the young age of 21, marking her grave.

EDCS-16300648.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Pacuvius Saturus, a dutiful person, lived for 85 years. He is laid to rest here.
EDCS-16300648.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Pacuvius Saturus, who lived to the venerable age of 85, indicating his burial site.

EDCS-16300649.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aulus Pompeius Salvius, a veteran, a dutiful person, lived for 65 years. He is laid to rest here.
EDCS-16300649.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Pompeius Salvius, a veteran who lived for 65 years, commemorating his burial.

EDCS-16300650.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Nahanius Saturninus, son of Saturninus, pious, lived 52 years. He lies here.

EDCS-16300651.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Numisius Honoratus, son of Marcus, of the Arnensian tribe, pious, lived 81 years. Buried in his own ground.
EDCS-16300651.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Numisius Honoratus, indicating he was buried on his own property, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16300667.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Mucius Agrasius, pious, lived 60 years. He lies here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Remmia Fructuosa, pious, lived 63 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Mucia Veneria, pious, lived 13 years, 6 months, 11 days.
EDCS-16300667.summary: This inscription is a triple funerary monument for Quintus Mucius Agrasius, Remmia Fructuosa, and Mucia Veneria, likely family members, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16300673.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Nahanius Martialis, pious, lived 110 years. He lies here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Totononia Rosula, pious, lived 85 years. She lies here.
EDCS-16300673.summary: This is a double funerary inscription for Quintus Nahanius Martialis and Totononia Rosula, likely a couple or family members, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16300674.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Namgedde, daughter of Adiu[tor] Menae, pious, lived 14 years. She lies here.
EDCS-16300674.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Namgedde, who died at the age of 14, likely set up by her family, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16300679.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Nanneia Sperata, pious, lived 30 years. She lies here.

EDCS-16300733.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Pomponia Victoria, daughter of Marcus, a univiria, pious, lived 27 years. May your bones rest well; may the earth be light to you. She lies here.
EDCS-16300733.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pomponia Victoria, daughter of Marcus, a univiria (a woman married only once), who lived 27 years.

EDCS-16300753.summary: This is a funerary monument recording the burials of two individuals, Remmia Fructa and Gaius Remmius Floridus, likely a husband and wife or other relatives.

EDCS-16300768.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sessia Honorata, pious, lived 105 years. She lies here. // Sacred to the Divine Shades. Napotia Mamonica, pious, lived 27 years. She lies here.

EDCS-16400156.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Servilius Faustus, pious, lived 67 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-16400156.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Servilius Faustus, probably from the Roman Imperial era.

EDCS-16400158.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Sallustius Adiutor, pious, lived 93 years. He is laid here.

EDCS-16400159.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sedia Accepta, daughter of Gaius, pious, lived 54 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-16400159.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sedia Accepta, daughter of Gaius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16400164.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [---]nia [---]rnina, pious, lived 100 years.
EDCS-16400164.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to a woman named [---]nia [---]rnina, who lived to the age of 100 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-16400182.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Saturninus Cabdolonis, pious, lived 71 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-16400182.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Saturninus Cabdolonis, likely from the Roman Imperial era.

EDCS-16400211.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aulus Terentius Felix, of the Quirina tribe, an excellent man, pious, lived 64 years. He is laid here. May your bones rest well; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-16400211.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Terentius Felix, a man of the Quirina tribe, including a common valedictory wish for the deceased, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-16400213.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Termasius Pr[---]
EDCS-16400213.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual named Termasius Pr[---], likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16400475.text_edition: Caius Hil Felix sacerdos Saturni cultor dei exegit V Kalendas Novembres Sabino et Anullino consulibus per Baricem magg(istrum)
EDCS-16400475.translation: Gaius Hil Felix, priest of Saturn, worshiper of the god, carried out [this] on the fifth day before the Kalends of November, in the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus, through Barices the master.
EDCS-16400475.summary: This inscription records an act or dedication performed by Gaius Hil Felix, a priest and worshiper of Saturn, on October 28th, 269 AD (the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus), through a master named Barices.

EDCS-16600017.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Venusta Datti, daughter of Taege, pious, lived 67 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-16600017.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Venusta Datti, daughter of Taege, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16600021.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Veratia Spicula, pious, lived 28 years, 4 months, 24 days. She is laid here.
EDCS-16600021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Veratia Spicula, recording her age and place of burial.

EDCS-16600027.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Vibius Festus, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, pious, lived 32 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-16600027.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Vibius Festus, noting his lineage, tribe, age, and place of burial.

EDCS-16600046.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aulus Vitellius Felix Victorianus, pious, lived 96 years. He is laid here.

EDCS-16800127.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcia Zabulllica Sabidia, pious, lived [---] years. She is laid here. Saturninus Sergianus, pious, lived 58 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-16800127.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating two individuals, Marcia Zabulllica Sabidia and Saturninus Sergianus, noting their piety, ages, and places of burial.

EDCS-16800129.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Tannonius Felix, pious, lived 11 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-16800129.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tannonius Felix, recording his young age and place of burial.

EDCS-16800148.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Magnius Primus, pious, lived 56 years.
EDCS-16800148.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Magnius Primus, recording his piety and age, though the place of burial is not explicitly stated.

EDCS-16800150.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Magnia Eutycetis, pious, lived 41 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-16800150.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Magnia Eutycetis, recording her piety, age, and place of burial.

EDCS-16800155.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Papirius Sarmato lived 70 years.
EDCS-16800155.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Papirius Sarmato, recording his age.

EDCS-16800167.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Saufeia Victoria, pious, lived 75 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-16800167.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saufeia Victoria, noting her piety, age, and place of burial.

EDCS-16800187.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Saturnina, pious, lived 56 years, 10 months.
EDCS-16800187.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saturnina, recording her piety and precise age at death.

EDCS-16800189.translation: To the Sacred Shades. Lucius Morasius, son of Aevidius, lived piously for 51 years.
EDCS-16800189.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Morasius, erected by an unspecified relative, indicating he lived for 51 years.

EDCS-17700377.text_edition: Hymetii Caio Iulio Regino decurioni Karthaginis aedili IIviro quinquennalicio gentis Severianae Alexandrianae Augustae flamini flamini perpetuo curatori splendidissimae reipublicae Thimidensium Regiorum ordo decurionum ex sportulis suis ob merita dono dedit
EDCS-17700377.translation: To Hymetius Caius Iulius Reginus, decurion of Carthage, aedile, duumvir with quinquennial power, of the Severian Alexandrian Augustan gens, flamen, perpetual flamen, curator of the most splendid republic of the Thimidensian Regii: the order of decurions, from their own contributions, gave this gift in recognition of his merits.
EDCS-17700377.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the order of decurions to Hymetius Caius Iulius Reginus, honoring his extensive public and religious career in Carthage and Thimida Regia.

EDCS-17700378.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Princeps Iuventutis, with tribunician power, imperator for the second time, son of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of divine Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of divine Hadrian, and great-great-great-grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus and divine Nerva. Gnaeus Cal[...].
EDCS-17700378.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus), detailing his imperial titles and prestigious imperial ancestry, erected by Gnaeus Cal[...].

EDCS-17700379.translation: To Julia Domna Augusta, Mother of the Camps, Mother of the Emperors, wife of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus. Quintus Silicius Victor and Gaius Tadius Fortunatus, for the honor of their perpetual flaminate, erected a statue with a base from 2,000 sesterces, with a third added legitimately by decree of the pagans of the Pagus Mercurialis of the Medelitan veterans, at their own expense, and dedicated it themselves.
EDCS-17700379.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to Empress Julia Domna, erected by Quintus Silicius Victor and Gaius Tadius Fortunatus, veterans of the Pagus Mercurialis, to honor their perpetual flaminate, circa the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-17700408.publication: CIL 08, 00914 (p 2337) = CIL 08, 11184 = ILTun 00253 = IIulian 00155 = LBIRNA 00706 = Epigraphica-2016-62
EDCS-17700408.translation: Public vows of the Biienses. Administered by Quintus Mycius Endulcentius, perpetual flamen, curator of the republic of the Biienses. A temple, begun many centuries ago, started from its foundations by two individuals in the year of the duumvirate of Gaius Aurelius Galosus and Marcus Favonius Macdo, was completed in the year of the duumvirate of [---] Splendonius Restutianus and Publius Aurelius Roburus Pr[---], partly with public money and partly from the offering of the decurions. [---] Victorinus, vir clarissimus, consular of the province of Valeria Byzacena, dedicated it, with Arrius Secundianus overseeing. By the sacred indulgence of our lords, the greatest Augusti Constantius and Constans.
EDCS-17700408.summary: This inscription details the completion and dedication of a temple in Biia, originally begun centuries prior, under the administration of Quintus Mycius Endulcentius, funded by public money and decurion contributions, and dedicated by Victorinus, a provincial governor, with imperial indulgence from Constantius and Constans, likely in the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-17700470.translation: During the consulship of Gaius Caesar Imperator for the fourth time, Lucius Pomponius Malchus, freedman of Lucius, duumvir for the fifth time, oversaw the construction of the entire town wall from squared stone.
EDCS-17700470.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Pomponius Malchus, a freedman and five-time duumvir, oversaw the construction of the entire town wall, during Julius Caesar's fourth consulship, in 44 BCE.

EDCS-17700471.translation: During the consulship of Marcus Appuleius and Publius Silius, and with Gnaeus Domitius Malchion as quinquennial duumvir, Lucius Sertorius Alexander and Lucius Vitruvius Alexander, aediles, provided a continuous parapet, two schools, likewise a sundial, and oversaw the paving of a road.
EDCS-17700471.summary: This inscription details the public works undertaken by aediles Lucius Sertorius Alexander and Lucius Vitruvius Alexander, including a parapet, two schools, a sundial, and a paved road, during the consulship of Marcus Appuleius and Publius Silius in 20 BCE.

EDCS-17700849.translation: To the Sacred Shades. [---] Rufrius Adiutor, son of Decimus, of the Oufentina tribe, from Mediolanum, a soldier of the 13th Urban Cohort, in the century of Publilius, served for 15 years. He lived for 40 years. He lies here. Erected with the care of Titus Nepius Velox, his heir.
EDCS-17700849.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rufrius Adiutor, a soldier from Mediolanum who served in the 13th Urban Cohort for 15 years and lived to be 40, erected by his heir, Titus Nepius Velox.

EDCS-17700850.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Vilanius Quinti filius Voltinia Nepos Philippis |(centurio) cohortis XIII urbanae donis donatus a Domitiano ob bellum Dacicum item ab eodem ob bellum Germanicum item torquibus armillis ob bellum Dacicum vixit nnos L militavit annos XXXII Marcus Silius Quintianus optio bene merenti posuit
EDCS-17700850.translation: To the Sacred Shades. Quintus Vilanius Nepos, son of Quintus, of the Voltinia tribe, from Philippi, centurion of the 13th Urban Cohort, honored with gifts by Domitian for the Dacian War, likewise by the same for the Germanic War, also with torques and armillae for the Dacian War. He lived for 50 years, served for 32 years. Marcus Silius Quintianus, optio, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-17700850.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Vilanius Nepos, a centurion of the 13th Urban Cohort, highly decorated by Emperor Domitian for his service in the Dacian and Germanic Wars, erected by Marcus Silius Quintianus, an optio, in the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-17700851.translation: To the Sacred Shades. And Antigona will live and feast. Vitalis, our Augustus' messenger, will live and feast. While I am Vitalis and I live, I made this tomb, and while I pass, I read my own verses, and I toured the entire pedestrian region with my diploma, and I caught hares with dogs and finally foxes. Afterwards, I gladly drank many potions from cups. I did many youthful deeds because I am destined to die. Whoever is wise, young man, while you live, build your own tomb.
EDCS-17700851.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vitalis, a messenger of the emperor (Augusti n. tabellarius), who built his own tomb while alive and inscribed it with a poetic and philosophical reflection on life, death, and youth, dating from an unspecified imperial period.

EDCS-17700854.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Rufina lived 43 years.
EDCS-17700854.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Rufina, who lived to be 43 years old, dedicated to the Manes (Spirits of the Departed).

EDCS-17700855.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. To Lucius Antonius Martialis, a most devoted brother.
EDCS-17700855.summary: This is a funerary dedication made to Lucius Antonius Martialis, described as a most devoted brother, dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed.

EDCS-17700857.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Aufdius Maximus, devoted, lived 7[3] years. May the earth be light for you; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-17700857.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Aufdius Maximus, who lived 70-79 years, including common funerary wishes for the deceased.

EDCS-17700859.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Caecilius Paulinus, son of Marcus, of the Arnensian tribe, devoted, lived 23 years. He is laid to rest here.
EDCS-17700859.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Caecilius Paulinus, a 23-year-old devoted man from the Arnensian tribe, stating he is buried at this spot.

EDCS-17700860.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. [---] Caecilius Saturninus Commorianus, devoted, lived 75 years. He is laid to rest here. May your bones rest well; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-17700860.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilius Saturninus Commorianus, who lived to be 75 years old, including wishes for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-17700861.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caelia Prima lived 40 years. She is laid to rest here.
EDCS-17700861.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caelia Prima, who lived 40 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-17700863.translation: Caesetia Ingenua, daughter of Marcus. This monument is for Caesetia Ingenua, daughter of Marcus, who is also buried here. She lived 13 years, and her bones are in the altar of the monument, within the enclosure. The rest [is outside], where she lies. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-17700863.summary: This is a funerary monument for Caesetia Ingenua, a 13-year-old girl, specifying that her bones are within an altar in the monument, which is itself inside an enclosure.

EDCS-17700864.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. To Caria Galla, daughter of Quintus. She lived 7 years, 10 months, 12 days.
EDCS-17700864.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caria Galla, a young girl who lived just over seven years, dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed.

EDCS-17700865.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. [---]dia Cerealis, devoted. She lived 17 years.
EDCS-17700865.summary: This is a funerary inscription for [---]dia Cerealis, a devoted individual who lived 17 years, dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed.

EDCS-17700867.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Concordia Quieta, devoted, lived 22 years. She is laid to rest here. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-17700867.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Concordia Quieta, who lived 22 years, stating that she is buried here and includes a wish for her bones to rest well.

EDCS-17700868.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Daphnis Candida, pious, lived for 35 years; she lies here. Nicetes made this for his most pious companion.
EDCS-17700868.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Daphnis Candida, erected by her companion Nicetes.

EDCS-17700869.translation: To the Divine Manes. [---]s Felicio lived for [---] years.
EDCS-17700869.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a funerary dedication to Felicio.

EDCS-17700873.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulia Durmiana lived for 21 years; she lies here.
EDCS-17700873.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Durmiana, indicating her age at death and place of burial.

EDCS-17700874.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Licinius Crescens, pious, lived for 35 years.
EDCS-17700874.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinius Crescens, stating his age and piety.

EDCS-17700875.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Mania Certula, pious, lived for 30 years; she lies here.
EDCS-17700875.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mania Certula, noting her age and place of burial.

EDCS-17700876.translation: Maximilla, daughter of Bassus, pious, lived for 19 years; she lies here.
EDCS-17700876.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maximilla, identifying her parentage, age, and place of burial.

EDCS-17700877.translation: Lucius Otavius Epaphroditus, freedman of Lucius Ligur, made this for Octavia Optata, his freedwoman, and for himself while still alive.
EDCS-17700877.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Lucius Otavius Epaphroditus for his freedwoman Octavia Optata and for his own future burial.

EDCS-17700878.translation: [---] Octavius Primus, innocent, lived for 20 years and 7 months.
EDCS-17700878.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Octavius Primus, noting his innocence and short life.

EDCS-17700879.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Primigenius, pious, lived for 22 years; he lies here.
EDCS-17700879.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Primigenius, indicating his age at death and place of burial.

EDCS-17700882.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Rufus Bamseaus lived for 60 years; he lies here. // Sacred to the Divine Manes. Secunda, daughter of Rogatianus, lived for 70 years; she lies here.
EDCS-17700882.summary: This is a funerary monument with two separate epitaphs, one for Rufus Bamseaus and another for Secunda, daughter of Rogatianus, both indicating their age and place of burial.

EDCS-17700886.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Saturninus lived 50 years. He lies here.
EDCS-17700886.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Lucius Saturninus at the age of 50, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-17700887.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Saturninus...
EDCS-17700887.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Saturninus, typical of Roman tomb markers.

EDCS-17700889.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Saturninus, the dutiful son of Rufus Faustus, lived 65 years. He lies here. May your bones rest well; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-17700889.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saturninus, son of Rufus Faustus, who lived 65 years, and includes a common valediction for the deceased.

EDCS-17700890.translation: Marcus Silius Maximus made this tomb for his dearest wife. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Vibia Saturnina lived 33 years, two months, and 16 days.
EDCS-17700890.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Marcus Silius Maximus for his beloved wife, Vibia Saturnina, detailing her age at death and the dedicant.

EDCS-17700891.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sulvia Castula lived...
EDCS-17700891.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Sulvia Castula, likely part of a tombstone.

EDCS-17700892.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Sufenas Felix, the dutiful, lived for [N] years and 10 days. He lies here. May your bones rest well. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-17700892.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Sufenas Felix, stating his age at death and including a dual valediction for the deceased, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-17700894.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Volussienus Pacatus Saturianus, son of Proculus, lived 2 years, 4 months. So may whatever you wish come to pass for you, provided you do not violate this sacred monument.
EDCS-17700894.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Volussienus Pacatus Saturianus, a child who lived 2 years and 4 months, and includes a curse or warning against violating the tomb.

EDCS-17700902.translation: ...[He] lies here, dutiful. He lived 15 years. He lies here.
EDCS-17700902.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual who lived 15 years, with the common phrase 'he lies here'.

EDCS-17700915.translation: Victorinus rests in peace and tranquility.
EDCS-17700915.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Victorinus, expressing hopes for his peaceful rest, likely dating to the later Imperial period due to the phrasing.

EDCS-17701040.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pertinaci Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli designato II patri patriae civitas Ucres pecunia publica fecit et dedicavit anno Corneli Anullini proconsulis clarissimi viri et Valeri Festi legati eius
EDCS-17701040.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, greatest pontiff, holding tribunician power, consul designate for the second time, father of his country, the city of Ucres made and dedicated this with public money in the year of Cornelius Anullinus, the most distinguished proconsul, and Valerius Festus, his legate.
EDCS-17701040.summary: This is a public dedication, likely for a statue or monument, erected by the city of Ucres with public funds to honor Emperor Septimius Severus, specifying the dating by the proconsul and his legate (around 199/200 AD).

EDCS-17701044.translation: To Sextus Caecilius Crescentius Volusianus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina voting tribe, prefect of engineers, priest, curio for performing sacred rites, advocate of the imperial treasury in Rome, procurator of the 20th inheritance tax, in charge of imperial letters from the divine Antoninus, in charge of imperial letters from the Augusti, patron of the municipality; by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-17701044.summary: This inscription records a public dedication to Sextus Caecilius Crescentius Volusianus, detailing his distinguished civic and imperial career, likely erected by a municipality in the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-17701050.text_edition: Lucius Domitius Ahenobarbus pro consule trimodium posuit qua civitates salem emetirentur
EDCS-17701050.translation: Lucius Domitius Ahenobarbus, as proconsul, set up a three-modius measure, by which cities might measure out salt.
EDCS-17701050.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Domitius Ahenobarbus, acting as proconsul, established a standard three-modius measure for the distribution of salt to cities, likely during the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-17701090.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filio divi Commodi fratri divi Antonini Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiani Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae adnepoti Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVII imperatori XII consuli III patri patriae et Imperatori Caesari Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filio divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto tribunicia potestate XI imperatori II consuli III et Publio Septimio Severo Getae [nobilissimo Caesari principi iuventutis et Iuliae Domnae Augustae] matri Auggustorum et castrorum colonia Septimia Vaga nomini et auspiciis divinis eorum inlustrata per Titum Flavium Decimum proconsulem clarissimum virum colonia deducta arcum fecit et numini Augusti eorum dicavit
EDCS-17701090.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, son of the divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the divine Commodus, grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 17th time, acclaimed Imperator for the 12th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of his country; and to Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, holding tribunician power for the 11th time, acclaimed Imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 3rd time; and to Publius Septimius Severus Geta, most noble Caesar, prince of youth; and to Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the Augusti and of the camps: the colony of Septimia Vaga, illuminated by their divine name and auspices through Titus Flavius Decimus, the proconsul, a most distinguished man, having been established as a colony, built this arch and dedicated it to their divine majesty.
EDCS-17701090.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an arch by the newly founded colony of Septimia Vaga to the Imperial family of Septimius Severus, Caracalla, Geta, and Julia Domna, celebrating their divine protection and the establishment of the colony around 209 AD.

EDCS-17701124.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Capras lived 10 years.
EDCS-17701124.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual named Capras, who died at the age of 10, indicating a child's burial, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-17701277.publication: CIL 08, 01406 = CIL 08, 14906 = D 06795 = Thignica 00004 = LBIRNA 00501 = Epigraphica-2020-437
EDCS-17701277.translation: Under Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, son of the great Antoninus Pius, grandson of the divine Severus Pius, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 8th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of his country, and under Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of the Augusti and of the camps and of the senate and of the fatherland: the municipality of Septimium Aurelium Antoninianum Alexandrianum Herculeum Frugiferum Thignica, devoted to their divinity and majesty, rebuilt and dedicated from its foundations, with public money, the macellum (market) which had collapsed due to old age.
EDCS-17701277.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding and dedication of a market (macellum) in the municipality of Thignica by the local government, using public funds, during the reign of Emperor Severus Alexander and his mother Julia Mamaea, around 229 AD.

EDCS-17701278.translation: For the safety of our Lords Diocletian and Maximian, Augusti, and Constantius and Maximian, most noble Caesars, Lucius [...]ius Caecilianus [...] having killed a bull, performed a taurobolium.
EDCS-17701278.summary: This inscription records a taurobolium ritual performed by Lucius Caecilianus for the health and safety of the Tetrarchs Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Maximian, indicating a religious dedication in the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-17900382.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, Minerva Augusta, for the safety of the Emperors Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus and Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus Armeniacus Medicus Parthicus Maximus, and their divine household, Lucius Marcius Simplex and Lucius Marcius Simplex Regillianus made this at their own expense.
EDCS-17900382.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Marcius Simplex and Lucius Marcius Simplex Regillianus to Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva, for the health and safety of the co-emperors Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus and their imperial family, financed privately in the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-17900394.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris divi Septimi Severi Pii Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Britannici maximi filii divi Marci Antonini Germanici Sarmatici nepotis divi Antonini Pii pronepotis divi Hadriani abnepotis divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepotis Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Parthici maximi Britannici maximi Germanici maximi pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate XVII imperatoris III consulis IIII patris patriae proconsulis et Iuliae Domnae Piae Felicis Augustae matris Augusti et castrorum et senatus et patriae totiusque divinae domus eorum templum Victoriae Augustae domini nostri quod Gabinia Hermiona testamento suo ex |(sestertium) C milibus nummum fieri iussit perfectum et dedicatum est suo testamento die dedicationis et deinde quodannis epulum decurionibus ab heredibus suis dari praecepit item agrum qui appellatur circus ad voluptatem populi rei publicae remisit
EDCS-17900394.translation: For the safety of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus, son of the divine Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the divine Marcus Antoninus Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 17th time, acclaimed imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of his country, proconsul; and of Julia Domna Pia Felix Augusta, mother of the Augustus and of the camps and of the senate and of the fatherland, and of their entire divine household: the temple of Victoria Augusta of our lord, which Gabinia Hermiona ordered to be built in her will for 100,000 sesterces, was completed and dedicated according to her will on the day of its dedication, and furthermore, she ordered that an annual banquet be given to the decurions by her heirs; likewise, she remitted to the public the field called 'circus' for the pleasure of the people.
EDCS-17900394.summary: This inscription records the completion and dedication of a temple to Victoria Augusta, funded by Gabinia Hermiona's will, which also provided for an annual banquet for decurions and donated land for public entertainment, all done for the health and safety of Emperor Caracalla and Julia Domna around 214 AD.

EDCS-17900406.translation: To Asicia Victoria, priestess; the Thuggenses placed this due to her generosity and singular liberality towards the public, who, for the flamonia of her daughter Vibia Asiciana, promised 100,000 sesterces, from the income of which theatrical games and sportulae would be given to the decurions; the decurions of both orders erected this.
EDCS-17900406.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the decurions of Thugga to Asicia Victoria, a priestess, in recognition of her generous donation of 100,000 sesterces for public games and distributions, made in honor of her daughter's flamonia.

EDCS-17900416.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Dolabellius Felix, son of Candidus, pious, lived 43 years, lies here.
EDCS-17900416.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dolabellius Felix, son of Candidus, who lived for 43 years, indicating a personal burial, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-17900419.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Volceia Victoria lived piously for 65 years; she lies here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Cornelius Solutor Sissaris lived piously for 3[X] years; he lies here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Botuvia Victoria lived piously for [X] years.
EDCS-17900419.summary: This is a collection of three funerary inscriptions, each dedicated to the spirits of the departed, commemorating Volceia Victoria, Quintus Cornelius Solutor Sissaris, and Botuvia Victoria, detailing their ages and pious lives.

EDCS-18300018.text_edition: Marco Flavio Marci filio Sabarro Vettio Severo clarissimo viro proconsuli provinciae Africae decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-18300018.translation: To Marcus Flavius Sabarrus Vettius Severus, son of Marcus, a most distinguished man, proconsul of the province of Africa, by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-18300018.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by the decurions with public funds to Marcus Flavius Sabarrus Vettius Severus, a prominent proconsul of Africa.

EDCS-18300231.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Aelius Laetianus lived for 18 years. To the memory of Gaius Iulius Saturninus, son of Gaius, of the Papirian tribe, from the colony of Sufetula, who performed all honors, to his grandsons.
EDCS-18300231.summary: This inscription combines a funerary dedication to Quintus Aelius Laetianus, who died at 18, with a memorial dedicated by Gaius Iulius Saturninus of Sufetula to his grandsons, noting his own distinguished public career.

EDCS-21000573.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Commodi Augusti Quintus Volusius Maximus Maximianus sacerdos quod nania Maxima uxor sua hoc munus fieri una secum voverat id Volusius Maximianus votum solvit
EDCS-21000573.translation: For the well-being of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Augustus, Quintus Volusius Maximus Maximianus, priest, fulfilled the vow that his wife Maxima had sworn to have this gift made together with him.
EDCS-21000573.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Quintus Volusius Maximus Maximianus, a priest, for the well-being of Emperor Commodus, fulfilling a vow made jointly with his wife Maxima.

EDCS-21000585.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to Emperor Septimius Severus, bearing his titles Arabicus and Adiabenicus, commemorating military victories.

EDCS-21000597.text_edition: Titi Camidieni Atimeti doliare ex praediis Plotinae Augustae
EDCS-21000597.translation: Pottery from the estates of Plotina Augusta belonging to Titus Camidienus Atimetus.
EDCS-21000597.summary: This inscription identifies a piece of pottery (doliare) as originating from the estates of Empress Plotina and belonging to Titus Camidienus Atimetus, likely indicating ownership or production.

EDCS-21000598.text_edition: Opus doliare ex praediis Domitiae Lucillae Flavi Probi Pontiano et Atiliano consulibus
EDCS-21000598.translation: Pottery made from the estates of Domitia Lucilla, belonging to Flavius Probus, in the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus.
EDCS-21000598.summary: This inscription identifies a piece of pottery as having been produced from the estates of Domitia Lucilla, owned by Flavius Probus, and dates its production to the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus (261 AD).

EDCS-21000604.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Marcus Manlius Felix sacerdos dono dedit dedicavit
EDCS-21000604.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Marcus Manlius Felix, priest, gave and dedicated this as a gift.
EDCS-21000604.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication to Silvanus Augustus, made as a gift by the priest Marcus Manlius Felix.

EDCS-21000613.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Aurelio Commodo Augusto Imperatoris Antonini Augusti filio divi Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiani Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae atnepoti Germanico Sarmatico tribunicia potestate IIII imperatori IIII consuli II patri patriae res publica Uchitanorum Maiorum sua pecunia fecit itemque dedicavit decreto decurionum
EDCS-21000613.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Commodus Augustus, son of Emperor Antoninus Augustus, grandson of the deified Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Germanicus Sarmaticus, with tribunician power for the fourth time, acclaimed imperator for the fourth time, consul for the second time, father of the country. The republic of the Greater Uchitani made and dedicated this with its own money, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-21000613.summary: This inscription records a dedication and construction project (likely a monument or building) by the republic of the Greater Uchitani using their own funds and by decurial decree, honoring Emperor Commodus with a full titulature dating to 179 AD.

EDCS-21000614.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto principi iuventutis tribunicia potestate V consuli proconsuli patri patriae Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Parthici maximi Arabici Adiabenici fortissimi felicissimi principis sena et Iuliae Domnae Augustae matris Augustorum et castrorum filio et Publi Septimi Getae nobilissimi Caesaris fratri divi Marci Antonini Pii nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani et divi Nervae adTnepoti#adnepoti#ATNEPOTI res publica Uchitanorum Maiorum decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-21000614.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, princeps iuventutis, with tribunician power for the fifth time, consul, proconsul, father of the country; son of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Parthicus Maximus Arabicus Adiabenicus, most brave, most fortunate prince, and of Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the Augusti and of the camps; and brother of Publius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar; grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan and the deified Nerva. The republic of the Greater Uchitani made this by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-21000614.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by the republic of the Greater Uchitani using public funds and by decurial decree, honoring Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus) with an extensive titulature, along with his imperial family, dating to 202 AD.

EDCS-21000615.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Valerio Diocletiano Pio Felici Augusto et
EDCS-21000615.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletianus Pius Felix Augustus and...
EDCS-21000615.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication to the Emperor Diocletian, likely part of a larger public monument, dating to his reign (late 3rd/early 4th century AD).

EDCS-21000617.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Tuscilius Luci filius Quintianus miles cohortis XI urbanae stipendiorum trium pius vixit annis XXIII hic situs est
EDCS-21000617.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Tuscilius Quintianus, son of Lucius, a soldier of the 11th Urban Cohort, having served three years, pious, lived for 23 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-21000617.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating Quintus Tuscilius Quintianus, a soldier of the urban cohort, erected by his family or comrades after his death.

EDCS-22000652.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto tribunicia potestate IX imperatori consuli II patri patriae et Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto tribunicia potestate IIII et Lucio Septimio [Getae] Caesari Augusto Quinto Anicio Fausto legato Augustorum consulari [legionis III Augustae piae vindicis]
EDCS-22000652.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, with tribunician power for the 9th time, imperator, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country; and to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, with tribunician power for the 4th time; and to Lucius Septimius [Geta] Caesar Augustus; Quintus Anicius Faustus, consular legate of the Augusti, [of the Third Augustan Legion, Pious and Vindicator]...
EDCS-22000652.summary: This is a public dedication, likely for a monument or building, honoring the emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), and Geta, erected by Quintus Anicius Faustus, a provincial governor and commander of Legio III Augusta, dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-22000655.text_edition: Tiberio Claudio Caesari Augusto Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consul II imperator III Quintus Marcius Cai filius Barea consul XVvir sacris faciundis fetialis proconsul dedicavit
EDCS-22000655.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, consul for the second time, imperator for the third time; Quintus Marcius Barea, son of Gaius, consul, quindecimvir for performing sacred rites, fetial priest, proconsul, dedicated this.
EDCS-22000655.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Quintus Marcius Barea, a high-ranking Roman official, to Emperor Claudius, likely commemorating a public work or monument during Claudius's reign (mid-1st century AD).

EDCS-22000659.text_edition: Silvanus consul proconsul XVvir sacris faciundis  de sua pecunia faciendum curavit
EDCS-22000659.translation: Silvanus, consul, proconsul, quindecimvir for performing sacred rites, oversaw the construction of this at his own expense.
EDCS-22000659.summary: This inscription records that Silvanus, a prominent Roman official holding multiple prestigious titles, funded and oversaw the construction of a monument or building using his own money.

EDCS-22000675.text_edition: Quinto Egrilio Plariano legato pro praetore patrono Gigthenses publice
EDCS-22000675.translation: To Quintus Egrilius Plarianus, propraetorian legate, patron; the people of Gigthis publicly (dedicated this).
EDCS-22000675.summary: This is a public dedication by the citizens of Gigthis to Quintus Egrilius Plarianus, a provincial governor and their patron, likely honoring his service or benefaction.

EDCS-23200247.text_edition: Iuliae Flaviae Herenniae Caeciliae Honoratianae Optatae clarissimae puellae filiae Flavi Pollionis Flaviani clarissimi viri curatoris rei publicae praetoris kandidati decreto decurionum pecunia publica
EDCS-23200247.translation: To Iulia Flavia Herennia Caecilia Honoratiana Optata, a most distinguished girl, daughter of Flavius Pollio Flavianus, a most distinguished man, curator of the republic, candidate for praetor; by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-23200247.summary: This inscription is a public honorific dedication to a young noble girl, Iulia Flavia Herennia Caecilia Honoratiana Optata, funded by the local council (decurions) and public money, acknowledging her distinguished family.

EDCS-23200257.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aecia, pious, lived for 14 years. Mappalicus placed this for his daughter. She is buried here.
EDCS-23200257.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Mappalicus to his daughter Aecia, who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-23200258.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aelia Cyrpe, also known as Eutycia, lived for 42 years. Her husband placed this for his wife.
EDCS-23200258.summary: This is a tombstone erected by a husband for his wife, Aelia Cyrpe (also known as Eutycia), who lived to be 42 years old.

EDCS-23200261.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aemilia Marcella, pious, lived for 7 years. [Someone] Roman [placed this]. She is buried here.
EDCS-23200261.summary: This is a fragmented tombstone commemorating Aemilia Marcella, who died at the age of seven, likely erected by a family member.

EDCS-23200262.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Antonius Concessus, lived 95 years, he is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. [A woman, name illegible], lived [illegible] years, [illegible]. Marcus Antonius Concessus placed this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-23200262.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Marcus Antonius Concessus, aged 95, and a separate dedication by him to his dearest wife, whose name and age are partially lost, likely from the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-23200264.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Arrania Sperata, lived [3 years, 3] or 4 months, [illegible] days. S[illegible] made this for her dearest and dutiful daughter. She is buried here.
EDCS-23200264.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arrania Sperata, who lived a short life, placed by a parent for their pious daughter, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23200265.translation: Medocus. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Asidonia Victoria, lived 65 years. She is buried here. Her sons placed this for their mother. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Asidonius December, lived 70 years. He is buried here. His sons placed this for their father.
EDCS-23200265.summary: This is a double funerary inscription dedicated by the sons to their mother, Asidonia Victoria (who lived 65 years), and their father, Asidonius December (who lived 70 years), likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23200266.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Assidonius Marianus the dutiful, [lived 3 years], he is buried here. Iu[illegible] placed this for the dutiful [illegible].
