EDCS-58200017.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Pomponius Vitalis, devoted, lived 83 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-58200017.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Pomponius Vitalis, noting his devotion and age at death.

EDCS-58200018.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Mucius Solutor, devoted, lived for years...
EDCS-58200018.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a funerary dedication to Quintus Mucius Solutor, though his full age is lost.

EDCS-58200020.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Servius Agilio, devoted, lived 85 years.
EDCS-58200020.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Servius Agilio, noting his devotion and age at death.

EDCS-58200024.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Caecilius Vitalis lived 75 years, 2 months. He is buried here. Caecilius Vitalianus, Barbarus, and Rusticulus, his sons, made this for their most excellent father, most knowledgeable in all literature, and most skilled in both languages, Greek and Latin, a good man, propagator of this lineage.
EDCS-58200024.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Quintus Caecilius Vitalis, dedicated by his three sons to their highly educated and multilingual father.

EDCS-59600004.text_edition: Neroni Claudio divi Claudi filio Germanici nepoti Tiberi Caesaris pronepoti divi Augusti abnepoti Caesari Augusto Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VIII imperatori consuli IIII patri patriae Servius Cornelius Servi filius Lemonia Orfitus quaestor divi Claudi praetor urbanus consul pontifex sodalis Augustalis proconsul patronus dedicavit Publio Silio Celere legato  columnas et super columnia et super ostia in tribus Ithymbal Arinis filius Sabinus Tapapius flamen divi Augusti sufetu faciendas curavit idemque curator pecuniae publicae eas porticus
EDCS-59600004.translation: To Nero Claudius, son of the deified Claudius, grandson of Germanicus, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar, great-great-grandson of the deified Augustus, Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 8th time, imperator, consul for the 4th time, father of the country. Servius Cornelius, son of Servius, of the Lemonian tribe, Orfitus, quaestor of the deified Claudius, urban praetor, consul, pontifex, Augustalis sodalis, proconsul, patron, dedicated the columns and the architraves and the doorframes on three Ithymbal Arinis, son of Sabinus Tapapius, flamen of the deified Augustus, sufet, oversaw their construction, and the same man, curator of public funds, [dedicated] these porticoes.
EDCS-59600004.summary: This inscription records the dedication of architectural elements to Emperor Nero by Servius Cornelius Orfitus, a high-ranking official, and the supervision of their construction by Sabinus Tapapius, a local flamen and public funds curator, likely dating to Nero's reign (ca. 61-62 CE).

EDCS-59600080.translation: To Geminius Optimus Mucian[us], flamen of Aescula[pius], whose ancestors [held] honors.
EDCS-59600080.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication to Geminius Optimus Mucianus, a priest of Aesculapius, mentioning his ancestors' honors, likely a public or honorary monument.

EDCS-59800105.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Ulpius Fortunatus, freedman and procurator of Augustus, a dutiful man, lived 42 years. He is buried here. Coelia Thallusa, daughter of Aulus, his most devoted wife, made this.
EDCS-59800105.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Fortunatus, a freedman and imperial procurator, set up by his devoted wife Coelia Thallusa.

EDCS-59800108.translation: To the divine virtue and perpetual and greatest clemency, to the founder of eternal peace, restorer of public safety and common liberty, our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most brave Emperor, the municipium Iulium Aurelium Mustitanum, devoted to his divinity and majesty, [set this up].
EDCS-59800108.summary: This inscription is a public dedication from the municipium Iulium Aurelium Mustitanum to Emperor Constantine, praising his virtues and acknowledging him as a restorer of peace and liberty.

EDCS-59800109.translation: To Julia Domna Augusta, mother of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and of the camps, and of the senate, and of the fatherland, wife of the deified Severus Pius, father of our lord Imperator Antoninus Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, Father of the Fatherland. The order of Musti[tanum] [set this up] to Fortuna Redux and Fecunditas by decree of the decurions with public money.
EDCS-59800109.summary: This inscription is a public dedication from the local council of Mustitanum to Empress Julia Domna, honoring her multiple imperial titles and familial connections, and simultaneously dedicating the monument to the goddesses Fortuna Redux and Fecunditas, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-60500082.translation: Nunnius, son of [A]unus, lived 30 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-60500082.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Nunnius, stating his parentage, age, and burial place.

EDCS-60500083.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Primigenius, slave of Flavius Avitus, lived 25 years.
EDCS-60500083.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Primigenius, a slave of Flavius Avitus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-61300095.translation: [...] you understand the prevarication which he practiced not only with Allius Maximus, our adversary, but with almost all the contractors, against right and to the detriment of your accounts, without moderation, so that not only did he forbear to investigate, though we pressed and supplicated for so many years past, and adduced your divine rescript, but he also indulged this same Allius Maximus, a most favored contractor, in the end, by sending soldiers into the same Burunitan saltus to seize and harass some of us, to bind others, and to flog with rods and clubs not a few Roman citizens, evidently for no other merit of ours than that, going in such grave injury (for the measure of our mediocrity) and so manifest, we had used an illicit letter to implore your majesty, the evidence of which injury, Caesar, can indeed be estimated from this, that [he found] it most gratifying to [be recognized] or altogether to know [about the things] he thought [were missing] among our [concerns] where [we hoped] he would learn [about the performance] of tasks [requested]. This compelled us, most wretched men, again to supplicate your divine providence, and therefore we ask, most sacred Emperor, that you help us so that by the head of Hadrian's law, which is written above, the right for procurators, let alone for a contractor, to increase agrarian shares or the provision of labor or draft animals has been taken away, and that it may be without any controversy, as is stated in the letters of the procurators which are in your archive in the district of Carthage, that we owe no more than two ploughing, two sowing, and two reaping labors annually, since this is engraved in bronze and established by all our neighbors everywhere in a perpetual form up to the present day, and also confirmed by the letters of the procurators which we have written above. Help us, and since we, rustic and humble men, supporting our lives by the labor of our hands, are unequal to the contractor, who is most favored by lavish gifts, before your procurators, to whom he is known through successive changes of contract terms, have mercy and deign by sacred rescript to decree that we owe no more than by Hadrian's law and by the letters of your procurators, that is, three times two labors, so that by the benefit of your majesty, your rustics, native inhabitants and tenants of your saltus, may not be troubled beyond this by the contractors of the fiscal lands. Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Sarmaticus Germanicus Maximus to Lucius Lucullus and others, procurators, regarding the observance of my discipline and institution, will ensure that no more than three times two labors are demanded from you by injustice contrary to the perpetual form, and I have written and recognized with another hand. Copy of the letter of the esteemed procurator Tussanius Aristo and Chrysanthus to their Andronicus, greetings, according to the sacred subscription of our most holy Lord Imperator, which Lucius Lucullus received given on his petition. And with another hand, we wish you to live a very happy life. Farewell. Given the day before the Ides of September at Carthage. Happily completed and dedicated on the Ides of May, with Aurelianus and Cornelianus as consuls, under the care of Gaius Julius Peloope Salaputus, master.
EDCS-61300095.summary: This extensive inscription is a legal petition from a community of tenant farmers (coloni) in North Africa to Emperor Commodus, complaining about abuses by a contractor and procurators regarding labor obligations and property rights, with the emperor's favorable response and the dedication of the inscribed text.

EDCS-61800284.translation: Sentia Vitalis, bearing the sign of the omnipotent God. Whom you now see, who lived thirty years and six months, until it was permitted for me to reach the end of life, I left the light and my little children. Hear, husband, live kindly while it is permitted to you.
EDCS-61800284.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a woman named Sentia Vitalis, expressing her life span, her departure from her children, and a final plea to her husband to live kindly.

EDCS-64800298.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Mussius Ingenuus lived for 40 years.
EDCS-64800298.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Mussius Ingenuus, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-64800299.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. For Marcus Fadius Clarus, he lived 35 years, 3 months, 25 days.
EDCS-64800299.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Fadius Clarus, detailing his precise age at death, probably from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65000109.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iudas Icositanus monumentum vivus fecit
EDCS-65000109.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Judas Icositanus made this monument during his lifetime.
EDCS-65000109.summary: This inscription records that Judas Icositanus, from Icosium (modern Algiers), prepared his own funerary monument while still alive, a common practice in the Roman world.

EDCS-65000121.summary: This is a brief inscription stating only the name of Lucius Gravius Romanus, likely a personal identification marker of some kind.

EDCS-65400059.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Porcius Verus lived 47 years. Fulvius Primus placed this for his father. He is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Porcius Martialis lived 22 years. Quintus Porcius Verus placed this for his brother. He is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Fulvia Tertiola lived 4 years. Quintus Porcius Verus, her father, placed this. She is buried here.
EDCS-65400059.summary: This is a funerary monument consisting of three separate inscriptions, dedicated by Quintus Porcius Verus to his brother Porcius Martialis and his daughter Fulvia Tertiola, and by Fulvius Primus to his father Quintus Porcius Verus, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-65600082.translation: Aemilia Euphrosyne placed this sepulchral vault for her freedman Aemilius Danus. He lived 35 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead.
EDCS-65600082.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Aemilia Euphrosyne erected a tomb for her freedman, Aemilius Danus, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-65600083.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Agria Sabina, a chaste matron, lived 30 years. She is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Asidonius Repentinus lived [---] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600083.summary: This funerary monument contains two distinct inscriptions, one commemorating Agria Sabina, described as a chaste matron, and the other for Asidonius Repentinus.

EDCS-65600084.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Clodia Venusta lived 80 years. Her most devoted children placed this.
EDCS-65600084.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the devoted children of Clodia Venusta, who lived to the age of 80.

EDCS-65600085.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. For Gaius Cornelius Perpetuus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, Vipianus, he lived 6 years. He is buried here. Gaius Cornelius Primus Pucio placed this for his dearest son.
EDCS-65600085.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Cornelius Primus Pucio to his dearest son, Gaius Cornelius Perpetuus Vipianus, who died at the young age of six.

EDCS-65600086.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Lartius Vegetus lived 80 years. He is buried here. A pious son placed this. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Julia Quintulla lived 60 years. She is buried here. A pious son placed this. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Lartius Pudens lived 42 years. He is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Edinia Regilla lived [---] years.
EDCS-65600086.summary: This is a collective funerary monument containing multiple inscriptions for members of the Lartius and Edinia families, including Titus Lartius Vegetus, Julia Quintulla, Titus Lartius Pudens, and Edinia Regilla, with some erected by a pious son.

EDCS-65600087.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Marius Tertulus Luciolus lived for fifty years. To him, with whom he wished [to be] with [---]. His sons, Marius Aquilus Luciolus, placed this for their dearest father. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600087.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a father's death, set up by his two sons, Marius Aquilus Luciolus and [Marius] Luciolus, for Gaius Marius Tertulus Luciolus, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-65600088.translation: Numonia Candida, a woman without equal, lived 38 years. She is buried here. Her parents, whom she struck with bitter death, placed this so that the memory of her name may endure through reading.
EDCS-65600088.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by the parents of Numonia Candida, a woman who lived 38 years, to preserve her memory.

EDCS-65600090.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Tilia Concordia lived 17 years, 30 days. Tilius N. P., her devoted father, placed this. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Tilius [---] lived [---] years [---].
EDCS-65600090.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a larger monument for a family, set up by Tilius N. P. for his daughter Tilia Concordia, who lived 17 years and 30 days, and presumably for another family member Tilius [---].

EDCS-65600091.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ulpia Cata, a woman of honorable memory, lived 28 years. She is buried here. Ulpius Colonus made this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-65600091.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpius Colonus to his most devoted wife, Ulpia Cata, who lived for 28 years.

EDCS-65600093.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavius M[---] lived [---] years. He is buried here. Primitivus placed this for his dearest son. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavius Felix, the second, lived 16 years, [---] months. [---] made this for his devoted son. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavius Primitivus lived [---]6 years, 3[---] months. He is buried here. The Flavii placed this for their father.
EDCS-65600093.summary: This funerary monument records three generations of the Flavian family, with inscriptions for Flavius M[---], Flavius Felix (who lived 16 years), and Flavius Primitivus, set up by their respective family members.

EDCS-65600094.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Fortunata [---].
EDCS-65600094.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Iulia Fortunata, indicating her dedication to the Divine Shades.

EDCS-65600121.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Iulius Agilis, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe [---].
EDCS-65600121.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Agilis, son of Gaius and a member of the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-65600131.translation: [---]nicius [---]mus, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, served for 20 years and lived for [---] years [---].
EDCS-65600131.summary: This fragmentary inscription commemorates [---]nicius [---]mus, a veteran of Legio III Augusta who served for 20 years.

EDCS-65600133.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Aemilius Saturninus lived 2[---] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600133.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Aemilius Saturninus, who lived at least 20 years and is buried at the site of the inscription.

EDCS-65600134.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Appia Galla. She lived 90 years. Her devoted sons placed this. She is buried here.
EDCS-65600134.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by her devoted sons to Appia Galla, who lived 90 years and is interred at this location.

EDCS-65600135.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Asclepiades lived for [---] years. Asidonia Felicitas piously erected this for her [---]. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600135.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Asclepiades, erected by Asidonia Felicitas, likely a relative, with the deceased buried on the spot.

EDCS-65600136.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Avius from the Quirina tribe, [---] lived for [---] years. She is buried here.
EDCS-65600136.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Avius from the Quirina tribe, indicating her burial location.

EDCS-65600137.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for Aurelia Matita, indicating her age at death.

EDCS-65600138.translation: [---]dius Felix, dutiful, lived for [---] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600138.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for a man named [---]dius Felix, noting his dutiful character and burial place.

EDCS-65600141.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fabius [---] Victor [---] erected this [---]. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600141.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to Fabius [---] Victor, indicating his burial spot and a dedicator, likely a relative.

EDCS-65600142.translation: Gavinnia Dorchas lived for 56 years. Her dutiful sons erected this.
EDCS-65600142.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gavinnia Dorchas, erected by her pious sons, indicating her age at death.

EDCS-65600143.translation: To the Divine Shades. Geminia (?) Spes lived for 6 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-65600143.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Geminia Spes, a young girl who lived for six years, and indicates her burial location.

EDCS-65600145.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Julius Subitanus, a boy of good nature, lived for 12 years. Julius Generosus erected this for his foster-child. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600145.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Subitanus, a young boy, erected by his foster-father, Julius Generosus, indicating the deceased's age and burial place.

EDCS-65600146.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Julia Concordia lived for 17 years and 30 days. Her son (?) erected this out of piety.
EDCS-65600146.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Concordia, who died at 17 years and 30 days old, erected by her son out of piety.

EDCS-65600148.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aulus Numonius Rogatus lived for 75 years. He is buried here. His sons erected this for their dutiful father.
EDCS-65600148.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Numonius Rogatus, erected by his sons for their pious father, indicating his age at death and burial place.

EDCS-65600149.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Valerius Liberalis lived for 30 years. He lies here. His father placed this for his son.
EDCS-65600149.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Publius Valerius Liberalis, set up by his father, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-65600150.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Varius Restutus lived for [X] years. He lies here.
EDCS-65600150.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Varius Restutus, likely from the imperial period, with his age and exact burial location partially lost.

EDCS-65600151.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Vi[---]tus, son of Masus, lived for 29 years.
EDCS-65600151.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Vi[---]tus, son of Masus, who died at the age of 29, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-65600158.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Vinnius Trophimus, freedman of Gaius, lived for 91 years. He lies here. Gaius Vinnius Servilius placed this for his dutiful father.
EDCS-65600158.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Vinnius Trophimus, a freedman who lived to 91, erected by Gaius Vinnius Servilius, his son, likely during the imperial period.

EDCS-65600161.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Sallustina lived for 16 years. She lies here. Her sister Grata placed this for her.
EDCS-65600161.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sallustina, who died at 16, set up by her sister Grata, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-65600162.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aventius Protagoras lived for 60 years. Asidonia Buntesia made this for a good man. He lies here.
EDCS-65600162.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aventius Protagoras, who lived to 60, erected by Asidonia Buntesia for a good man, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-65600163.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Adventus lived for 70 years. His sons placed this for their dutiful father. He lies here.
EDCS-65600163.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Adventus, who died at 70, erected by his sons for their dutiful father, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-65600164.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Ae[lius?].
EDCS-65600164.summary: This is a very fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for a certain Quintus Ae[lius?], with most details missing.

EDCS-65600165.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Aelius Catullinus lived for 50 years. He lies here.
EDCS-65600165.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aelius Catullinus, who lived to 50, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-65600166.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Arrius Avicius, son of Lucius, lived for 27 years, as fate permitted. V.V. C.S. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Commenialis lived for [X]A years.
EDCS-65600166.summary: This is a composite funerary inscription for two individuals: Arrius Avicius, who lived 27 years, and Aelius Commenialis, whose age is partially preserved, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-65600167.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Carvilia Antonilla lived 87 years. Her sons placed this for their most rare mother. She is buried here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Aelius (?) Saturninus lived 90 years. His sons placed this for their most rare father. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600167.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, with one side dedicated by sons to their mother Carvilia Antonilla, who lived 87 years, and the other side dedicated by sons to their father Quintus Aelius (?) Saturninus, who lived 90 years.

EDCS-65600168.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Ianuaria lived 28 years. Caninius Felix placed this for his devoted wife.
EDCS-65600168.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Caninius Felix dedicated a monument to his devoted wife, Aelia Ianuaria, who lived for 28 years.

EDCS-65600171.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilia Rogatina, pious, lived 10 (?) years. She is buried here. Aemilius Capito, her father, placed this at his own expense for his daughter.
EDCS-65600171.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Aemilius Capito, the father, erected a monument at his own expense for his pious daughter, Aemilia Rogatina, who lived around 10 years.

EDCS-65600172.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aeternius lived 27 years and 6 months.
EDCS-65600172.summary: This is a funerary inscription simply recording the lifespan of Aeternius, who lived 27 years and 6 months.

EDCS-65600173.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Allia Rogatina lived 27 years. She is buried here. Lucius Geminius Orfitianus [rest of text missing].
EDCS-65600173.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Allia Rogatina, who lived 27 years, with a partial dedication by Lucius Geminius Orfitianus.

EDCS-65600174.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Antonius Baricio lived 50 years. He is buried here. His devoted sons placed this.
EDCS-65600174.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by the devoted sons of Marcus Antonius Baricio, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-65600175.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Antonius Fortunatus lived [3] years. [Someone] placed this for his son. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600175.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Antonius Fortunatus, indicating he lived for an unspecified number of years and was buried there, with a dedication by a family member (likely a parent) for their son.

EDCS-65600176.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Antonia Marciana lived 20 years. She is buried here. Marianus placed this for his devoted wife.
EDCS-65600176.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Marianus dedicated a monument to his devoted wife, Antonia Marciana, who lived for 20 years.

EDCS-65600177.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Apria Maiga lived 35 years, poor but a matron. Statilius Paternianus, her devoted husband, placed this. She is buried here.
EDCS-65600177.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Statilius Paternianus, a devoted husband, to his wife Apria Maiga, who lived 35 years and was described as 'poor but a matron'.

EDCS-65600178.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Nina Isperata lived 65 years. Her devoted son (?) placed this. She is buried here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Apuleius Secundinus lived 70 years. His devoted son (?) placed this for his father. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600178.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, with one side dedicated by a son (?) to his mother Nina Isperata, who lived 65 years, and the other side dedicated by a son (?) to his father Apuleius Secundinus, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-65600180.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Arruntia Maior lived for 105 years. Her children placed this for their devoted mother. She is buried here.
EDCS-65600180.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Arruntia Maior, dedicated by her children, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600181.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Asidonia Asiane lived for 45 years. She is buried here. Asidonius Facilis and Athena and A[---]danus made this for their dearest mother.
EDCS-65600181.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Asidonia Asiane, erected by her children, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600182.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Redducta Victoria lived for 35 years and 5 months. Asidonius Calvus placed this for his sister. She is buried here.
EDCS-65600182.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Redducta Victoria, set up by her brother Asidonius Calvus, probably from the Imperial era.

EDCS-65600183.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Attia Marciana lived for 50 years. She is buried here. Modius Victoricus placed this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-65600183.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Attia Marciana, dedicated by her husband Modius Victoricus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600184.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Augusta (?) lived for [---] years and [---] months. Her devoted children placed this.
EDCS-65600184.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a woman named Augustica (?), dedicated by her pious children, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600185.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelia Victorina (?), pious [---].
EDCS-65600185.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription likely for Aurelia Victorina, indicating a dedication to the dead, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600186.translation: Publius Baebidius Victor, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, pious, lived for 35 years. He is buried here. Quintus Geminius Secundus placed this for his best friend.
EDCS-65600186.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Baebidius Victor, a Roman citizen, dedicated by his friend Quintus Geminius Secundus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600188.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Baebinia Donatula lived for 57 years. Her children placed this for their devoted mother. She is buried here.
EDCS-65600188.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baebinia Donatula, set up by her children, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600190.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [---] Bucosus lived for 21 years. He is buried here. [---] placed this for his devoted son.
EDCS-65600190.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Bucosus, dedicated by his parent, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600191.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [---] Bolanius Liberalis lived for 46 years. [---]
EDCS-65600191.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Bolanius Liberalis, recording his age at death, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600192.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caecilia Secundula, who was also Monnula, lived for 19 years, 7 months. Her devoted mother placed this. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Caecilius Secundianus lived for 52 years. Gavia Septimina, his devoted wife, placed this. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600192.summary: This is a funerary monument for two individuals, Caecilia Secundula and Quintus Caecilius Secundianus, dedicated by their respective family members, likely a mother and a wife, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600193.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caecilius [---]ulus [---]s [---].
EDCS-65600193.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed, mentioning a Caecilius, suggesting a burial monument from the Imperial era.

EDCS-65600194.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caecilia Donata lived for [approximately 13] years.
EDCS-65600194.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Caecilia Donata, who lived to an unspecified age, likely placed by a family member, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600195.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caecilia Iulla, devoted, lived for 18 years, 6 months, and 3 days. Caecilius Amandus, her father, placed this for his devoted daughter. She is buried here.
EDCS-65600195.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Iulla, dedicated by her father, Caecilius Amandus, commemorating her short life and marking her burial place, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600196.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caecilia Tertia lived for 14 years. She is buried here. Quintus Caecilius Firmanus placed this for his daughter. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caecilia Privata lived for 27 years. She is buried here. Quintus Caecilius Firmanus placed this for his devoted wife.
EDCS-65600196.summary: This is a combined funerary monument, likely a double inscription, dedicated by Quintus Caecilius Firmanus to his daughter, Caecilia Tertia, and to his devoted wife, Caecilia Privata, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600197.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Caelius Germanus lived for 26 years. He is buried here. Lucius Caelius [---]us placed this for his devoted brother.
EDCS-65600197.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Caelius Germanus, dedicated by his brother, Lucius Caelius [---]us, marking his burial place, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600198.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Caelus Martialis lived for 51 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600198.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Caelus Martialis, noting his age and burial place, likely placed by a family member, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600199.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caelia Euporia lived for 38 years. She is buried here. Caelius Praetor(?) placed this for his excellent wife.
EDCS-65600199.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caelia Euporia, dedicated by her husband, Caelius Praetor(?), commemorating her and marking her burial, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600200.translation: A[---]anius [---] lived for 51 years, [---] months. Caelia Tertulla, his wife, made this for her deserving and devoted husband.
EDCS-65600200.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated by Caelia Tertulla to her husband, who lived for 51 years, indicating a commemorative monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600201.translation: [---]is Ca[---]o[---]is. The Caelii placed this for their father. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600201.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated by children, likely named Caelii, to their father, marking his burial place, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600203.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Calventius Commodus lived for 81 years.
EDCS-65600203.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Calventius Commodus, stating his age at death and dedicating the monument to the spirits of the dead.

EDCS-65600205.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Camurenus Saturninus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 54 years; he is buried here. Camurenus Saturninus, his son, set this up for his devoted father.
EDCS-65600205.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Camurenus Saturninus for his father, Gaius Camurenus Saturninus, detailing the deceased's name, tribe, age, and burial location.

EDCS-65600207.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Carmeius Nundinarius lived for 11 years; he is buried here. And Carmeia Augurina lived for 9 years; she is buried here. Carmeius Augurinus, the father, set this up for his most devoted children. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Volussia Servilia lived for 55 years; she is buried here. Canneius Augurinus set this up for his dearest and most devoted wife. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Carmeius Felix lived for 87 years; he is buried here. Carmeia Festa lived for 75 years; she is buried here. Carmeius Augurinus set this up for his most devoted father and mother.
EDCS-65600207.summary: This is a triple funerary inscription where Carmeius Augurinus dedicates monuments to his two children, his wife Volussia Servilia, and his parents Titus Carmeius Felix and Carmeia Festa, indicating their names, ages, and burial locations.

EDCS-65600209.translation: [---]a, daughter of Lucius, the devoted Cata, lived for 10 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-65600209.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Cata, daughter of Lucius, recording her age at death and stating she is buried there.

EDCS-65600210.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Claudius Colonicus lived for 75 years; he is buried here. Domitia, his wife, set this up.
EDCS-65600210.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudius Colonicus, erected by his wife Domitia, detailing his age and burial place.

EDCS-65600211.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titia Sallusa Candidiana lived for 23 years and 10 months. For a most compliant daughter, she is buried here. Gaius Titius Agrius Maximus, her father, set this up. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Claudia Candida lived for 59 years and 6 months. For an incomparable wife, an example of modesty, praised everywhere, a most excellent matron, she is buried here. Gaius Titius Agrius Maximus, her husband, set this up.
EDCS-65600211.summary: This is a double funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Titius Agrius Maximus to his daughter, Titia Sallusa Candidiana, and his wife, Claudia Candida, detailing their ages and virtues.

EDCS-65600212.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Claudia Tryphena lived for 75 years; [she is buried here].
EDCS-65600212.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia Tryphena, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-65600213.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Claudia [---] lived for 44 years; she is buried here. Clodius [---]anus for his devoted and chaste wife, who never lacked faith, integrity, or freedom. This loss, who has lost it, truly lost it.
EDCS-65600213.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia [---], erected by her husband Clodius [---]anus, praising her virtues and lamenting her death.

EDCS-65600214.translation: Estenia Paulina dedicated this to her husband. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Clodius Crescentianus lived for 63 years, more or less.
EDCS-65600214.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodius Crescentianus, erected by his wife Estenia Paulina, stating his approximate age at death.

EDCS-65600215.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Clodius Tracalus lived for 19 years, 7 days, 7 hours. His father set this up for his excellent son; he is buried here.
EDCS-65600215.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a father to his son, Lucius Clodius Tracalus, precisely recording his very short life span and burial location.

EDCS-65600216.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. For Cornelius Antinianus, a most sweet infant. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Ulpia Severa lived 33 years. She lies here.
EDCS-65600216.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals: an infant named Cornelius Antinianus and Ulpia Severa, who lived 33 years, both likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600217.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Cornelius Felix lived 49 years. He lies here. Porcia Laeta placed this for her excellent husband.
EDCS-65600217.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Porcia Laeta for her husband, Lucius Cornelius Felix, who lived 49 years, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600218.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Cornelia Dubitata lived 54 years, 3 months. She lies here. Caecilius Amandus placed this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-65600218.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Caecilius Amandus for his most devoted wife, Cornelia Dubitata, who lived 54 years and 3 months, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600219.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Cornelia Extricata lived 22 years. Larnia Sal-- her daughter placed this. She lies here.
EDCS-65600219.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelia Extricata, who lived 22 years, erected by her daughter Larnia, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600220.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Cornelia Felicitas lived 36 years, 10 months, 19 days. Lucius Sittius Maritus placed this for his dearest wife. She lies here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius, her son, lived 30 days. His devoted father placed this. He lies here.
EDCS-65600220.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelia Felicitas, placed by her husband Lucius Sittius Maritus, and also for their son Lucius, placed by his devoted father, both likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600221.translation: Marcus Decumus Capito lived 48 years. Manlia Victoria placed this for her husband.
EDCS-65600221.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Manlia Victoria for her husband, Marcus Decumus Capito, who lived 48 years, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600222.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Deuterius lived 6 years, 7 months, 19 days. He lies here. Iulii [---]lus and Nice and Cirus made this for their most sweet foster-child.
EDCS-65600222.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Deuterius, an infant who lived 6 years, 7 months, and 19 days, made by Iulii [---]lus, Nice, and Cirus for their beloved foster-child, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600223.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Domitius lived 35 years. He lies here. Pursaccus placed this for his dearest brother.
EDCS-65600223.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Pursaccus for his dearest brother, Publius Domitius, who lived 35 years, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600224.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Aeternia Feculina lived 92 years. Cornelius Felix made this out of his devotion. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Domitius Felix lived 65 years. He lies here. Iulius Nundinarius, his son, placed this for his father.
EDCS-65600224.summary: This is a double funerary inscription for Aeternia Feculina, who lived 92 years, set up by Cornelius Felix, and for Domitius Felix, who lived 65 years, set up by his son Iulius Nundinarius, both likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600225.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Eventius lived 5 years. His devoted father made this. He lies here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Eventius lived two years. His devoted father made this.
EDCS-65600225.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, likely for two different children, both named Eventius, who died at ages 5 and 2 respectively, both erected by their devoted father, probably dating to the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600226.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Fabius Pirforus lived 81 years. He is buried here. His sons placed this for their father.
EDCS-65600226.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Fabius Pirforus, set up by his sons, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600227.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Fabia Secundula lived 50 years. Iunius Fabianus, her devoted son, placed this.
EDCS-65600227.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabia Secundula, erected by her pious son, Iunius Fabianus, likely from the Imperial era.

EDCS-65600228.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Fa[... Hos]pes(?) lived 6 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, Gaius Fa[...] Hospes, probably set up by his family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600229.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Flaminius Castulus lived 40 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Geminia Maxima lived [---] years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Flaminia Estricatula lived 1 year and 7 days. Vibia Urbana(?) [---]
EDCS-65600229.summary: This is a multi-part funerary inscription, likely from a family tomb, commemorating Flaminius Castulus, Geminia Maxima, and Flaminia Estricatula, possibly set up by Vibia Urbana or other family members, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600230.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. [---] Flamon[---] lived 6 years, 3 months, 6 days. His pious parents placed this.
EDCS-65600230.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child, whose name is partially lost, set up by their devoted parents, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600231.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Flavius Conventalis lived 36 years. He is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Magnia Voluptas lived 28 years. She is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Flavia Victoria lived 7 years, 3 months. She is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Flavius Secundus lived 13 years, 3 months. He is buried here. His uncles placed this.
EDCS-65600231.summary: This is a collective funerary inscription, likely from a family mausoleum, commemorating Flavius Conventalis, Magnia Voluptas, Flavia Victoria, and Flavius Secundus, with the last placed by his uncles, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600232.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Flavius Fronto lived 2 years. He is buried here. Quintus Flavius Felix, his father, placed this.
EDCS-65600232.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child, Titus Flavius Fronto, erected by his father, Quintus Flavius Felix, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600233.translation: Flavius Valerius Falacrius lived 33 years, 2 months. He is interred here.
EDCS-65600233.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Valerius Falacrius, likely set up by his family, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600234.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Flavius Romanus lived 29 years. Fidus always in friendship, without [---] blood and [---] mother [---] one [---] [---] He is buried here. His brother-in-law.
EDCS-65600234.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription for Flavius Romanus, possibly highlighting his friendships and family connections, and placed by his brother-in-law, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600235.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Flavius Saturninus lived 11 years, 7 months. He is buried here. Liberalis made this for his good son.
EDCS-65600235.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, Lucius Flavius Saturninus, set up by his father Liberalis, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600236.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Flavius Urbanianus junior lived for 1 year and 7 months. Flavius Urbanianus, his father, set this up for his son. He is laid to rest here.
EDCS-65600236.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the early death of Lucius Flavius Urbanianus junior, erected by his grieving father, Flavius Urbanianus.

EDCS-65600238.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Fortitosa, daughter of Peculiaris, lived for 12 years. Her dearest mother, her two sisters, and her four brothers, out of piety, set up this monument. She is laid to rest here.
EDCS-65600238.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fortitosa, a young girl who died at the age of twelve, erected by her devoted mother, sisters, and brothers.

EDCS-65600239.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the name Fortunatus, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-65600240.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Frumentia, daughter of Frumentius, lived for 9 years and 1 month. She is laid to rest here.
EDCS-65600240.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Frumentia, the daughter of Frumentius, who died at a young age.

EDCS-65600242.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Galla lived for 35 years, 6 months, and 10 days. She is laid to rest here. Liberalis set this up for his devoted wife.
EDCS-65600242.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Galla, who lived for 35 years, erected by her husband Liberalis for his devoted wife.

EDCS-65600243.translation: Gargilia, daughter of Gaius, Spes Daia Flavia, lived for 2 years. She is laid to rest here. Runicilia Faustus took care of it.
EDCS-65600243.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gargilia, also known as Spes Daia Flavia, a young child who lived for two years, with Runicilia Faustus responsible for its care.

EDCS-65600244.translation: [Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead.] Lucius Geminius [lived] for 74 years. Quintus Geminius Martialis made this for his father. He is laid to rest here.
EDCS-65600244.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Geminius, who lived to be 74, erected by his son Quintus Geminius Martialis.

EDCS-65600245.translation: Felix Geminius, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, from Carthage.
EDCS-65600245.summary: This is an inscription identifying Felix Geminius by his name, father's name, tribe, and place of origin, likely part of a funerary or civic monument.

EDCS-65600247.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Geminia Secundula lived for 32 years. She is laid to rest here. Papius Servilianus set this up for his devoted wife.
EDCS-65600247.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Geminia Secundula, who lived for 32 years, erected by her husband Papius Servilianus for his devoted wife.

EDCS-65600248.translation: ... Nabor lived for 21 years, 4 months, 26 days. He is laid to rest here. [Someone] lived for 60-something years. Geminius(?) ... to the dearest ...
EDCS-65600248.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Nabor, who died at 21, and possibly mentions another individual, Geminius, with partial dedicatory remarks.

EDCS-65600249.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gentia Tertullica lived 19 years. She lies here. Gentius, her father, placed this for his dutiful daughter.
EDCS-65600249.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Gentia Tertullica's age at death and was dedicated by her father, Gentius, to her memory.

EDCS-65600250.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Golaia lived for 10 years and [an unknown number of] months/days. She lies here. Her mother placed this.
EDCS-65600250.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Golaia, who died at age ten, and was dedicated by her mother.

EDCS-65600251.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Helvius Iovinus...
EDCS-65600251.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Helvius Iovinus, with the remainder of the text lost.

EDCS-65600253.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Herennius Victorianus, their foster-child, lived 10 years. He lies here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Herennius Victor lived 47 years, 10 months, 12 days. He lies here. Considia Victoria placed this for her dutiful husband.
EDCS-65600253.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, with the first part commemorating a foster-child named Herennius Victorianus, and the second part dedicated by Considia Victoria to her husband, Titus Herennius Victor, detailing his age at death.

EDCS-65600255.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Hail, Honoratus! What did you live beyond decree and brief fate?
EDCS-65600255.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Honoratus, featuring an unusual and poignant rhetorical question about the brevity of his life.

EDCS-65600256.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Agria Fortunata lived 116 years. Her grandsons and son-in-law placed this for her, well-deserving. She lies here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Hortensius Dativus lived 40 years, 4 months. His dutiful mother placed this. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Hortensius Barigbal lived 67 years. His dutiful son and wife placed this.
EDCS-65600256.summary: This is a triple funerary inscription, commemorating Agria Fortunata (died at 116, honored by grandsons and son-in-law), Gaius Hortensius Dativus (died at 40, honored by his mother), and Gaius Hortensius Barigbal (died at 67, honored by his son and wife).

EDCS-65600257.translation: [---]ilius, sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Kalvaria [placed this] for her parents. Hor[ius] Vindemialis lived 87 years, 5 hours. Hor[ia] Silvana, his wife, lived 72 years, 2 hours.
EDCS-65600257.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Kalvaria to her parents, Hor[ius] Vindemialis and Hor[ia] Silvana, detailing their lifespans with unusual precision in hours.

EDCS-65600258.translation: Gaius Iulius Donatus lived 90 years. Iulius Donatus placed this for his dutiful father. He lies here.
EDCS-65600258.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulius Donatus to his father, Gaius Iulius Donatus, commemorating his long life of 90 years.

EDCS-65600259.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Iulius Fortunatus lived for [an unknown number of] years...
EDCS-65600259.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Iulius Fortunatus, with details of his age and the remainder of the text lost.

EDCS-65600260.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulius Hilarus...
EDCS-65600260.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Iulius Hilarus, with most of the text missing.

EDCS-65600261.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Iulius Saturninus lived for 25 years and six months. His mother placed this monument.
EDCS-65600261.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Iulius Saturninus by his mother, recording his age at death.

EDCS-65600262.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Iulius Silvinus lived for 40 years; he lies here. Popilia Florida placed this for her devoted husband.
EDCS-65600262.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Popilia Florida for her husband, Gaius Iulius Silvinus, stating his age and place of burial.

EDCS-65600263.translation: This is the eternal home of Iulius Trifolius, which he made for himself while alive, who lived as many years as he could, not as many as he wished; he lies here. This is the eternal home of Iulia Victoria, which she made for herself while alive, who lived as many years as she could, not as many as she wished; she lies here.
EDCS-65600263.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, likely on a tomb, recording the self-dedication of an eternal home by Iulius Trifolius and Iulia Victoria during their lifetimes, reflecting on the brevity of life.

EDCS-65600264.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Iulius Tutocosus lived for [around] 17 years; he lies here. He placed this for himself while alive.
EDCS-65600264.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Iulius Tutocosus, who preemptively set up his own monument during his lifetime.

EDCS-65600265.translation: [He lies] here. Gaius Iulius Felix placed this for his devoted son. [She] lived for 8 years. Gaius Iulius Felix, the father, placed this for his devoted daughter.
EDCS-65600265.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly from a double tomb, where Gaius Iulius Felix commemorates both his son and his daughter.

EDCS-65600267.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Primitiva lived for 70 years; she lies here. Antonia Rog(ata?) placed this for her most devoted mother.
EDCS-65600267.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Antonia Rogata to her most devoted mother, Iulia Primitiva.

EDCS-65600268.translation: Iulia Rogata, daughter of Marcus, lived for [around] 4 years; she lies here.
EDCS-65600268.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl, Iulia Rogata, specifying her parentage and age at death.

EDCS-65600270.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Iunius Ingenuus lived for 40 years; he lies here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iunia Sisso[---] lived for 5[---] years; she lies here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Iunius Mauricius lived for 2 years; he lies here. Viria Romana placed this for her husband and sons.
EDCS-65600270.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription, likely for a family tomb, where Viria Romana dedicates monuments to her husband, Quintus Iunius Ingenuus, and her two children, Iunia Sisso[---] and Quintus Iunius Mauricius.

EDCS-65600271.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Labienus Saturus lived for 35 years; he lies here. Iulia Pupa, out of devotion, placed this for her husband.
EDCS-65600271.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Pupa to her husband, Lucius Labienus Saturus, out of piety.

EDCS-65600272.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Laelius Maximus lived for 26 years and 48 days; he lies here. His mother placed this for her most devoted and sweet son.
EDCS-65600272.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother to her most devoted and sweet son, Laelius Maximus, detailing his age at death.

EDCS-65600276.translation: [...] He lived for [XX] years. He is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Lucretius Severus lived for 45 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600276.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, an unnamed person who lived 20 years, and Gaius Lucretius Severus, who lived 45 years, indicating two separate burials or a shared monument.

EDCS-65600277.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Antonia Fortunata lived for 60 years. She is buried here. Her husband [set this up] for his wife. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Maevius Heros lived for 45 years, 2 months. He is buried here. Maevius Speratus set this up for his son. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Maevia Secunda lived for 33 years. She is buried here. A dutiful father set this up for his dutiful daughter. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Maevius Aeros lived for 114 years. He is buried here. Lucius Maevius Speratus set this up for his father.
EDCS-65600277.summary: This monument contains four separate funerary inscriptions for members of the Maevia/Maevius family, recording Antonia Fortunata, Quintus Maevius Heros, Maevia Secunda, and Lucius Maevius Aeros, erected by their respective family members.

EDCS-65600278.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Mevius Martialis Nepos, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 50 years, 7 months. He is buried here. Cocceia Fortunata set this up for her dutiful son.
EDCS-65600278.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Cocceia Fortunata for her dutiful son, Lucius Mevius Martialis Nepos, who lived for 50 years and 7 months.

EDCS-65600279.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Mevius Rusticus lived for 1 year, 11 months. He is buried here. Mevius Victor, his father, set this up for his dutiful son.
EDCS-65600279.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mevius Rusticus, who lived just under two years, erected by his father, Mevius Victor.

EDCS-65600280.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Mevia Paula, daughter of Publius, lived for 60 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-65600280.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mevia Paula, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-65600281.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Mevia Rufina, daughter of Gaius, lived for 68 years. She is buried here. Cornelia Marcella, her daughter, set this up for her dutiful mother.
EDCS-65600281.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Cornelia Marcella for her dutiful mother, Mevia Rufina, who lived 68 years.

EDCS-65600282.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Mevia Veneria lived for 35 years. Rutillus Aculini set this up for his dutiful daughter. She is buried here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Mevia Novella lived for 71 years. Rutillus Aculini set this up for his wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-65600282.summary: This monument contains two separate funerary inscriptions by Rutillus Aculini, one for his dutiful daughter, Mevia Veneria, who lived 35 years, and another for his wife, Mevia Novella, who lived 71 years.

EDCS-65600283.translation: Abacus I. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Magnius Felix Ianuarius lived for 3 months. He is buried here. Lampadarius and Aquilina, his parents, set this up for their son.
EDCS-65600283.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the infant Gaius Magnius Felix Ianuarius, who lived only three months, erected by his grieving parents, Lampadarius and Aquilina.

EDCS-65600284.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Magnius Saturninus lived for 12 years, 2 months. He is buried here. Magnius, his father, set this up for his dutiful son.
EDCS-65600284.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Magnius Saturninus, who lived 12 years and 2 months, erected by his father, Magnius.

EDCS-65600285.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Magnia Quinta, a saintly woman, lived for 25 years and [x] days. She is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Magnius Murena lived for 103 years, 2 months, 4 days. He is buried here. Quintus Magnius Rufinus Fortunatus set this up for his excellent father.
EDCS-65600285.summary: This monument contains two funerary inscriptions: one for Magnia Quinta, a 'saintly woman' who lived 25 years, and another for Lucius Magnius Murena, a centenarian who lived 103 years, erected by his son Quintus Magnius Rufinus Fortunatus.

EDCS-65600286.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Magnia Successa lived for 81 years. Her dutiful children placed this.
EDCS-65600286.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the dutiful children of Magnia Successa, recording her age at death, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600287.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [---]ilia Optata, dutiful, lived for 66 years. Aemilius Capito placed this. She is buried here.
EDCS-65600287.summary: This is a funerary inscription for M[---]ilia Optata, erected by Aemilius Capito, indicating she lived for 66 years and is buried at the site, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600288.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Marcius Sillanus lived for 30 years.
EDCS-65600288.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Publius Marcius Sillanus, noting his age at death, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600289.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Mar[---]anuarius [---]
EDCS-65600289.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Publius Mar[---]anuarius, with the remainder of the text missing, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600290.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Maria Faustina lived for 75 years; she is buried here. Cornia Faustina, her dutiful granddaughter, placed this.
EDCS-65600290.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maria Faustina, placed by her dutiful granddaughter Cornia Faustina, recording Maria's age at death and her burial place, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600291.translation: To the Divine Shades Below. [---]s Silvanus [---] to Marta, daughter of Marcus, his good mistress [or wife], who lived for [---] years, 5 months [---]. Matur[---]
EDCS-65600291.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated by [---]s Silvanus to Marta, daughter of Marcus, likely his wife or mistress, recording her age at death, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600292.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Merula lived for 36 years; she is buried here. Marcus Flavius Secundus placed this for his dutiful wife.
EDCS-65600292.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Merula, placed by her husband Marcus Flavius Secundus, recording her age at death and burial place, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600293.translation: [---] Victorinus Felix [---] lived for 65 years, 4 months. Lucius Minucius Fortis lived for 75 years.
EDCS-65600293.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, Victorinus Felix and Lucius Minucius Fortis, recording their respective ages at death, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600294.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Munatia [---] lived for 45 years [---]; she is buried here. Valerianus [---] placed this.
EDCS-65600294.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Munatia, recording her age at death and burial place, likely placed by Valerianus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600297.translation: [---] he is buried here. Marcus Nonius, his son, placed this for his dutiful father.
EDCS-65600297.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an unnamed father, placed by his son Marcus Nonius, recording the father's burial place, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600298.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Octavius Antonianus lived for 23 years. He lies here. Octavius, his devoted father, made this.
EDCS-65600298.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Quintus Octavius Antonianus at age 23, erected by his devoted father, Octavius.

EDCS-65600299.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Octavius Magnus lived for 2 years. His devoted mother placed this. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Octavius Magnus lived for 36 years. His devoted wife placed this. He lies here.
EDCS-65600299.summary: This is a pair of funerary inscriptions, one for Quintus Octavius Magnus (age 2) set up by his mother, and another for a different Quintus Octavius Magnus (age 36) set up by his wife.

EDCS-65600301.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Ofillius Processus lived for [...] years.
EDCS-65600301.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Publius Ofillius Processus, whose age and the monument's dedicator are lost.

EDCS-65600302.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Of Aulus Orbius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, Saturninus (?) Orbianus (?), who lived for [...] years.
EDCS-65600302.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Aulus Orbius Saturninus/Orbianus, whose age at death and the dedicator are missing.

EDCS-65600303.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Orbia Paulina, the most saintly wife of Marcus Ulpius Alexander, who lived for 20 years and 10 months. She lies here.
EDCS-65600303.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Ulpius Alexander to his most saintly wife, Orbia Paulina, who died at the age of 20 years and 10 months.

EDCS-65600304.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Otacilia Quartilla Thader lived for 18 years. She lies here.
EDCS-65600304.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Otacilia Quartilla Thader at the age of 18.

EDCS-65600305.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Asinia Surisca, devoted, lived for 35 years. She lies here. // Papirius Bassus // Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Papirius Bassus lived for 40 years. He lies here. His parents and Fortunata placed this.
EDCS-65600305.summary: This is a pair of funerary inscriptions, one for Asinia Surisca (age 35), and another for Quintus Papirius Bassus (age 40) erected by his parents and Fortunata.

EDCS-65600306.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Maria Faustina lived for 90 years. She lies here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Papirius Victor lived for 80 years. He lies here. His sons placed this for their devoted father.
EDCS-65600306.summary: This is a pair of funerary inscriptions, one for Maria Faustina (age 90), and another for Quintus Papirius Victor (age 80) erected by his sons in honor of their devoted father.

EDCS-65600308.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Percennius Marcianus lived for [...] years. He lies here. [...] placed this for his son.
EDCS-65600308.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Percennius Marcianus, with his age and the full name of the dedicator, his father, missing.

EDCS-65600309.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Petilius Restutus lived for 50 years. His wife, out of devotion, placed this.
EDCS-65600309.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Petilius Restutus, who died at the age of 50, erected by his devoted wife.

EDCS-65600310.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Petronia Honorata lived 50 years; she is buried here. Her children placed this for their devoted mother. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Petronius Antigonus lived 60 years; he is buried here. His children, because of his exceptional devotion to his children, placed this for their most devoted father. // May your bones rest well. // May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-65600310.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, likely for a husband and wife, Petronia Honorata and Gaius Petronius Antigonus, erected by their children, with wishes for the deceased to rest peacefully.

EDCS-65600313.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. [name ending in]s YM Phonicus lived 35 years. Hellen[a/us] F[ecit/ilia].
EDCS-65600313.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a man named Phonicus who lived 35 years, likely erected by someone named Hellen[a/us].

EDCS-65600314.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Septimia Ingenua lived 76 years; she is buried here. Gaius Pompeius Faustus placed this for his most devoted mother. // Gaius Pompeius Simplicius, a man of rare innocence, lived 42 years; he is buried here. Septimia Ingenua, his wife, and Pompeius Faustus, his son, placed this for their parent because of his exceptional affection.
EDCS-65600314.summary: This is a double funerary inscription dedicated to Septimia Ingenua by her son Gaius Pompeius Faustus, and to Gaius Pompeius Simplicius by his wife Septimia Ingenua and son Pompeius Faustus, both celebrating their familial devotion.

EDCS-65600315.translation: He/She is buried here. Pompeia Draca lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-65600315.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Pompeia Draca, who lived 25 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-65600316.translation: Pompeia Rogata, devoted, lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-65600316.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pompeia Rogata, a devoted woman who lived 70 years.

EDCS-65600317.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Pompeia lived 13 years, 4 months; she is buried here. Fructosa placed this for her daughter.
EDCS-65600317.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Pompeia, who died at the age of 13 years and 4 months, erected by her mother Fructosa.

EDCS-65600320.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Postumius Festus lived 70 years; he is buried here. His children made a sepulchral vault for their dearest father.
EDCS-65600320.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Postumius Festus, who lived 70 years, and for whom his children constructed a sepulchral monument.

EDCS-65600321.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Postumia Buntesia lived 60 years; she is buried here. Her dearest daughter placed this.
EDCS-65600321.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Postumia Buntesia, who lived 60 years, erected by her dearest daughter.

EDCS-65600322.translation: He/She lived 90 years; he/she is buried here. Postumia Laeta placed this for her devoted mother-in-law.
EDCS-65600322.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an unnamed individual who lived 90 years, placed by Postumia Laeta for her devoted mother-in-law.

EDCS-65600323.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Prescenius Sorex lived 68 years. His most affectionate son placed this. He is buried here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Prescenius Fortunatus lived 65 years. He placed this for himself while still living. He is buried here.
EDCS-65600323.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, one for Prescenius Sorex placed by his son, and another for Prescenius Fortunatus, who had it made for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-65600324.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelia Dativa lived 81 years. She lies here. Racilius Neptunalis placed this for his devoted mother. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Racilius Tibullus lived 93 years. He lies here. Racilius Neptunalis placed this for his devoted father.
EDCS-65600324.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Racilius Neptunalis for his devoted mother, Aurelia Dativa, and his devoted father, Quintus Racilius Tibullus, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600325.translation: To the Divine Shades. This monument was placed for Roscia Victoria. She lived 60 years. She lies here.
EDCS-65600325.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Roscia Victoria, placed by an unnamed individual, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600327.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [...] Securus [...] lived 11 years. His parents [...] placed this for their son. He lies here.
EDCS-65600327.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a young boy named Securus, who lived 11 years, placed by his parents, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600328.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sempronius Dubitatus lived 100 years.
EDCS-65600328.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Sempronius Dubitatus, who lived to an impressive age of 100 years, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600330.translation: He lived 51 years. His devoted wife and sons made this. They are buried here. And for Septiminus, his son, he lived 11 years.
EDCS-65600330.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a wife and sons to an unnamed man who lived 51 years, and also to their son/brother Septiminus, who lived 11 years, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600331.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Servilius Pacatus lived many years. He lies here. Fruentius [placed this] for his devoted father.
EDCS-65600331.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Fruentius to his devoted father, Marcus Servilius Pacatus, who lived many years, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600332.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sittius Actus, his grandson, lived 8 years, 2 months, 21 days. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sittia Aecta lived 75 years.
EDCS-65600332.summary: This is a double funerary inscription dedicated to Sittius Actus, who was a grandson and died young, and to Sittia Aecta, who lived 75 years, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600333.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sittia Gemellina lived 5 years. [...] Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Victorilla lived 27 years. Marcus Sittius [...]nalis placed this for his wife.
EDCS-65600333.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, one for Sittia Gemellina who died at age 5, and another for Valeria Victorilla who died at 27, erected by Marcus Sittius [...]nalis for his wife, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600334.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Spesina lived 25 years, 5 months. Sabucius placed this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-65600334.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Sabucius to his dearest wife, Spesina, who lived 25 years and 5 months, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-65600335.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary or dedicatory monument, mentioning the name Stat[---] and identifying him as the son of Gaius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-65600336.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Stenia Severa lived for 40 years; she is laid here. Allius Fortunatus placed this for his devoted wife.
EDCS-65600336.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Stenia Severa, placed by her husband Allius Fortunatus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600337.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Tadius Epicte/tus lived for 46 years; he is laid here. Lucius Tadius Epagatus placed this for his devoted brother.
EDCS-65600337.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tadius Epicte/tus, erected by his brother Lucius Tadius Epagatus, typical of the Imperial Roman era.

EDCS-65600338.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Terentius Augustalis lived for 30 years. His devoted mother placed this; he is laid here.
EDCS-65600338.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Terentius Augustalis, dedicated by his grieving mother, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600339.translation: Tillia Primulla, daughter of Nymphodotus, lived for 50 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-65600339.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tillia Primulla, daughter of Nymphodotus, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-65600340.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Titinius Victor lived for 70 years. Titinius Victoricus placed this for his devoted father; he is laid here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Sallustia Quirilla lived for 60 years. Titinius Victoricus placed this for his devoted mother; she is laid here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Aemilia Maxima lived for 50 years. Iulia Felicitas placed this for her devoted mother; she is laid here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Aemilia Castula lived for 6 years. Iulia Felicitas placed this for her devoted sister; she is laid here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Titinius Vitoricus lived for 5 years and 10 months. His devoted father placed this; he is laid here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Titinius Donatus lived for two years. His father placed this for his son; he is laid here.
EDCS-65600340.summary: This monument is a large funerary complex with six separate inscriptions, dedicated by family members Titinius Victoricus and Iulia Felicitas to commemorate various deceased relatives including parents, siblings, and children, indicative of an affluent Roman family during the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600341.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Trebbius Felix lived for 31 years; he is laid here. Aemilia Amata, his devoted wife, placed this for her husband.
EDCS-65600341.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Trebbius Felix, erected by his devoted wife Aemilia Amata, consistent with Imperial Roman funerary practices.

EDCS-65600342.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Tullius Aquilinus lived for 15 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-65600342.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Tullius Aquilinus, likely placed by his family, dating to the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-65600343.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Turrania Veneria Augustia lived for one year and three months; she is laid here. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Turranius Sucessus lived for seven months. Turranius Nasir lived for 14 years. P. T. A. lived for 8 years; he is laid here. // Turranius Saturnius and Valeria Donata, parents, placed this.
EDCS-65600343.summary: This is a complex funerary monument with multiple inscriptions, collectively dedicated by Turranius Saturnius and Valeria Donata to commemorate their deceased children: Turrania Veneria Augustia, Turranius Sucessus, Turranius Nasir, and an individual abbreviated as P. T. A., indicating a family plot from the Imperial period.

EDCS-65600344.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Valerius Felix is laid here; he lived for 18 years. Publius Valerius Fortunatus, his father, placed this for his devoted son. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Secundula is laid here; she lived for 30 years. Publius Valerius Fortunatus placed this for his most devoted wife. // Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Valerius Fortunatus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 87 years; he is laid here. His sons placed this for their devoted father.
EDCS-65600344.summary: This monument is a multi-generational funerary inscription from the Imperial Roman period, erected by Publius Valerius Fortunatus for his son Publius Valerius Felix and his wife Iulia Secundula, and subsequently by his own sons for Publius Valerius Fortunatus himself.

EDCS-65600345.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Valerius Restutus lived for 32 years; he is laid here. For a good friend.
EDCS-65600345.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Restutus, placed by a friend rather than a family member, suggesting a close bond during the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-65600346.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, Vitalis, lies here. Lucius Valerius, his father, made this.
EDCS-65600346.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument set up by Lucius Valerius for his son, Lucius Valerius Vitalis, who lies buried there.
